https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Temmin_.description: Temmin "Snap" Wexley was a human male pilot born in the city of Myrra on the planet Akiva. As a child he was separated from his parents when his father Brentin was seized by the Galactic Empire and his mother Norra left to fight for the Rebel Alliance, leaving him in the care of his aunt. A gifted mechanic, he became an independent junk dealer and built a modified B1 battle droid, Mister Bones, as a bodyguard and companion. After the Battle of Endor he reunited with his mother and helped foil a secret Imperial gathering on Akiva. He went on to train as a pilot, gained Wedge Antilles as a stepfather, and flew at the Battle of Jakku. Decades later he became a founding pilot of the Resistance, serving in Black Squadron under Poe Dameron and marrying fellow pilot Karé Kun. He fought at the destruction of Starkiller Base and was ultimately shot down and killed during the Battle of Exegol.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Temmin_.timeline:
11 BBY - Birth on Akiva

Temmin Wexley is born in the city of Myrra on Akiva to Brentin and Norra Wexley.

~4 ABY - Separated from his parents

His father Brentin is taken by the Galactic Empire and his mother Norra leaves to join the Rebel Alliance, leaving young Temmin in the care of his aunt. He becomes an independent junk dealer and builds the battle droid Mister Bones.

4 ABY - Reunion on Akiva

Following the Battle of Endor, the teenage Temmin reconciles with his mother Norra and joins her to disrupt a secret Imperial meeting on Akiva.

~4 ABY - A new family

Temmin begins training as a pilot, and his mother's relationship with ace Wedge Antilles eventually makes Antilles his stepfather.

5 ABY - Battle of Jakku

Temmin flies an X-wing for the first time during the final major battle of the Galactic Civil War.

~34 ABY - Founding pilot of the Resistance

Known now as "Snap," he becomes one of the Resistance's founding pilots, serving in Black Squadron under Poe Dameron and marrying fellow pilot Karé Kun.

34 ABY - Assault on Starkiller Base

Snap flies with the Resistance in the attack that destroys the First Order's Starkiller Base.

35 ABY - Death at the Battle of Exegol

Fighting in his X-wing against the Sith Eternal fleet, Snap Wexley is shot down and killed during the Resistance's victory at Exegol.

birth.source: Wookieepedia (Temmin Wexley) and corroborating sources state he was born 11 BBY in Myrra on Akiva; consistent with him being a teenager in 4 ABY.

death.source: Wookieepedia/sources: killed 35 ABY during the Battle of Exegol, depicted in The Rise of Skywalker.

notes.current: Episode VII: The Force Awakens, Episode IX: The Rise of Skywalker
notes.suggested: Consider noting his substantial non-film canon appearances (the Aftermath novel trilogy, the Marvel Poe Dameron comic, and the game Star Wars Outlaws), since most of his biography comes from those.
notes.detail: His origin, family, Mister Bones, and Jakku arc all come from the Aftermath trilogy and comics, not the two films our timeline plots. The films alone are fine for the seenIn field, but the maintainer may want to acknowledge the source material.

notes.current: Description ends at the Aftermath/Akiva events and does not mention his later life.
notes.suggested: Extend to cover his pilot career, Wedge Antilles as stepfather, Resistance/Black Squadron service, marriage to Karé Kun, and death at the Battle of Exegol.
notes.detail: The current summary stops around 4 ABY and omits the entire second half of his life, including his death, which our own endYear records.

notes.suggested: Could add affiliations (Rebel/New Republic, Resistance / Black Squadron) or family (stepson of Wedge Antilles, husband of Karé Kun).
notes.detail: These are defining, well-sourced facts not currently captured in metadata.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Jango_F.description: Jango Fett was a human Mandalorian bounty hunter widely regarded as the best in the galaxy during the final years of the Galactic Republic. Hailing from Concord Dawn, he wore distinctive Mandalorian armor equipped with a jetpack, a flamethrower, and dual WESTAR-34 blaster pistols, and flew the Firespray-class starship Slave I. Around 32 BBY he was chosen — through Count Dooku, acting on behalf of the fallen Jedi Sifo-Dyas — as the genetic template for the clone army secretly bred on Kamino, and in payment he requested an unaltered clone whom he raised as his son, Boba Fett. In 22 BBY, Fett was hired by Dooku to assassinate Senator Padmé Amidala, a plot that unraveled when Obi-Wan Kenobi tracked him to Kamino and then to Geonosis. During the First Battle of Geonosis, Fett was killed in the arena by Jedi Master Mace Windu, though his genetic legacy endured in the Grand Army of the Republic and in his son.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Jango_F.timeline:
~66 BBY - Birth on Concord Dawn

Jango Fett is born, becoming tied to the Mandalorian people and their warrior culture.

Rise as a Bounty Hunter

Fett grows into the galaxy's most feared and capable bounty hunter, mastering marksmanship and unarmed combat and adopting his signature Mandalorian armor and starship Slave I.

~32 BBY - Chosen as the Clone Template

Recruited through Count Dooku on behalf of the Jedi Sifo-Dyas, Fett becomes the genetic source for the clone army bred in secret on Kamino. As part of his payment he requests an unaltered clone, whom he names Boba and raises as a son.

22 BBY - The Assassination Plot

Working for Dooku, Fett is contracted to kill Senator Padmé Amidala. He hires the shapeshifter Zam Wesell, then kills her with a toxic dart to keep his employer's identity hidden.

22 BBY - Confrontation on Kamino

Obi-Wan Kenobi traces the assassination to the cloning facility on Kamino and confronts Fett, who battles the Jedi and escapes with Boba aboard Slave I, dueling Kenobi through an asteroid field before fleeing to Geonosis.

22 BBY - Death at the Battle of Geonosis

During the First Battle of Geonosis, Fett fights in the arena but is beheaded by Jedi Master Mace Windu, ending his career as the clone template of the Republic army.

birth.source: Commonly cited birth year of 66 BBY, derived from his age of about 44 at his death in 22 BBY; this figure originates in Legends reference material and is not firmly fixed by an in-universe canon source.

death.source: Killed by Mace Windu during the First Battle of Geonosis in Attack of the Clones (22 BBY).

notes.suggested: Treat 66 BBY as an estimate (startYearUnknown: true)
notes.detail: The 66 BBY birth year stems from Legends sources placing Jango at ~44 years old at his death; current canon does not firmly establish a birth year, so presenting it as fact overstates certainty. The death year (22 BBY) is solidly canon via Attack of the Clones.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Gial_Ac.description: Gial Ackbar was a male Mon Calamari from the ocean world of Mon Cala who became one of the most celebrated military commanders in galactic history, serving across four conflicts over roughly six decades. As captain of the Mon Calamari Guard during the Clone Wars, he fought at the side of Prince Lee-Char against the Quarren separatists and the Karkarodon warlord Riff Tamson, helping preserve his people's throne with Republic and Gungan aid. Under the Galactic Empire he was enslaved along with much of his species, and after his rescue he rose through the Alliance to Restore the Republic to become commander of the rebel fleet, planning and leading the assault on the second Death Star at the Battle of Endor in 4 ABY — the engagement that made his warning of a trap into one of the most quoted moments of the war. He continued to serve the New Republic as an admiral and statesman for his homeworld before retiring, then answered General Leia Organa's call to join the Resistance against the First Order, advising the fleet through the destruction of the Hosnian system and the Battle of Starkiller Base. In 34 ABY he was killed when First Order fighters strafed the bridge of the Resistance flagship Raddus during the evacuation of D'Qar, ending a career defined by patient, meticulous tactical genius.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Gial_Ac.timeline:
~55 BBY - Birth on Mon Cala

Gial Ackbar is born on the ocean world of Mon Cala. Canon gives only an approximate window for his birth, roughly between 61 BBY and 52 BBY.

~22 BBY - Captain of the Mon Calamari Guard

Ackbar rises to captain of the Mon Calamari Guard and serves as a trusted military adviser to King Yos Kolina and his son, Prince Lee-Char.

21 BBY - The Mon Cala civil war

After the king's assassination, Quarren separatists backed by the Karkarodon Riff Tamson attack the Mon Calamari. Ackbar fights alongside Jedi reinforcements, Anakin Skywalker's forces and Gungan warriors sent by Padmé Amidala, protecting Lee-Char through the underwater battles.

21 BBY - Victory and Lee-Char's coronation

With Tamson defeated and the Quarren reconciled, Lee-Char is crowned king of a reunited Mon Cala, with Ackbar established as his leading military commander.

~19 BBY - The Empire subjugates Mon Cala

Following the rise of the Galactic Empire, Mon Cala is occupied and much of its population, Ackbar included, is pressed into servitude.

~4 BBY - Service with the Rebel Alliance

Freed from Imperial captivity, Ackbar joins the Alliance to Restore the Republic, where his tactical skill quickly earns him fleet command and the rank of admiral.

4 ABY - Briefing at Sullust

Ackbar presents the Alliance battle plan against the second Death Star, coordinating Han Solo's strike team on the Endor moon with the fleet's attack on the battle station.

4 ABY - Battle of Endor

Commanding from the Home One, Ackbar realizes the Alliance has flown into an Imperial ambush and orders the fleet to engage the Star Destroyers at point-blank range, a decision that keeps the rebels alive until the Death Star's shield falls and the station is destroyed.

5 ABY - Campaigns for the New Republic

Ackbar continues to lead fleets in the final campaigns against Imperial remnants as the New Republic is established, and serves his homeworld in its government.

~28 ABY - Joining the Resistance

Retired from New Republic service, Ackbar answers Leia Organa's summons and helps organize the Resistance against the rising First Order, becoming its senior naval adviser.

34 ABY - Battle of Starkiller Base

After the First Order destroys the Hosnian system, Ackbar helps plan the Resistance strike that brings down Starkiller Base.

34 ABY - Death aboard the Raddus

During the Resistance evacuation of D'Qar, First Order TIE fighters and Kylo Ren's TIE silencer strafe the bridge of the flagship Raddus. Ackbar is blown into space and killed, leaving Leia Organa to command the surviving fleet.

birth.source: Wookieepedia "Gial Ackbar" infobox gives a birth range of c. 61 BBY – c. 52 BBY rather than a fixed year (derived from statements about his age/decades of service). 55 BBY sits mid-range and is a reasonable positioning guess, but canon does not pin a year.

death.source: Wookieepedia "Gial Ackbar": killed in 34 ABY aboard the Resistance flagship Raddus when TIE/sf fighters and Kylo Ren's TIE silencer strafed the bridge during the evacuation of D'Qar, depicted in Episode VIII: The Last Jedi.

notes.suggested: Remove "Ahsoka"
notes.detail: Ackbar has no appearance in the Ahsoka series; he is dead by 34 ABY and Ahsoka is set c. 9 ABY, and Wookieepedia's appearances list does not include it. This looks like a data-entry error (possibly confusion with the Clone Wars Mon Cala arc, which is already listed separately).

notes.suggested: Keep -55 and keep startYearUnknown: true
notes.detail: Canon gives no fixed birth year. Wookieepedia lists a range of c. 61 BBY – c. 52 BBY, so 55 BBY is a sound mid-range placeholder — the "guess" flag is correct and should not be removed.

notes.suggested: Add a rank/affiliation fact, e.g. "Rank: Admiral" or "Affiliation: Mon Calamari Guard, Rebel Alliance, New Republic, Resistance"
notes.detail: Ackbar's defining identity across the timeline is his command role; his affiliations span four distinct factions, which is unusually informative for a timeline column.

notes.current: "a veteran soldier and strong revolutionary leader… Throughout his sixty years of service"
notes.suggested: Replace with the new description
notes.detail: The existing text is a thin paraphrase of the Wookieepedia lead and omits his most notable milestones entirely — Mon Cala, Endor, and his death aboard the Raddus.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Breha_O.description: Breha Organa was a human female of Alderaan who reigned as queen of that world during the final decades of the Galactic Republic and the early years of the Empire, also serving as her planet's Minister of Education and as head of the House of Organa. Married to Senator Bail Prestor Organa, she was unable to bear a healthy child of her own — a difficult pregnancy left her dependent on a cybernetic heart — and at the end of the Clone Wars in 19 BBY she and Bail adopted the newborn daughter of Padmé Amidala and Anakin Skywalker, raising her as Princess Leia Organa. Under Palpatine's New Order, Breha quietly turned Alderaan's resources, relief missions and refugee networks toward the growing rebellion, working alongside Bail as one of the movement's founding architects while shielding Leia's true parentage and, for as long as she could, keeping her daughter out of the fight; she mentored Leia in statecraft and taught her the traditions of Alderaanian leadership. Breha remained on Alderaan to the end, dying in 0 BBY when the Empire destroyed the planet with the Death Star's superlaser.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Breha_O.timeline:
~54 BBY - Birth on Alderaan

Breha is born into Alderaanian nobility. Canon never gives a year; sources place her birth somewhere between 59 and 49 BBY.

~33 BBY - Ascension to the Alderaanian throne

Breha becomes Queen of Alderaan, heading the royal family and later serving as the planet's Minister of Education.

Before 22 BBY - Marriage to Bail Organa

She weds Bail Prestor Organa, who represents Alderaan in the Galactic Senate while she rules from Aldera.

Before 19 BBY - A failed pregnancy and a cybernetic heart

Complications during pregnancy nearly kill Breha and leave her with a cybernetic heart; the couple are unable to have a biological child.

19 BBY - The adoption of Leia

At the end of the Clone Wars, Bail returns from Polis Massa with a newborn girl. Breha welcomes the infant, and the child is raised as Princess Leia Organa of Alderaan.

19 BBY onward - Quiet resistance under the Empire

As the New Order tightens, Breha lends Alderaan's ships, credits and relief operations to the nascent rebellion her husband is helping to assemble, while keeping Leia's origins secret.

9 BBY - Leia's abduction

When Leia is kidnapped by agents seeking to blackmail Bail, Breha endures an agonizing wait on Alderaan until Obi-Wan Kenobi recovers her daughter and returns her home.

~3 BBY - Leia's Day of Demand

Breha presides over the ceremony in which the sixteen-year-old Leia claims her right to the throne, then works to keep her daughter clear of the rebel network she and Bail are building.

2 BBY - The Alliance declared

With the rebellion openly formed, Alderaan under Breha becomes one of its crucial covert backers.

0 BBY - Death in the destruction of Alderaan

Grand Moff Tarkin orders the Death Star to fire on Alderaan. Breha dies with her world and its billions of inhabitants.

birth.source: Wookieepedia's canon "Breha Organa" article dates her birth only to a range — between 59 BBY and 49 BBY (derived from her being roughly of an age with Bail Organa and an adult ruler by the late Republic). No canon work states a year; -54 is the midpoint of that range and should remain flagged as a guess.

death.source: Wookieepedia "Breha Organa" / Alderaan articles: she died in 0 BBY when the Death Star destroyed Alderaan, as depicted in Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope (dating per Star Wars: Galactic Atlas).

notes.suggested: 54 BBY (estimated)
notes.detail: Automatic check: birth year in data.json disagrees with the year Claude verified. Wookieepedia's canon "Breha Organa" article dates her birth only to a range — between 59 BBY and 49 BBY (derived from her being roughly of an age with Bail Organa and an adult ruler by the late Republic). No canon work states a year; -54 is the midpoint of that range and should remain flagged as a guess.

notes.suggested: ~54 BBY (still a guess)
notes.detail: Canon gives no birth year for Breha. Wookieepedia brackets her birth between 59 BBY and 49 BBY; 45 BBY sits outside that window entirely and would make her a teenager at the time she was already reigning as queen. Keeping startYearUnknown true is right, but the guess itself should move to roughly 54 BBY.

notes.suggested: Add Titles: Queen of Alderaan / Minister of Education; note her cybernetic heart
notes.detail: Her roles as Queen and Minister of Education are core to her identity (StarWars.com Databank), and the cybernetic heart from her failed pregnancy (Leia, Princess of Alderaan) is the reason the Organas adopted Leia.

notes.current: Description stops at the adoption of Leia
notes.detail: The current text omits the two things she is most notable for beyond the adoption: her active role in founding and funding the Rebel Alliance, and her death in the destruction of Alderaan in 0 BBY — which is the very date the entry asserts as her endYear.
notes.suggested: Extend the summary through her rebel activity and death at Alderaan.

notes.current: Episode III: Revenge of the Sith, Obi-Wan Kenobi
notes.suggested: Both are correct; note that Episode III is her only film appearance
notes.detail: StarWars.com confirms Revenge of the Sith as her sole film appearance, and Simone Kessell plays her in Obi-Wan Kenobi. Most of her characterization (the cybernetic heart, the Day of Demand, the rebel financing) comes from the novels Leia, Princess of Alderaan and Bloodline rather than anything your timeline plots — worth flagging if you ever surface sources.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Greedo.description: Greedo was a male Rodian bounty hunter and mercenary, most infamous for his fatal encounter with the smuggler Han Solo in the Mos Eisley cantina. Born on the swamp world of Rodia, Greedo relocated in his youth to the desert planet Tatooine, growing up in Mos Espa. He fancied himself a big-time hunter working for the Hutt crime lord Jabba, though few took him seriously. During the Clone Wars he was hired amid the Trade Federation's blockade of Pantora to kidnap the daughters of Chairman Notluwiski Papanoida, only to be cornered by the chairman himself and forced to lead him to the captives. Years later, dispatched by Jabba to collect on Han Solo's debt, the overconfident and unlucky Rodian was gunned down by Solo before he could pull the trigger.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Greedo.timeline:
44 BBY - Birth on Rodia

Greedo is born on the swamp planet Rodia, homeworld of the Rodian species. He relocates during his youth to Tatooine, growing up in Mos Espa and later entering the bounty hunting trade.

~21 BBY - Kidnapping of the Papanoida daughters

During the Clone Wars and the Trade Federation's blockade of Pantora, Greedo and a Gotal accomplice kidnap Che Amanwe and Chi Eekway Papanoida, daughters of Pantoran Chairman Notluwiski Papanoida, to pressure him toward the Separatists. Papanoida and his son Ion track Greedo down and force him to lead them to the captives.

0 BBY - Death in the Mos Eisley cantina

Hired by Jabba the Hutt to collect on Han Solo's debt, Greedo corners Solo in the Mos Eisley cantina on Tatooine. Before he can act, Solo shoots and kills the luckless Rodian, shortly before the Battle of Yavin.

birth.source: Wookieepedia (Greedo) states he was born in 44 BBY on Rodia.

death.source: Wookieepedia (Greedo); killed by Han Solo in the Mos Eisley cantina during A New Hope, dated 0 BBY.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Dooku.description: Count Dooku, also known by his Sith title Darth Tyranus, was a Force-sensitive human male born into the noble ruling family of Serenno. Trained as a Jedi by Grand Master Yoda, he became one of the Order's most respected swordmasters and lightsaber duelists, but grew disillusioned with the Jedi's subservience to a Republic he saw as corrupt. Following the death of his former Padawan Qui-Gon Jinn, Dooku left the Jedi Order, reclaimed his hereditary title of Count of Serenno, and secretly became the second Sith apprentice of Darth Sidious. As the public leader and Head of State of the Confederacy of Independent Systems, he ignited and led the Separatist cause during the Clone Wars, orchestrating the conflict as part of Sidious's plan to destroy the Jedi and seize control of the galaxy. He trained dark side agents such as Asajj Ventress and Savage Opress and dueled the galaxy's greatest Jedi. Ultimately, Dooku was betrayed by his own master: during the Battle of Coruscant he was defeated and executed by Anakin Skywalker at Sidious's command, a casualty of the very Sith treachery he had embraced.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Dooku.timeline:
102 BBY - Birth on Serenno

Dooku is born into the wealthy noble House of Serenno, heir to the title of Count. As a Force-sensitive infant he is given over to the Jedi Order and taken to the Jedi Temple on Coruscant.

~92 BBY - Training under Yoda

As a youngling and later Padawan, Dooku is trained in the ways of the Force by Grand Master Yoda, growing into one of the Order's most skilled and respected members and a master of lightsaber combat.

~44 BBY - Master to Qui-Gon Jinn

Dooku takes Qui-Gon Jinn as his Padawan, forging a bond that would later shape his disillusionment with the Jedi and the Republic.

32 BBY - Departure from the Jedi Order

The death of his former apprentice Qui-Gon Jinn at the Battle of Naboo deepens Dooku's disillusionment. He leaves the Jedi Order and reclaims his hereditary title as Count of Serenno.

~32 BBY - Becoming Darth Tyranus

Dooku is taken as the second apprentice of the Sith Lord Darth Sidious, adopting the identity Darth Tyranus. On Sidious's orders he arranges for the creation of a secret clone army on Kamino under the name of the late Jedi Sifo-Dyas.

~24 BBY - Rise of the Separatist movement

Dooku publicly emerges as the charismatic leader of a growing secessionist movement, rallying thousands of star systems in opposition to the Galactic Republic.

22 BBY - The Battle of Geonosis

Dooku's Separatist plans are exposed on Geonosis. He captures Obi-Wan Kenobi, duels Kenobi, Anakin Skywalker, and Yoda, and escapes as the first battle of the Clone Wars erupts, delivering the Death Star plans to Sidious.

22-19 BBY - Leading the Confederacy in the Clone Wars

As Head of State of the Confederacy of Independent Systems, Dooku directs the Separatist war effort, trains dark side agents such as Asajj Ventress and Savage Opress, and repeatedly clashes with Jedi across the galaxy.

19 BBY - Death at the Battle of Coruscant

During the Separatist assault on Coruscant, Dooku duels Anakin Skywalker and Obi-Wan Kenobi aboard the flagship Invisible Hand. Anakin overpowers and disarms him, then decapitates him at Palpatine's command—revealing Dooku had been a disposable pawn in his own master's scheme.

birth.source: Wookieepedia "Dooku" article states he was born in 102 BBY on Serenno.

death.source: Wookieepedia "Dooku" article; killed in 19 BBY during the Battle of Coruscant, depicted in Revenge of the Sith.

notes.current: Attack of the Clones, The Clone Wars (movie), The Clone Wars (TV series), Revenge of the Sith
notes.suggested: Add Tales of the Jedi
notes.detail: The 2022 canon animated anthology series 'Tales of the Jedi' devotes three episodes to Dooku's arc as a Jedi and his path toward the dark side, making it a significant appearance not currently tracked.

notes.suggested: Could also note 'Count of Serenno'
notes.detail: Minor: 'Count Dooku' already reflects the Serenno title, but Darth Tyranus is his only alternate identity, so the current altTitle is fine as-is.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Anakin_.description: Anakin Skywalker was a Human Force-sensitive born into slavery on Tatooine, discovered by Jedi Master Qui-Gon Jinn and believed to be the Chosen One prophesied to bring balance to the Force. Trained by Obi-Wan Kenobi, he became one of the most powerful Jedi of his generation and, as a General during the Clone Wars, earned the nickname the "Hero With No Fear." He secretly married Senator Padmé Amidala, and fear of losing her — manipulated by Chancellor Palpatine (Darth Sidious) — drove him to the dark side. Reborn as the Sith Lord Darth Vader, he helped exterminate the Jedi Order and enforce the Galactic Empire's rule for over two decades. Ultimately, confronted by his son Luke Skywalker, he turned against the Emperor to save Luke, fulfilling the prophecy and dying redeemed as Anakin Skywalker.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Anakin_.timeline:
41 BBY - Birth on Tatooine

Anakin is born to Shmi Skywalker, who claimed he had no father. He grows up enslaved on the desert world of Tatooine.

32 BBY - Discovered by Qui-Gon Jinn

At age nine, Anakin is found by Qui-Gon Jinn, wins his freedom by winning the Boonta Eve podrace, and travels to Coruscant. After the Battle of Naboo, Obi-Wan Kenobi vows to train him as a Jedi.

22 BBY - Reunion with Padmé and secret marriage

Now a Padawan, Anakin protects Senator Padmé Amidala, fights at the Battle of Geonosis where he loses his right forearm, and secretly marries Padmé, beginning the Clone Wars.

22-19 BBY - The Clone Wars

Anakin serves as a Jedi General and Knight, takes Ahsoka Tano as his Padawan, and becomes a celebrated war hero known as the "Hero With No Fear."

19 BBY - Fall to the dark side and rebirth as Darth Vader

Seduced by Palpatine's promise to save Padmé from death, Anakin becomes Darth Vader, participates in the destruction of the Jedi Order, is defeated by Obi-Wan on Mustafar, and is rebuilt into the armored Sith Lord.

9 BBY - Hunting the surviving Jedi

As Darth Vader, he pursues fugitive Jedi and duels his former master Obi-Wan Kenobi once more during Obi-Wan's efforts to protect the Skywalker children.

0 BBY - The Battle of Yavin

Vader oversees the Empire's efforts to recover the stolen Death Star plans and participates in the battle over Yavin, where his TIE fighter is knocked away before the station's destruction.

3 ABY - Pursuit of Luke Skywalker

At Cloud City, Vader defeats Luke in a duel, severs his hand, and reveals that he is Luke's father.

4 ABY - Redemption and death

Aboard the second Death Star, Vader turns back to the light to save Luke from Emperor Palpatine, killing the Emperor at the cost of his own life and dying redeemed as Anakin Skywalker.

~11 ABY - Force ghost

As a Force spirit, Anakin appears to his former Padawan Ahsoka Tano, guiding her through a vision of their shared past during the Clone Wars.

birth.source: Wookieepedia/canon; corroborated by ScreenRant age breakdown — age 9 in The Phantom Menace (32 BBY) places birth at 41 BBY

death.source: Return of the Jedi (4 ABY); dies at age 45 aboard the second Death Star after redemption

notes.current: Skywalker earned the moniker & quot;Hero With No Fear" ... His alter ego, Darth Vader...
notes.suggested: Replace with clean HTML (no broken entities).
notes.detail: The existing description contains a mangled HTML entity ('& quot;' with a stray space) that will render as literal broken text. Otherwise the content is roughly accurate but garbled.

notes.current: No 'Chosen One' or affiliation facts tracked
notes.suggested: Consider adding a note that he is the prophesied Chosen One and his Sith identity Darth Vader / Dark Lord of the Sith
notes.detail: The altTitle already captures Darth Vader; the Chosen One status is his single most defining canonical trait and could be surfaced in metadata.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Grievou.description: Grievous, born Qymaen jai Sheelal, was a Kaleesh male warlord from the Wild Space world of Kalee who became the cyborg Supreme Commander of the Droid Army of the Confederacy of Independent Systems during the Clone Wars. A brutal and calculating field commander rather than a Force-sensitive, he was rebuilt into a towering armored cyborg housing little more than his organic brain, eyes and internal organs, and he was trained in lightsaber combat by Count Dooku so that he could hunt Jedi — a pursuit that made him infamous for the collection of lightsabers taken from his kills. Serving directly beneath Dooku and, unknowingly, the Sith Lord Darth Sidious, Grievous led Separatist campaigns across the galaxy from his flagship, including the Malevolence offensive, the capture of Jedi Master Eeth Koth, an attempted invasion of Kamino, and the raid on the Jedi Temple's holocron vault by way of his forces. His greatest strike came in 19 BBY when he led the Separatist attack on Coruscant and abducted Supreme Chancellor Palpatine, only to lose his fleet's flagship and flee to Utapau, where he was cornered and killed by Jedi General Obi-Wan Kenobi — a death that, with Dooku's, effectively decapitated the Separatist war effort on the eve of the Empire.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Grievou.timeline:
Unknown date - Birth on Kalee

Qymaen jai Sheelal is born a Kaleesh male on the Wild Space world of Kalee. Canon sources do not establish a year.

Before 22 BBY - Rebuilt as a cyborg

Sheelal's body is destroyed and he is reconstructed as a heavily armored cyborg, retaining only his brain, eyes and some organs. Taking the name Grievous, he enters the service of the Confederacy of Independent Systems.

Before 22 BBY - Trained by Count Dooku

Count Dooku personally instructs Grievous in lightsaber combat, teaching the non-Force-sensitive general to fight and kill Jedi. Grievous begins collecting the lightsabers of his victims as trophies.

22 BBY - Supreme Commander of the Droid Army

With the outbreak of the Clone Wars, Grievous commands the Separatist droid armies in the field, becoming one of the most feared figures of the war.

~22 BBY - The Malevolence campaign

Grievous commands the ion-cannon warship Malevolence in a terror campaign against Republic fleets and medical stations before Jedi forces cripple and destroy it.

~22 BBY - Battle of Bothawui

Grievous's armored column is ambushed and defeated by Anakin Skywalker and Ahsoka Tano, forcing the general to abandon the field.

~21 BBY - Capture of Eeth Koth

Grievous overwhelms and captures Jedi Master Eeth Koth, broadcasting the humiliation across the galaxy; a Jedi strike team led by Obi-Wan Kenobi rescues Koth, but Grievous escapes.

~21 BBY - Invasion of Kamino

Alongside Asajj Ventress, Grievous leads a Separatist assault on the cloning facilities of Kamino, which is repelled by Republic and clone defenders.

~20 BBY - Defeat on Florrum

Grievous is captured by pirates and Jedi younglings on Florrum and later traded back to the Separatists in a prisoner exchange.

19 BBY - Battle of Coruscant

Grievous leads the surprise Separatist attack on the Republic capital and abducts Supreme Chancellor Palpatine, holding him aboard the flagship Invisible Hand.

19 BBY - Escape from the Invisible Hand

After Anakin Skywalker and Obi-Wan Kenobi board his flagship and Dooku is killed, Grievous flees the crippled cruiser in an escape pod and retreats to Utapau, becoming leader of the Separatist Council in Dooku's absence.

19 BBY - Death at the Battle of Utapau

Tracked to Utapau, Grievous duels Obi-Wan Kenobi and is killed when Kenobi shoots him in his exposed organic sac, igniting him from within. His death precedes the execution of Order 66 and the collapse of the Confederacy.

birth.source: Wookieepedia's canon 'Grievous' article gives a birthplace (Kalee) but no birth date; no canon source states a year. The 60 BBY figure comes from the Legends article (Grievous/Legends), which is not canon.

death.source: Wookieepedia 'Grievous' (canon): killed by Obi-Wan Kenobi at the Battle of Utapau in 19 BBY, as depicted in Episode III: Revenge of the Sith.

notes.suggested: Keep startYearUnknown: true, but consider moving the placeholder to ~-60 or documenting it as arbitrary
notes.detail: Canon gives Grievous no birth year at all — Wookieepedia's canon article states his birthplace (Kalee) but no date. 65 BBY appears to be invented for column placement. The only published figure, 'by 60 BBY,' comes from the Legends article and should not be treated as canon. The guess flag is correct; 65 BBY has no basis in either continuity.

notes.current: Homeworld: Kalee; Species: Kaleesh; Force Sensitive: No
notes.suggested: Add a metadata entry such as 'Affiliation: Confederacy of Independent Systems' and/or 'Rank: Supreme Commander of the Droid Army'
notes.detail: His defining canon attribute is his Separatist command role and his cyborg reconstruction; neither is captured in metadata. Note also that the in-universe title is Supreme Commander of the Droid Army, not 'Supreme Martial Commander' as the old description says.

notes.current: "Grievous held the title of Supreme Martial Commander of the Separatist Droid Army"
notes.suggested: Supreme Commander of the Droid Army
notes.detail: 'Supreme Martial Commander' is the Legends-era title. Canon material uses Supreme Commander of the Droid Army. The old description is a stale lift from an earlier Wookieepedia revision.

The name originated in Legends (the 2005 Visual Dictionary / Labyrinth of Evil era). Wookieepedia's canon article now opens with it, but it was canonized only via later reference material rather than any film or series — worth knowing if you want to distinguish screen-canon from reference-book-canon names.

notes.current: The Clone Wars (TV series), Episode III: Revenge of the Sith
notes.suggested: Add 'The Clone Wars (film)'
notes.detail: Grievous appears in the 2008 theatrical Star Wars: The Clone Wars film, which the entry does not list. (The 2003 Tartakovsky Clone Wars micro-series, where he debuted, is Legends and correctly excluded.)

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Nute_Gu.description: Nute Gunray was a Neimoidian male business executive and politician who rose to become Viceroy of the Trade Federation and, later, one of the most visible figures of the Confederacy of Independent Systems. Greedy, cowardly and easily manipulated, he was recruited by the Sith Lord Darth Sidious, who used the Federation's commercial grievance over the taxation of trade routes as the pretext for the blockade and invasion of Naboo in 32 BBY — a manufactured crisis engineered to topple Supreme Chancellor Finis Valorum and elevate Sidious's public persona, Senator Sheev Palpatine, to the chancellorship. Gunray occupied Naboo, imprisoned its population and pressured Queen Padmé Amidala to sign a treaty legitimizing the occupation, only to be captured when the Naboo and Gungans retook Theed. Despite four trials before the Supreme Court he kept his title and his freedom, and by 22 BBY he had thrown in with Count Dooku's separatist movement, demanding Amidala's death as the price of the Trade Federation's droid armies and signing the Confederacy into being on Geonosis. Throughout the Clone Wars he sat on the Separatist Council, surviving capture on Rodia in a scheme that saw him briefly held by the Republic before Asajj Ventress broke him out. His usefulness ended with the war: hidden on Mustafar with the rest of the Council on Sidious's orders, he was cut down by the newly christened Darth Vader in 19 BBY, the final casualty of the conspiracy he had helped set in motion.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Nute_Gu.timeline:
Birth year unknown - Born on Neimoidia

Nute Gunray was born a Neimoidian male, a species raised in grub hives whose survivalist upbringing bred the acquisitiveness and cowardice that defined him. Canon sources record no birth year.

~34 BBY - Rises to Viceroy of the Trade Federation

Gunray climbed the Trade Federation hierarchy to the office of Viceroy, commanding its vast mercantile fleet and battle droid army, and came under the secret influence of Darth Sidious.

32 BBY - Blockade of Naboo

On Sidious's instruction, Gunray ringed Naboo with Trade Federation battleships in protest of the Republic's taxation of the outlying trade routes, opening the crisis that would make Palpatine Supreme Chancellor.

32 BBY - Attempted murder of the Jedi ambassadors

When Chancellor Valorum sent Jedi Qui-Gon Jinn and Obi-Wan Kenobi to negotiate, Gunray tried to have them killed aboard his flagship with dioxis gas and droidekas; both escaped to the planet below.

32 BBY - Invasion and occupation of Naboo

Gunray landed his droid army, seized Theed, and herded the Naboo into camps while pressing Queen Amidala to sign a treaty that would make the occupation legal. She refused and fled offworld.

32 BBY - Defeat at the Battle of Naboo and capture

Amidala returned with Gungan allies; when Anakin Skywalker destroyed the Droid Control Ship, Gunray's army shut down and he was taken prisoner in his own throne room and delivered to the Republic.

Between 32 and 22 BBY - Four trials, no punishment

Gunray faced four trials before the Supreme Court and escaped meaningful sanction, retaining his viceroyalty — an outcome that deepened public cynicism about Republic justice.

22 BBY - Backs the Separatist movement and demands Amidala's death

Approached by Count Dooku on Geonosis, Gunray agreed to commit the Trade Federation's droid armies to the Confederacy only on the condition that Senator Padmé Amidala be assassinated; earlier attempts on her life on Coruscant were made at his urging.

22 BBY - Signs the Confederacy into existence

In the Geonosian war room Gunray signed the treaty founding the Confederacy of Independent Systems alongside the Techno Union, Banking Clan and Commerce Guild, and took a seat on the Separatist Council.

22 BBY - Battle of Geonosis

The Clone Wars erupted with the Republic's assault on Geonosis. Gunray fled the planet as his droid forces were routed, and spent the war moving between hidden Separatist strongholds.

~21 BBY - Capture on Rodia

Lured to Rodia by Senator Onaconda Farr's feigned defection, Gunray was arrested by Padmé Amidala and Jar Jar Binks and taken into Republic custody as the highest-value Separatist prisoner of the war.

~21 BBY - Escape aboard the Tranquility

While being transported for interrogation by Jedi Luminara Unduli and Ahsoka Tano, Gunray was freed by Asajj Ventress and the treacherous Senate Guard captain Argyus, returning to the Separatist Council.

19 BBY - Ordered into hiding on Mustafar

With Dooku and General Grievous dead and the war ending, Darth Sidious directed Gunray and the remaining Separatist leaders to the volcanic world of Mustafar, promising their safety.

19 BBY - Killed by Darth Vader

Anakin Skywalker, newly anointed Darth Vader, arrived on Mustafar and slaughtered the Separatist Council. Gunray, begging for mercy and reminding Vader that Sidious had promised him safety, was the last to be cut down — ending the Confederacy and the Clone Wars.

birth.source: Canon sources give no birth year for Nute Gunray. Wookieepedia's canon article lists no birth date in its infobox; the only age discussion (the Wookieepedia forum thread "KB:Nute Gunray's age") concerns Legends material and reached no canon conclusion.

death.source: Wookieepedia (Nute Gunray, canon) and Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith — Gunray is killed by Darth Vader on Mustafar in 19 BBY, the last of the Separatist Council to die.

notes.suggested: Keep flagged as a guess; no canon basis for 69 BBY specifically
notes.detail: Canon gives no birth year for Nute Gunray — the Wookieepedia canon article's infobox carries no birth date. The only age discussion is a Wookieepedia forum thread about his Legends age, which is not canon and reached no firm figure. The flag is correctly set; just be aware 69 BBY is entirely invented and should not be presented anywhere as a date.

notes.suggested: Verify or remove
notes.detail: Gunray's presence in the 2008 The Clone Wars theatrical film is doubtful — the Separatist side there is Dooku, Ventress, Ziro and Jabba. He is unambiguously in The Clone Wars TV series (notably the Rodia capture and the Tranquility escape). Worth checking a credits list before keeping the movie entry.

notes.suggested: Consider adding Tales of the Empire / other appearances only if verified
notes.detail: Our list covers the core five plus the TV series, which is reasonable for a plotted timeline. No confirmed omission found, but the entry does not note his significant non-film canon appearances in reference works (e.g. the Star Wars: Complete Vehicles / Visual Dictionary line), which is fine for this format.

notes.suggested: Add an Affiliation field: Trade Federation (Viceroy) / Confederacy of Independent Systems (Separatist Council)
notes.detail: His defining fact is institutional rather than personal — Viceroy of the Trade Federation and founding Separatist Council member. Tracking affiliation would let him link to Dooku, Grievous and the CIS era rows.

notes.suggested: Correct as-is; optionally record cause: killed by Darth Vader on Mustafar
notes.detail: Death year 19 BBY and the film are both confirmed. Adding the killer/location would be useful, since Gunray is the specific character Vader kills last in that sequence.

notes.current: Existing description ends at the four Supreme Court trials
notes.suggested: Extend through the Separatist Council years and his death on Mustafar
notes.detail: The current text is accurate but stops around 32 BBY, omitting the Geonosis treaty, his demand for Amidala's assassination, his Clone Wars capture on Rodia, and his execution by Vader — which is the event our own endYear is built on.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Qui-Gon.description: Qui-Gon Jinn was a Force-sensitive human male Jedi Master who lived during the final decades of the Galactic Republic. Trained by Count Dooku and later mentor to Obi-Wan Kenobi, he was respected within the Jedi Order for his wisdom and skill, yet his maverick adherence to the philosophy of the Living Force — following the will of the Force even when it conflicted with the High Council — kept him at odds with the Jedi Council, whose offered seat he never took up on the Council's terms. During the Naboo crisis of 32 BBY he discovered the young slave Anakin Skywalker on Tatooine and became convinced the boy was the prophesied Chosen One destined to bring balance to the Force. Qui-Gon was struck down by the Sith Lord Darth Maul during the Battle of Naboo; with his dying breath he charged Obi-Wan with training Anakin. He later became one of the first Jedi to learn to retain his consciousness after death, returning as a guiding voice through the Force.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Qui-Gon.timeline:
~80 BBY - Birth

Qui-Gon Jinn is born to a family on a terrestrial world that remains unidentified in canon, leaving the planet as an infant to be raised within the Jedi Order.

~72 BBY - Training in the Jedi Order

Identified as strongly Force-sensitive, Qui-Gon is trained at the Jedi Temple on Coruscant and eventually taken as the Padawan of Jedi Master Count Dooku.

~44 BBY - Master and Apprentice

Now a Jedi Master, Qui-Gon takes the young Obi-Wan Kenobi as his Padawan learner. Around this period he undertakes the fateful mission to Pijal recounted in the novel Master & Apprentice, and is offered but declines a seat on the Jedi High Council.

32 BBY - The Naboo Blockade

Sent with Obi-Wan by Chancellor Valorum to negotiate with the Trade Federation over its blockade of Naboo, Qui-Gon escapes an assassination attempt and reaches the surface, rescuing Queen Amidala and the Gungan Jar Jar Binks.

32 BBY - Discovery of Anakin Skywalker

Stranded on Tatooine, Qui-Gon meets the slave boy Anakin Skywalker, senses his extraordinary connection to the Force, and wins his freedom through a podrace, believing him to be the Chosen One.

32 BBY - Duel on Naboo and Death

During the liberation of Naboo, Qui-Gon duels the Sith Lord Darth Maul in the Theed generator complex and is mortally wounded. As he dies, he asks Obi-Wan to train Anakin as a Jedi.

~19 BBY and beyond - Voice in the Force

Having learned to preserve his identity after death, Qui-Gon's spirit later guides Obi-Wan Kenobi and Yoda, and speaks to Obi-Wan during his years of exile.

birth.source: Wookieepedia gives 80 BBY, a figure derived (not stated in-universe) from Master & Apprentice-era age references placing him around 40 in the years before The Phantom Menace; widely accepted canon date.

death.source: Killed by Darth Maul during the Battle of Naboo in Episode I: The Phantom Menace, dated 32 BBY.

notes.suggested: Unidentified
notes.detail: In current canon Qui-Gon's homeworld is explicitly unidentified — Wookieepedia maintains a dedicated 'Qui-Gon Jinn's homeworld' article describing an unnamed terrestrial world he left as an infant and later revisited (source of the River of Light Force-stone). He was raised on Coruscant at the Jedi Temple but was not born there. 'Coruscant' as homeworld appears to come from non-canon/fan wikis.

notes.suggested: -80 with a 'guess' flag (startYearUnknown: true)
notes.detail: 80 BBY is the accepted date but is calculated from age references rather than stated outright in canon; the older Legends birth year was 92 BBY. Worth flagging as estimated rather than asserting it as firmly known.

notes.current: Episode I: The Phantom Menace only
notes.suggested: Add later Force-spirit/voice appearances
notes.detail: Qui-Gon returns as a disembodied Force voice or spirit in The Clone Wars, Tales of the Jedi, and notably appears (Liam Neeson) as a Force ghost in the Obi-Wan Kenobi (2022) series finale. These are post-death appearances but are real canon appearances the timeline could note.

notes.suggested: Master: Count Dooku (Dooku); Apprentice: Obi-Wan Kenobi
notes.detail: His lineage — trained by Dooku, trained Obi-Wan — is a defining canon fact worth surfacing in metadata.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Grogu.description: Grogu, affectionately nicknamed "Baby Yoda" and known to many simply as "the Child," is a Force-sensitive foundling belonging to the same mysterious species as Jedi Grand Master Yoda and Jedi Master Yaddle. Born around 41 BBY, he was raised as an initiate at the Jedi Temple on Coruscant during the Clone Wars before being rescued from the Great Jedi Purge and hidden away for decades. Long afterward he was tracked down by the Mandalorian bounty hunter Din Djarin, who defied his Imperial-aligned clients to protect the child, forging a deep father-and-son bond. Grogu briefly began Jedi training under Luke Skywalker but ultimately chose his attachment to Din over the Jedi path, was formally adopted as "Din Grogu," and continued at the Mandalorian's side. He is remarkable for his precocious command of the Force despite his youth and for embodying the intersection of the Jedi and Mandalorian traditions.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Grogu.timeline:
~41 BBY - Birth

Grogu is born, a member of the same rare, unnamed species as Yoda. His homeworld is never identified in canon. This year is derived from his stated age of fifty during the events of 9 ABY.

~41 BBY to 19 BBY - Raised at the Jedi Temple

Grogu is taken to the Jedi Temple on Coruscant and raised as a Jedi initiate through the final years of the Republic and the Clone Wars, trained by several masters.

19 BBY - Rescue from the Great Jedi Purge

During Order 66 and the fall of the Jedi Order, the young Grogu is rescued from the Jedi Temple by Jedi Master Kelleran Beq with the aid of sympathetic Naboo forces, and is subsequently hidden away.

19 BBY to 9 ABY - Decades in hiding

For roughly the next fifty years Grogu's memories become clouded and his whereabouts unknown, as he is concealed from those who would exploit his connection to the Force.

9 ABY - Found by Din Djarin

The Mandalorian Din Djarin accepts a bounty and locates Grogu, but rather than surrender the child to his Imperial-aligned clients he rescues him, beginning their journey together across the galaxy.

9 ABY - Protected from the Empire

Din Djarin shields Grogu from Moff Gideon and Imperial remnant forces seeking the child for his blood, which is rich in the Force, as the pair seek out the Jedi.

9 ABY - Training under Luke Skywalker

After Grogu is captured and rescued, Luke Skywalker arrives and takes the child to begin Jedi training, offering him the choice between the Jedi path and his bond with Din.

9 ABY - Choosing the Mandalorian bond

Grogu chooses Din Djarin's beskar chain mail over Yoda's lightsaber, leaving Luke's tutelage to reunite with the Mandalorian in The Book of Boba Fett.

9 ABY - Adventures and adoption as Din Grogu

Grogu accompanies Din Djarin through further trials, including the retaking of Mandalore, and is formally adopted as a Mandalorian apprentice under the name "Din Grogu."

birth.source: Wookieepedia/Wikipedia give ~41 BBY, derived from his stated age of 50 during The Mandalorian Season 1 (9 ABY); 9 + 41 = 50. No source states 41 BBY as an explicit birth date.

death.source: Grogu does not die in canon; he remains alive as of the most recent appearances. Our column end of 50 ABY is a rendering bound, not a death.

notes.current: birth 41 BBY presented as KNOWN (startYearUnknown absent/false)
notes.suggested: mark as estimated (startYearUnknown: true / "~41 BBY")
notes.detail: Canon never states 41 BBY as an explicit birth year. It is derived from Grogu being 'fifty years old' during The Mandalorian (9 ABY). The value is well-founded but is an estimate, not an asserted fact, so it should not be presented as known.

notes.suggested: consider noting 'Din Grogu' (adopted name) and 'Baby Yoda' (popular nickname)
notes.detail: Grogu was formally adopted as 'Din Grogu' by Season 3, and 'Baby Yoda' is his ubiquitous fandom nickname. The current altTitle only captures 'The Child.'

notes.suggested: note his rescue from the Jedi Temple by Kelleran Beq during Order 66 (19 BBY)
notes.detail: A significant canon milestone establishing how he survived the Purge; depicted in the Kelleran Beq rescue sequence, later canonized on-screen.

notes.suggested: keep endYearUnknown true
notes.detail: Correct as-is: Grogu is alive with no canon death. Flagging only to confirm the 50 ABY bound is a rendering value, not a death year.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Bo-Kata.description: Bo-Katan Kryze was a human female Mandalorian warrior of Clan Kryze and the younger sister of Duchess Satine Kryze, who rose from insurgent to Mand'alor and ultimately reunified her shattered people. Rejecting Satine's pacifist New Mandalorian government, she joined the terrorist splinter group Death Watch and led its elite Nite Owls under Pre Vizsla, helping Vizsla ally with the Sith Lord Maul's Shadow Collective to seize Mandalore in 20 BBY. When Maul killed Vizsla, claimed the Darksaber and murdered Satine, Bo-Katan broke away and fought Maul's regime as a resistance leader, aiding Obi-Wan Kenobi's escape and then joining Ahsoka Tano and the Republic's 501st Legion in the Siege of Mandalore in 19 BBY, after which she was installed as regent — only to be discarded when the Galactic Empire rose weeks later. She spent the Imperial Era leading a Mandalorian resistance, accepting the Darksaber from Sabine Wren in 1 BBY to become Mand'alor, but her claim ended in catastrophe when she surrendered the blade to Moff Gideon and the Empire carried out the Great Purge of Mandalore and the Night of a Thousand Tears. Broken and exiled, she was drawn back into the fight in 9 ABY by the Mandalorian Din Djarin: she guided him to Ahsoka Tano, helped capture Gideon, later rescued Din from the Mandalorian mines, glimpsed a living mythosaur in the Living Waters, and — after her castle on Kalevala was destroyed — bridged the divide between the exiled clans and the Children of the Watch. Leading the united Mandalorians in the Battle of Mandalore, she saw Gideon killed and the Darksaber destroyed, and reignited the Great Forge as the accepted leader of a people finally returning home.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Bo-Kata.timeline:
Date unknown - Birth into Clan Kryze

Bo-Katan was born a human female princess of Clan Kryze, of House Kryze, on Mandalore. Canon has never established her birth year or her age relative to any fixed event; she was the younger sister of Satine Kryze.

~21 BBY - Death Watch exiled from the Mandalore system

Rejecting her sister's pacifist New Mandalorian rule, Bo-Katan sided with Death Watch and its leader Pre Vizsla. After Vizsla was publicly exposed as the group's leader, Death Watch was driven from Mandalorian space and operated from exile.

~20 BBY - Nite Owls on Carlac

Commanding the Nite Owls, Bo-Katan helped run Death Watch's camp on the frozen world of Carlac, where the group terrorized a Ming Po village. She clashed with Ahsoka Tano, who exposed Death Watch's brutality to Lux Bonteri.

~20 BBY - Alliance with Maul and the Shadow Collective

Bo-Katan and Vizsla discovered the stranded Sith brothers Maul and Savage Opress. Together they forged the Shadow Collective, coercing the Black Sun, the Pyke Syndicate and the Hutts into a criminal army aimed at Mandalore.

~20 BBY - The takeover of Mandalore

With the Collective's crime waves staged to discredit Satine's government, Death Watch swept in as liberators. Satine was imprisoned and Vizsla installed himself as ruler, with Bo-Katan at his side.

~20 BBY - Maul seizes the Darksaber and Satine dies

Maul defeated and beheaded Vizsla in single combat, taking the Darksaber and rule of Mandalore. Bo-Katan refused to serve an outsider and led loyalist Nite Owls into open resistance; she freed Obi-Wan Kenobi, but Maul murdered Satine before their eyes, and Bo-Katan covered Kenobi's escape.

19 BBY - The Siege of Mandalore

Unable to dislodge Maul alone, Bo-Katan appealed to the Republic. Allied with Ahsoka Tano and Captain Rex's 501st Legion, she fought through Sundari's undercity; Maul was captured and Bo-Katan's forces retook the capital.

19 BBY - Regent of Mandalore, then discarded

Installed by the Republic as regent, her authority evaporated within weeks. Order 66 and the proclamation of the Galactic Empire ended the Clone Wars, and the new regime replaced her with compliant Imperial Mandalorian rulers.

1 BBY - Accepting the Darksaber

Fighting alongside Sabine Wren, Bo-Katan helped rescue Alrich Wren and destroy the Duchess, an Imperial weapon that vaporized Mandalorians inside their beskar armor. Sabine offered her the Darksaber, and Bo-Katan accepted it in her sister's memory, hailed as Mand'alor by the assembled clans.

~4 BBY to 4 ABY - Leading the Mandalorian resistance

As Mand'alor she rallied clans against Imperial occupation, but Mandalore's insurgency drew ever harsher Imperial reprisals.

~4 ABY to 5 ABY - The Great Purge and the Night of a Thousand Tears

Moff Gideon promised to spare Mandalorian lives and cities if Bo-Katan submitted and disarmed. She surrendered the Darksaber to him; he betrayed her, and the Empire bombarded Mandalore into a glassed ruin, scattering the survivors.

9 ABY - Meeting Din Djarin on Trask

Hunting Imperial weapons shipments with Koska Reeves and Axe Woves, Bo-Katan rescued Din Djarin, dismissed his Children of the Watch creed as a cult, and directed him to Ahsoka Tano in exchange for his help hijacking an Imperial freighter.

9 ABY - Boarding Gideon's cruiser

She joined Din's strike team to rescue Grogu and capture Moff Gideon. Din defeated Gideon and won the Darksaber, and Bo-Katan — bound by the tradition that the blade must be won in combat — refused to take it as a gift.

9 ABY - Exile on Kalevala

Her followers abandoned her for Axe Woves' mercenary fleet, and Bo-Katan withdrew alone to Kryze Castle on Kalevala.

9 ABY - The mines of Mandalore and the mythosaur

Answering a plea from Din, she descended into the ruined mines beneath Sundari, rescued him from a cybernetic Imperial creature, and saw a living mythosaur beneath the Living Waters — proving Mandalore was not cursed and remained habitable.

9 ABY - Kalevala destroyed

Gideon's forces bombarded Kryze Castle. Rescued by Din, Bo-Katan accepted refuge with his covert after being confirmed by the Armorer as one who had walked the Living Waters.

9 ABY - Reuniting the clans

Sent by the Armorer to gather the scattered Mandalorians, she reclaimed Axe Woves' fleet and persuaded exiles and the Children of the Watch to fight as one people.

9 ABY - The Battle of Mandalore

The united Mandalorians assaulted Gideon's hidden base. Gideon destroyed the Darksaber in combat with Bo-Katan and taunted that she had lost everything, but he was killed and the planet retaken.

9 ABY - Reigniting the Great Forge

With Mandalore reclaimed and reconstruction begun, Bo-Katan relit the Great Forge, leading her reunified people without need of the Darksaber. She remains alive; canon records no death.

birth.source: No canon source states a birth year. Wookieepedia's Bo-Katan Kryze article gives no birth date in its infobox, and the site's own talk/discussion pages confirm her age has never been established in canon. Fan estimates (~60 BBY, extrapolated from Obi-Wan Kenobi being ~19 when he met her older sister Satine) are not canon.

death.source: Bo-Katan is alive at the end of The Mandalorian Season 3 (9 ABY), having survived the Battle of Mandalore and reignited the Great Forge as leader of the reunified Mandalorians. No canon source depicts or dates her death.

notes.suggested: Keep the unknown flag; consider ~60 BBY if you want to align with the most common estimate
notes.detail: Canon genuinely gives no birth year for Bo-Katan — Wookieepedia's infobox lists none, and the site's own discussion pages confirm it. Your "guess" flag is correct and should stay. The widely repeated fan estimate is ~60 BBY, derived from tie-in material placing Obi-Wan at roughly 19 when he guarded Satine (born 57 BBY, so ~38-40 BBY) plus a small age gap between the sisters; none of that is canon-stated. 55 BBY is defensible but arbitrary.

notes.current: Description stops mid-Clone Wars, ending with Vizsla taking control of Mandalore, while the opening line already asserts she "later during the Imperial Era, became Mand'alor."
notes.suggested: Replace with the new description covering the Siege of Mandalore, the Darksaber, the Great Purge and The Mandalorian Season 3
notes.detail: The current text is a truncated lift of an older Wookieepedia intro. It omits every major beat after 20 BBY: her break with Maul, Satine's murder, the Siege of Mandalore (19 BBY), receiving the Darksaber from Sabine (1 BBY), surrendering it to Moff Gideon during the Great Purge, and her arc across The Mandalorian Seasons 2-3 ending with her leading a reunified Mandalore. It also stops at a point that makes her look like a Death Watch loyalist, which is the opposite of where she ends up.

notes.current: The Clone Wars, Star Wars Rebels, The Mandalorian
notes.suggested: Optionally note season-level scope: The Clone Wars (Seasons 4, 5, 7), Rebels (Season 4), The Mandalorian (Seasons 2, 3)
notes.detail: The list is accurate, but her appearances are narrow within each show. She debuts in The Clone Wars Season 4 ("A Friend in Need"), returns for the Shadow Collective arc in Season 5 and the Siege of Mandalore in Season 7; in Rebels she appears only in Season 4's "Heroes of Mandalore" (plus the finale montage); in The Mandalorian she appears in Seasons 2 and 3. She does not appear in The Book of Boba Fett.

notes.current: No entry for The Mandalorian & Grogu (released May 22, 2026)
notes.suggested: Do not add until her appearance is confirmed
notes.detail: Katee Sackhoff has repeatedly declined to confirm or deny Bo-Katan's presence in the film, saying only "you haven't seen the last of her" and that she "had not been in the armor in 2025" (filming wrapped December 2024). Coverage of the film's confirmed cameos does not list her. Worth re-checking, but it should not be asserted.

notes.suggested: Add e.g. Title: Mand'alor (1 BBY–c. 4 ABY; again 9 ABY), Affiliation: Death Watch / Nite Owls / Mandalorian resistance, Family: sister of Duchess Satine Kryze
notes.detail: Her sibling relationship to Satine and her tenure as Mand'alor are the two facts that most define her position on a timeline and are currently only buried in prose. Homeworld (Mandalore), Species (Human) and Force Sensitive (No) all check out against the official databank.

notes.suggested: If you plot the Great Purge, use c. 4 ABY–5 ABY
notes.detail: Wookieepedia dates the Great Purge of Mandalore, including the Night of a Thousand Tears, to between 4 ABY and 5 ABY. This dating derives from reference material (Star Wars: Timelines) rather than on-screen dialogue, and older fan sources place it much earlier in the Imperial Era, so treat it as reference-book precision rather than settled screen canon.

notes.suggested: No change required, but her documented story currently ends in 9 ABY
notes.detail: Flagging only so it is a deliberate choice: nothing in canon depicts Bo-Katan past The Mandalorian Season 3 (9 ABY), so the column drawn out to 50 ABY is pure rendering bound. The endYearUnknown flag is correct — she is alive, not dead.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Wilhuff.description: Wilhuff Tarkin was an Eriaduan bureaucrat, military officer, and politician whose career bridged the final decades of the Galactic Republic and the rise of the Galactic Empire. Born into the prestigious, militaristic Tarkin family in 64 BBY, he served in the Republic's Outland Regions Security Force and as Governor of Eriadu before returning to military service during the Clone Wars, where he became a close ally of Chancellor Sheev Palpatine. Under the Empire he rose to become the first Grand Moff, commanding the Outer Rim territories, and he championed the "Tarkin Doctrine" of rule through fear of overwhelming force. He is best known for overseeing the completion of the first Death Star, ordering the destruction of Alderaan, and dying aboard the battle station when it was destroyed by Luke Skywalker at the Battle of Yavin in 0 BBY.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Wilhuff.timeline:
64 BBY - Birth on Eriadu

Wilhuff Tarkin is born on the Outer Rim world of Eriadu, a scion of the ambitious and militaristic Tarkin family.

Republic Era - Early Career and Governorship

Tarkin serves in the Republic's Outland Regions Security Force before entering politics, eventually becoming lieutenant governor and then Governor of Eriadu and the Seswenna sector.

~22 BBY - Clone Wars Service

With the outbreak of the Clone Wars, Tarkin renews his military service as a captain in the Republic Navy, serving under Jedi General Even Piell.

~21 BBY - Imprisonment at the Citadel

Captured and held at the Separatist Citadel prison, Tarkin is rescued alongside Even Piell by a Jedi-led team including Anakin Skywalker and Ahsoka Tano, deepening his ties to the Republic's leadership.

~19 BBY - Rise Under Palpatine

Promoted to admiral and increasingly trusted by Chancellor Palpatine, Tarkin becomes a leading figure in the militarization of the Republic as the Clone Wars draw to a close.

19 BBY - Founding of the Empire

With the proclamation of the Galactic Empire, Tarkin is named its first Moff and is given oversight of the secret Death Star project, helping formulate the Tarkin Doctrine of rule through fear.

~18-4 BBY - Grand Moff and Imperial Enforcer

Elevated to the rank of Grand Moff, Tarkin is granted authority over the Outer Rim. He pursues rebel cells, oversees Imperial projects, and works to bring the Death Star to completion.

0 BBY - Death Star Operational and Destruction of Alderaan

The Death Star becomes operational under Tarkin's command. To demonstrate its power and coerce Princess Leia Organa, he orders the annihilation of the planet Alderaan.

0 BBY - Death at the Battle of Yavin

Refusing to evacuate the battle station, Tarkin is killed when Luke Skywalker destroys the Death Star during the Battle of Yavin.

birth.source: Wookieepedia (Wilhuff Tarkin) and corroborating Star Wars references state he was born in 64 BBY on Eriadu.

death.source: Killed aboard the first Death Star when it was destroyed at the Battle of Yavin, 0 BBY (A New Hope).

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Maul.description: Maul, originally styled Darth Maul, was a Force-sensitive Dathomirian Zabrak male who rose from a Nightbrother of Dathomir to become the secret Sith apprentice of Darth Sidious. Trained from childhood in the dark side and an unmatched practitioner of Juyo lightsaber combat wielding a double-bladed saber, he was unleashed on Naboo, where he killed Jedi Master Qui-Gon Jinn before being bisected by Obi-Wan Kenobi and left for dead. He survived in madness for years until his brother Savage Opress recovered him and Mother Talzin restored his mind and gave him cybernetic legs. Consumed by vengeance against Kenobi and the Jedi, he built the Shadow Collective and seized control of Mandalore, only to be defeated and cast aside by Sidious. In the years after the Clone Wars he operated as a crime lord, leading the Crimson Dawn syndicate and later scheming as a shadowy warlord. His decades-long obsession ended on Tatooine, where he was slain in a final duel with Obi-Wan Kenobi.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Maul.timeline:
54 BBY - Birth on Dathomir

Maul is born a Nightbrother of the Zabrak clans on Dathomir, a son of the Nightsister leader Mother Talzin.

~54 BBY - Taken by Darth Sidious

As a young child, Maul is given to or taken by Darth Sidious, who raises and trains him in secret as his Sith apprentice, Darth Maul.

32 BBY - Duel on Naboo

Sent by Sidious during the Naboo crisis, Maul kills Jedi Master Qui-Gon Jinn but is bisected by Obi-Wan Kenobi and falls down a reactor shaft, presumed dead.

~32 BBY - Survival on Lotho Minor

Sustained by the dark side and hatred, a broken Maul survives on the junk world of Lotho Minor for years, his lower body replaced by spider-like appendages as his mind fractures.

~20 BBY - Rescue and restoration

Savage Opress locates his brother and brings him to Mother Talzin, who restores Maul's sanity and fits him with cybernetic legs, reigniting his quest for revenge against Obi-Wan Kenobi.

~20 BBY - The Shadow Collective and Mandalore

Maul unites Death Watch and criminal syndicates into the Shadow Collective, seizes control of Mandalore, and kills Duchess Satine Kryze to torment Kenobi.

~20 BBY - Betrayed by Sidious

Darth Sidious arrives on Mandalore, kills Savage Opress, and defeats and captures Maul, unwilling to tolerate a rival.

19 BBY - Siege of Mandalore

Having escaped and retaken Mandalore, Maul is confronted by Ahsoka Tano and Republic forces; captured, he escapes amid the chaos of Order 66 as the Republic falls.

~13 BBY - Shadow warlord

During the early Imperial era Maul continues to build his hidden power base and manipulate criminal and dark-side factions from the shadows.

~10 BBY - Crimson Dawn

Maul secretly leads the Crimson Dawn crime syndicate, directing its operations through lieutenants such as Qi'ra during the events surrounding Han Solo's early exploits.

2 BBY - Death on Tatooine

Tracking Obi-Wan Kenobi to Tatooine, Maul confronts his old enemy in a brief final duel, is struck down, and dies in Kenobi's arms.

birth.source: Wookieepedia "Maul" article — born 54 BBY on Dathomir

death.source: Wookieepedia "Maul" — killed by Obi-Wan Kenobi on Tatooine in Star Wars Rebels episode "Twin Suns," 2 BBY (thirty years after their 32 BBY encounter)

notes.suggested: Dathomirian Zabrak
notes.detail: Wookieepedia classifies Maul specifically as a Dathomirian Zabrak (a Zabrak raised among the Nightbrothers of Dathomir), which is more precise than the generic 'Zabrak.'

notes.suggested: Star Wars Rebels — "Twin Suns"
notes.detail: His death occurs specifically in the Rebels episode 'Twin Suns' (Season 3), in 2 BBY, killed by Obi-Wan Kenobi on Tatooine. Naming the episode adds useful precision.

notes.suggested: Add a note on parentage / affiliations (son of Mother Talzin; Sith apprentice, later Crimson Dawn / Shadow Collective leader)
notes.detail: Canon establishes Maul as the son of Nightsister Mother Talzin and covers major affiliations (Sith, Shadow Collective, Crimson Dawn) not reflected in the tracked metadata.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Huyang.description: Huyang was a Mark IV architect droid and Jedi Order professor who, for over a thousand generations, instructed younglings in the construction of their lightsabers following the Gathering — the rite in which each initiate retrieved a kyber crystal. Operating for millennia aboard the Jedi training vessel Crucible, he claimed to have overseen the lightsaber construction of nearly every Jedi in the Order's history, including Grand Master Yoda and Mace Windu. During the Clone Wars he worked alongside Padawan Ahsoka Tano while escorting a group of younglings to Ilum, and after the fall of the Jedi Order he became Ahsoka's longtime companion and pilot aboard her T-6 shuttle. Decades later, during the New Republic era, he aided Ahsoka, Sabine Wren, and Hera Syndulla in their search for the missing Ezra Bridger and Grand Admiral Thrawn, remaining a steadfast repository of Jedi knowledge and one of the few living links to the Order's ancient past.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Huyang.timeline:
25020 BBY - Manufacture

Huyang is constructed as a Mark IV architect droid a few years after the founding of the Jedi Order, tasked with guiding younglings in building their lightsabers.

25020 BBY–20 BBY - A Thousand Generations of Teaching

For over a thousand generations Huyang serves aboard the Jedi vessel Crucible, overseeing the lightsaber construction of countless initiates, including future Masters Yoda and Mace Windu.

~20 BBY - The Gathering on Ilum

During the Clone Wars, Huyang accompanies Ahsoka Tano and a group of younglings to the ice caves of Ilum for the Gathering, where each youngling retrieves a kyber crystal.

~20 BBY - Building Lightsabers Aboard the Crucible

Huyang instructs the younglings in assembling their first lightsabers as they journey home, before the Crucible is attacked by the pirate Hondo Ohnaka's gang.

~20 BBY - Captured and Rescued

Damaged and dismantled during the pirate raid, Huyang is later reassembled and helps Ahsoka and the younglings escape, reaffirming his bond with them.

19 BBY - Companion to Ahsoka

Sometime after the end of the Clone Wars, Huyang joins Ahsoka Tano aboard her T-6 shuttle, becoming her enduring traveling companion.

9 ABY - Searching for Ezra and Thrawn

During the New Republic era, Huyang assists Ahsoka, Sabine Wren, and Hera Syndulla in their quest to locate the lost Ezra Bridger and prevent the return of Grand Admiral Thrawn, surviving the campaign that carries them to the distant galaxy of Peridea.

birth.source: Star Wars: Timelines (2023 DK reference book) dates Huyang's construction to 25,020 BBY, shortly after the founding of the Jedi Order; carried on Wookieepedia and corroborated by secondary coverage.

death.source: No death in canon. Huyang survives the events of the Ahsoka series (c. 9 ABY) intact and remains active with Ahsoka Tano.

notes.suggested: Add Model/Class: 'Mark IV architect droid' and, if desired, a Homeworld/Origin note
notes.detail: Our metadata only lists the generic 'Droid'. Huyang's specific designation is a Mark IV architect droid, which is central to his identity as the Jedi lightsaber-construction professor. His creation year (25,020 BBY) derives from the Star Wars: Timelines reference book, so it exists only in a companion source rather than on screen — worth flagging as reference-book-only precision.

notes.suggested: Consider whether to also plot his brief cameo appearances outside these two
notes.detail: The two listed appearances are correct and are his major roles (The Clone Wars Season 5 'Gathering' arc, and Ahsoka). No correction needed, but the maintainer may wish to confirm no other tracked title should include him.

notes.suggested: Keep -25020 but flag as an approximate, reference-book-sourced figure
notes.detail: The 25,020 BBY construction date comes solely from the non-screen reference book Star Wars: Timelines and is tied to the approximate founding date of the Jedi Order, so it is best treated as approximate ('~') rather than an exact canon on-screen fact.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Kanan_J.description: Kanan Jarrus, born Caleb Dume, was a Force-sensitive human male Jedi Knight who survived Order 66 and became a founding member of the rebellion against the Galactic Empire. Trained as a Padawan by Jedi Master Depa Billaba, he was on Kaller when the clone troopers turned on the Jedi; his master's dying sacrifice allowed him to escape, and he shed his identity, abandoned the Jedi ways, and lived as a fugitive under the name Kanan Jarrus. After meeting the Twi'lek pilot Hera Syndulla during the Gorse conflict, he joined her nascent rebel cell aboard the Ghost. He is best known for leading that crew, for reluctantly returning to the Jedi path to train the orphan Ezra Bridger as his own Padawan, and for his role in the early Alliance to Restore the Republic. Blinded in a duel with Maul on Malachor, he continued to fight alongside his found family until he gave his life on Lothal to save Hera, Ezra, and Sabine from an exploding fuel depot.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Kanan_J.timeline:
33 BBY - Birth on Coruscant

Caleb Dume is born on the galactic capital of Coruscant, a Force-sensitive human who is later identified and taken into the Jedi Order for training.

~22 BBY - Padawan to Depa Billaba

Around the outbreak of the Clone Wars, Caleb becomes the Padawan of Jedi Master Depa Billaba, serving alongside her and clone troopers in the conflict.

19 BBY - Order 66 on Kaller

While on Kaller with the Bad Batch clone squad and his master, Palpatine issues Order 66. Depa Billaba sacrifices herself so Caleb can flee, and the young Padawan narrowly escapes execution by the clones.

~19 BBY - Becoming Kanan Jarrus

Now a hunted fugitive, Caleb meets the smuggler Janus Kasmir, who teaches him to survive off the grid. He abandons the Jedi ways, adopts the alias Kanan Jarrus, and largely trades his lightsaber for a blaster.

~11 BBY - The Gorse Conflict

During the Gorse conflict, Kanan meets the Twi'lek pilot and rebel operative Hera Syndulla. Drawn to her cause, he agrees to join her growing rebel cell aboard the Ghost.

~5 BBY - Meeting Ezra Bridger

Operating out of Lothal with the Ghost crew, Kanan encounters the street orphan Ezra Bridger. Sensing the boy's strength in the Force, he reluctantly takes him on as his Padawan, returning to the Jedi path he once abandoned.

~4 BBY - Duel with the Grand Inquisitor

Kanan is captured and interrogated by the Empire, then rescued by his crew. In the ensuing confrontation he defeats the Grand Inquisitor, an early victory against the Imperial hunt for surviving Jedi.

~3 BBY - Blinded on Malachor

On the ancient Sith world of Malachor, Kanan is blinded by Maul during the battle. He adapts to his loss of sight, continuing to fight and train Ezra while wearing a protective mask over his eyes.

1 BBY - Death on Lothal

During the mission to rescue Hera on Lothal, Kanan uses the Force to hold back the flames of an exploding fuel depot, sacrificing himself so that Hera, Ezra, and Sabine can escape. He dies a Jedi, having come full circle from the frightened Padawan of Order 66.

birth.source: Wookieepedia / StarWars.com Databank: born Caleb Dume on Coruscant in 33 BBY

death.source: Wookieepedia: died on Lothal in 1 BBY, sacrificing himself in the Star Wars Rebels episode 'Jedi Night'

notes.suggested: 1 BBY (confirmed)
notes.detail: Automatic check: death year in data.json disagrees with the year Claude verified. Wookieepedia: died on Lothal in 1 BBY, sacrificing himself in the Star Wars Rebels episode 'Jedi Night'

notes.current: 0 BBY, presented as a known death year
notes.suggested: 1 BBY
notes.detail: Canon sources (Wookieepedia, StarWars.com) place Kanan's death on Lothal in 1 BBY during the Star Wars Rebels episode 'Jedi Night,' where he sacrifices himself to save the Ghost crew. Our entry asserts 0 BBY as known, which is incorrect.

notes.current: Coruscant is listed, which is correct, but note startYear -33 matches birth; no issue there
notes.detail: Homeworld Coruscant is correct per canon — no change needed; flagging only to confirm it checks out.

notes.suggested: Caleb Dume
notes.detail: His birth name Caleb Dume is a significant second identity (used throughout the Kanan comic and his Bad Batch / Clone Wars-era appearances) and would fit the altTitle field.

notes.suggested: Add Master: Depa Billaba and Apprentice: Ezra Bridger
notes.detail: His master (Depa Billaba) and Padawan (Ezra Bridger) are defining relationships worth tracking for a Jedi character.

notes.suggested: Could specify the episode 'Jedi Night' (Star Wars Rebels)
notes.detail: The series is correct; adding the specific episode would improve precision on how/when he died.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Maz_Kan.description: Maz Kanata is a diminutive, orange-skinned Force-sensitive alien of an undefined species and one of the galaxy's oldest known individuals, born roughly a thousand years before the war between the First Order and the Resistance. For much of her extraordinarily long life she ran a castle on the forest world of Takodana, earning a reputation as a "pirate queen" by offering safe harbor to smugglers, travelers, and rogues of all allegiances — provided they respected her house rules forbidding politics and war under her roof. A collector of antiques and relics, she came to possess the lightsaber built by Anakin Skywalker and later wielded by Luke Skywalker, which she offered to Rey in 34 ABY as the young scavenger's destiny began to unfold. After the First Order destroyed her castle, Maz allied herself with the Resistance, lending her wisdom and combat experience through the final campaign against the Sith Eternal, and she survived the war.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Maz_Kan.timeline:
973 BBY - Birth

Maz Kanata is born, a member of a species that remains undefined in canon. The Rise of Skywalker Visual Dictionary later gives her age as 1007, consistent with this birth year.

~973 BBY–34 ABY - Centuries as Takodana's Pirate Queen

Over roughly a millennium, Maz establishes and maintains her castle on Takodana, welcoming smugglers and travelers while enforcing strict prohibitions against politics and war, and amassing a vast collection of relics and antiques.

~19 BBY–34 ABY - Keeper of the Skywalker Lightsaber

At some point Maz comes into possession of the lightsaber Anakin Skywalker built during the Clone Wars and later passed to his son Luke. She keeps it stored in her castle's vaults, deflecting questions about how she acquired it.

34 ABY - Han Solo, Rey and Finn Visit Takodana

Maz receives Han Solo, Rey, Finn, and BB-8. Sensing Rey's potential, she offers her the Skywalker lightsaber, urging her to embrace the call of the Force.

34 ABY - Destruction of Maz's Castle

The First Order attacks Takodana in pursuit of BB-8 and the map to Luke Skywalker, destroying Maz's ancient castle. Maz survives and afterward aligns herself with the Resistance.

35 ABY - Aiding the Resistance Against the Final Order
