Maz supports the Resistance from its base on Ajan Kloss during the campaign against Emperor Palpatine's returned Sith forces, and is present among the survivors celebrating the victory at the Battle of Exegol.

birth.source: Wookieepedia and the Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker Visual Dictionary, which lists her age as 1007 (973 BBY + 35 ABY ≈ 1008).

notes.suggested: Unknown (undefined)
notes.detail: Correct as-is. Lucasfilm Story Group's Pablo Hidalgo confirmed in 2026 that Maz's species remains deliberately undefined in canon, so 'Unknown' is the right value; note only that any source claiming a defined species is in error.

Maz is firmly associated with Takodana, where her castle stood, and sources describe her as living there. However, because her species and true origins are undefined in canon, whether Takodana is literally her birth homeworld versus long-term residence is not definitively established. Leaving 'Takodana' is reasonable.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Max_Reb.description: Maximilian "Max" Rebo was a male Ortolan from the cold world of Orto and one of the galaxy's best-known jizz-wailer musicians, famous as the leader of the Max Rebo Band. Short, blue-skinned and long-snouted, he was instantly recognizable behind his red ball jett organ, an instrument he played with his feet rather than his hands. Rebo and his band made a career of playing for criminals and gangsters, most notoriously as the house act of the Hutt crime lord Jabba Desilijic Tiure, performing in Jabba's Tatooine palace and aboard his sail barge in 4 ABY — where the band's number "Jedi Rocks" accompanied the dancer Oola's death in the rancor pit and Jabba's capture of Leia Organa. Rebo survived the destruction of the sail barge over the Great Pit of Carkoon, and by 9 ABY he had found steadier and safer work on Tatooine as the resident musician at Garsa Fwip's cantina, the Sanctuary in Mos Espa, still playing a red ball jett organ when the newly installed Daimyo Boba Fett came calling. His fate after the Sanctuary's destruction is not established in canon.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Max_Reb.timeline:
Unknown date - Birth on Orto

Maximilian Rebo was born a member of the Ortolan species on the frigid planet Orto. Canon does not record the year of his birth, nor his age at any later point.

Unknown date - Rise as a jizz-wailer

Rebo became a popular musician in the jizz genre and formed the Max Rebo Band, taking the red ball jett organ as his signature instrument and playing its keys with his feet. The band built its reputation performing for criminals, gangsters and crime lords.

By 4 ABY - House band for Jabba the Hutt

Rebo's band took up residence in the Tatooine palace of the Hutt crime lord Jabba Desilijic Tiure, becoming the court's entertainment.

4 ABY - Performing in Jabba's palace

The Max Rebo Band played for Jabba's court as the Twi'lek dancer Oola was dropped into the rancor pit, and were still performing when Jabba sprang his trap on Leia Organa, who had infiltrated the palace disguised as the bounty hunter Boushh to free Han Solo from carbonite.

4 ABY - Aboard the sail barge and survival

Rebo and his band accompanied Jabba aboard the sail barge Khetanna on its journey to the Great Pit of Carkoon for the execution of Luke Skywalker, Han Solo and Chewbacca. The barge was destroyed in the ensuing fight, but Rebo survived.

9 ABY - Playing the Sanctuary in Mos Espa

Rebo was employed as the musician at the Sanctuary, the Mos Espa cantina run by the Twi'lek Garsa Fwip, where he performed on a newer model of red ball jett organ. He was playing there on more than one occasion when the former bounty hunter and new Daimyo of Mos Espa, Boba Fett, visited the establishment.

After 9 ABY - Fate unrecorded

No canon source records Rebo's death or his whereabouts after his appearances in Mos Espa; he is presumed alive.

birth.source: Wookieepedia canon article (now titled "Maximilian Rebo", redirected from "Max Rebo") gives no birth year, and no canon source states one or an age from which a year could be derived. Only his homeworld (Orto) is given.

death.source: No death in canon. He survives the destruction of Jabba's sail barge in 4 ABY and is alive and performing at Garsa Fwip's Sanctuary on Tatooine in 9 ABY (The Book of Boba Fett).

notes.suggested: Maximilian Rebo
notes.detail: Wookieepedia's canon article has been moved to "Maximilian Rebo" — that is his canonical full name, with "Max" as the professional/short form. The wookiepedia URL in the payload still works as a redirect, but consider updating it to https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Maximilian_Rebo.

notes.suggested: keep flagged as a guess
notes.detail: Correctly flagged. Canon gives no birth year, no age, and no stated Ortolan lifespan, so 20 BBY has no source behind it. Worth noting the guess is on the tight side: it would make him roughly 24 at Jabba's palace and 29 at the Sanctuary, which is not contradicted by anything but is not supported either. An earlier placeholder would sit more comfortably with his established status as an already-famous bandleader by 4 ABY.

notes.suggested: no change
notes.detail: Correct as-is — this checks out. Rebo has no canon death: he survives the sail barge's destruction in 4 ABY and is alive in 9 ABY. Flagging only to confirm the maintainer should not be tempted to fill in a death year.

notes.current: Max Rebo was a popular Jizz-wailer musician who led the Max Rebo Band. He mainly played the red ball jett organ, which he played with his feet; he and his band often performed for criminals and gangsters.
notes.suggested: Extend to cover the two things that make his timeline entry interesting: that he survived the destruction of Jabba's sail barge, and that by 9 ABY he was the house musician at Garsa Fwip's Sanctuary in Mos Espa.
notes.detail: The current text is lifted verbatim from the Wookieepedia lead and stops before any of his actual on-screen milestones, so the entry reads as a static character sketch with no events attached.

notes.current: Homeworld: Orto; Species: Ortolan; Force Sensitive: No
notes.suggested: Add an Affiliation/Occupation entry such as "Musician, Max Rebo Band"
notes.detail: Homeworld, species and Force sensitivity all verify against canon. The band leadership is his single defining fact and is currently only present in the prose description, not in the structured metadata.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Chewbac.description: Chewbacca, affectionately called Chewie, was a Wookiee warrior, smuggler, mechanic, pilot, and resistance fighter from the forest planet Kashyyyk. Renowned for his loyalty, strength, and skill as a co-pilot, he fought across generations of galactic conflict — the Clone Wars, the Galactic Civil War, and the war between the First Order and the Resistance. During the Clone Wars he was captured by Trandoshan slavers and imprisoned on Wasskah, escaping with the aid of fellow captive Jedi Commander Ahsoka Tano, and later helped defend his homeworld and cover Jedi Grand Master Yoda's escape during the Battle of Kashyyyk. He is best known as the lifelong companion and co-pilot of Han Solo aboard the Millennium Falcon, a partnership that began around a decade before the Battle of Yavin. Bound to Han by a Wookiee life debt, Chewbacca became a central figure of the Rebel Alliance, fighting at battles such as Yavin, Hoth, and Endor, and continued to serve the New Republic and later the Resistance, standing beside a new generation of heroes into the war against the First Order.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Chewbac.timeline:
200 BBY - Birth on Kashyyyk

Chewbacca is born on the forest world of Kashyyyk, homeworld of the Wookiee people.

~21 BBY - Captured by Trandoshan Slavers

During the Clone Wars, Chewbacca is captured by Trandoshan slavers and taken to the moon Wasskah to be hunted for sport.

~21 BBY - Escape from Wasskah with Ahsoka Tano

Working alongside fellow captive Jedi Commander Ahsoka Tano and a group of younglings, Chewbacca helps organize a resistance and escapes the Trandoshans' hunting grounds.

19 BBY - Battle of Kashyyyk

Chewbacca and Chieftain Tarfful lead Wookiee forces defending Kashyyyk alongside the Grand Army of the Republic under Jedi General Yoda. When Order 66 is issued, the two Wookiees help Yoda escape the planet and the destruction of the Jedi Order.

~10 BBY - Meeting Han Solo

Imprisoned by the Empire, Chewbacca meets Han Solo on Mimban, and the two escape together, beginning a lifelong friendship. He takes part in the Kessel heist aboard the Millennium Falcon.

0 BBY - The Battle of Yavin

Chewbacca and Han take on a charter from Obi-Wan Kenobi and Luke Skywalker, becoming entangled in the Rebellion and aiding in the rescue of Princess Leia and the destruction of the Death Star.

3 ABY - The Battle of Hoth and Bespin

Fleeing the Empire, Chewbacca flies the Falcon to Cloud City, where he fights Imperial forces and helps rescue the frozen, carbonite-encased Han Solo's companions after Han is captured by Boba Fett.

4 ABY - Rescue of Han Solo and the Battle of Endor

Chewbacca helps free Han from Jabba the Hutt and joins the strike team on the forest moon of Endor, contributing to the destruction of the second Death Star.

34 ABY - The Hunt for Luke and the Death of Han Solo

Reunited with the Millennium Falcon, Chewbacca aids the Resistance against the First Order and is present when Han is killed by his son, Ben Solo (Kylo Ren), on Starkiller Base.

34 ABY - The Battle of Crait

Chewbacca continues to fly the Falcon and support the dwindling Resistance during its escape and stand at Crait.

35 ABY - The Battle of Exegol

Chewbacca fights in the final campaign against the resurrected Emperor Palpatine and the Sith Eternal, surviving the war as one of the Resistance's most enduring heroes.

birth.source: Solo: A Star Wars Story establishes Chewbacca as 190 years old circa 10 BBY, placing his birth at 200 BBY; Wookieepedia and reference works (Ultimate Star Wars) use 200 BBY in canon.

death.source: Chewbacca survives all canon material and is alive at the end of The Rise of Skywalker (35 ABY); canon records no death.

notes.suggested: Wookiee
notes.detail: The species is spelled 'Wookiee' (double 'e') in Star Wars canon; 'Wookie' is a common misspelling.

notes.suggested: Keep 200 BBY but note it is derived, not explicitly stated in-universe
notes.detail: 200 BBY originates in Legends but is supported in canon: Solo: A Star Wars Story establishes Chewbacca as 190 years old around 10 BBY, and Wookieepedia/reference works use 200 BBY. It is well-founded, so presenting it as known is reasonable, though it is a derived age rather than a directly quoted birth date.

notes.current: Column ends at 50 ABY with endYearUnknown true
notes.suggested: Retain endYearUnknown
notes.detail: Correct — Chewbacca does not die in canon; he survives through The Rise of Skywalker (35 ABY) and is alive at the end of all released canon material. No death should be recorded.

notes.current: Lists films and series but omits some appearances
notes.suggested: Consider adding The Mandalorian-era/animated and reference appearances if tracked
notes.detail: Chewbacca also appears in Star Wars Rebels ('The Wynkahthu Job') and the Solo tie-ins; not essential, but worth noting the seenIn list is not exhaustive.

notes.suggested: Kashyyyk (correct)
notes.detail: Homeworld is accurate; no change needed — included only for confirmation, no action required.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Padm%C3.description: Padmé Amidala Naberrie was a human stateswoman from the planet Naboo who served her people during the final decades of the Galactic Republic. Elected Queen of Naboo as a teenager, she rose to prominence during the Trade Federation's invasion of her homeworld, forging an alliance with the Gungans to liberate Naboo. After her royal terms ended, she became Naboo's representative in the Galactic Senate, where she championed democracy, diplomacy, and a peaceful end to the Clone Wars, opposing the militarization of the Republic. Her secret marriage to Jedi Knight Anakin Skywalker would prove fateful for the galaxy: pregnant with his children, she died in 19 BBY shortly after giving birth to the twins Luke and Leia, whose destinies would shape the fate of the galaxy for generations. She is remembered as an idealist whose commitment to freedom outlived her.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Padm%C3.timeline:
46 BBY - Birth on Naboo

Padmé Naberrie is born on Naboo to Ruwee and Jobal Naberrie, growing up alongside her older sister Sola.

~40 BBY - Early public service

As a child and young teenager, Padmé becomes involved in politics and humanitarian work, serving in the Apprentice Legislators and as a junior member of Naboo's government.

~33 BBY - Elected Queen of Naboo

At age fourteen, Padmé is elected Queen of Naboo, taking the regnal name Amidala and beginning her reign.

32 BBY - Invasion of Naboo

The Trade Federation blockades and invades Naboo. Aided by the Jedi Qui-Gon Jinn and Obi-Wan Kenobi and meeting young Anakin Skywalker, Amidala escapes to Coruscant, calls for a vote of no confidence in Chancellor Valorum, then returns home to lead a Gungan-Naboo alliance that defeats the Federation.

~28 BBY - Senator of Naboo

After her terms as Queen conclude, Amidala is appointed to represent Naboo in the Galactic Senate, becoming a leading voice for democracy and peace.

22 BBY - Separatist Crisis and marriage

Targeted by assassination attempts over the Military Creation Act, Padmé is placed under Jedi protection and reunites with Anakin Skywalker. Following the Battle of Geonosis that opens the Clone Wars, she and Anakin secretly marry on Naboo.

22–19 BBY - The Clone Wars

Throughout the war, Senator Amidala undertakes numerous diplomatic missions, pursues peace initiatives, exposes corruption, and works to protect the Republic's democratic institutions while concealing her marriage to Anakin.

19 BBY - Pregnancy and Anakin's fall

Padmé becomes pregnant with Anakin's children. As the Clone Wars end and Order 66 is executed, Anakin turns to the dark side as Darth Vader. Padmé travels to Mustafar to reach him, only to be Force-choked by Anakin in his rage.

19 BBY - Death on Polis Massa

Taken to the medical facility on Polis Massa, Padmé gives birth to twins Luke and Leia before dying, having lost the will to live. Her death is concealed and she is later buried on Naboo, appearing pregnant to hide the twins' survival.

birth.source: Wookieepedia (Padmé Amidala Naberrie) and multiple canon references; she died in 19 BBY at age 27, consistent with a 46 BBY birth.

death.source: Wookieepedia; died on Polis Massa in 19 BBY during the events of Revenge of the Sith, shortly after giving birth to Luke and Leia.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Hondo_O.description: Hondo Ohnaka is a Weequay pirate, smuggler and self-styled businessman whose decades-long career made him one of the galaxy's most persistently opportunistic survivors. Operating out of the Outer Rim — most famously from a fortified base on Florrum at the head of the Ohnaka Gang — he first drew galactic attention during the Clone Wars by capturing and ransoming Count Dooku, and thereafter repeatedly crossed paths with Obi-Wan Kenobi, Anakin Skywalker and Ahsoka Tano, alternately robbing them, arming their enemies and, when the price or the mood was right, helping them. Weapons dealing to the Onderon rebels, the abduction of a group of Jedi younglings, and the destruction of his operation by Maul's Shadow Collective all followed in quick succession. Reduced to scavenging under the Empire, he resurfaced years later as a fence and freelancer who befriended the Padawan Ezra Bridger, sold the Ghost crew both useful intelligence and useless junk, and drifted into loose cooperation with the rebellion. Decades on he was still trading — running Ohnaka Transport Solutions out of Black Spire Outpost on Batuu and quietly borrowing the Millennium Falcon for jobs — a scoundrel whose loyalty was always for sale but whose affection for a handful of Jedi was, by his own insistence, entirely genuine.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Hondo_O.timeline:
Unknown date - Birth

Canon has never given Hondo Ohnaka a birth year. He is a Weequay, a species associated with the world Sriluur, and by the Clone Wars he is an established pirate captain with a long criminal history behind him.

~32 BBY - Dealings with Darth Maul

Around the time of the Naboo crisis, a younger Ohnaka is already operating as a pirate and trader in the criminal underworld, crossing paths with Darth Maul during the Sith apprentice's hunt for a captured Jedi Padawan.

~21 BBY - Capturing Count Dooku

Ohnaka's gang seizes Count Dooku and attempts to ransom the Separatist leader to the Republic, ending up with Obi-Wan Kenobi and Anakin Skywalker as unwilling guests — and then prisoners — at his Florrum stronghold.

~21 BBY - Extortion on Felucia

His pirates shake down Felucian farmers for their harvest, only to be driven off after the villagers hire a band of bounty hunters trained up with Jedi help.

~20 BBY - Arming the Onderon rebels

Ohnaka sells weapons to the Onderonian insurgency fighting the Separatist puppet government, profiting from a rebellion the Jedi were quietly backing.

~20 BBY - The kidnapped younglings

His crew boards the Jedi training cruiser Crucible and takes Ahsoka Tano and a group of younglings captive on Florrum; the younglings escape, steal his ship and force him into a grudging alliance against General Grievous.

~20 BBY - Florrum destroyed

Maul's Shadow Collective, backed by Death Watch, assaults Florrum and shatters the Ohnaka Gang, leaving Hondo with a handful of survivors and no base of operations.

~3 BBY - Meeting Ezra Bridger

Now a lone operator flying the Broken Horn, Ohnaka encounters the young Padawan Ezra Bridger, spins him a story of partnership, and begins a long, mutually exasperating relationship with the crew of the Ghost.

~2 BBY - The last battle on Agamar

Ohnaka drags Ezra, Zeb and Chopper into a scheme to loot a Separatist supply ship, waking a droid army still fighting the Clone Wars decades after it ended.

~2 BBY - Selling junk to the rebellion

He continues fencing salvage, stolen goods and dubious intelligence to the growing rebel network, including a shipment of purportedly valuable cargo that proves worthless.

~1 BBY - Working with Saw Gerrera

A deal with the rebels goes sideways and Ohnaka finds himself alongside Saw Gerrera's partisans investigating an Imperial construction project, before slipping away to save his own skin.

~34 ABY - Ohnaka Transport Solutions on Batuu

Decades later and still very much alive, Ohnaka runs a shipping-and-smuggling outfit from Black Spire Outpost on Batuu, hiring freelance crews and making use of the Millennium Falcon while the First Order and Resistance struggle around him.

notes.suggested: keep the guess and the unknown flag
notes.detail: Canon gives Hondo Ohnaka no birth year at all — no film, series, novel or reference work states one, and Wookieepedia's canon article carries no birth date in its infobox. 52 BBY is a reasonable positioning guess (he is a seasoned pirate captain by 21 BBY), but nothing supports it, so the 'this is a guess' flag must stay. The 39 BEF figure that circulates online comes from a fan wiki, not from canon.

notes.suggested: Sriluur (species homeworld; Hondo's own birthplace unconfirmed in canon)
notes.detail: Sriluur is canonically the Weequay homeworld, but canon does not actually state that Hondo was born there — that specificity is inherited from Legends material. Worth a caveat if the field is presented as fact.

notes.suggested: consider clamping the column to ~34-35 ABY
notes.detail: endYearUnknown is correct — Hondo has no canon death and is alive in the sequel era. But his last confirmed canon presence is Batuu circa 34-35 ABY (Galaxy's Edge / Millennium Falcon: Smugglers Run); running the column to 50 ABY asserts more longevity than canon shows.

notes.current: The Clone Wars (TV series), Star Wars Rebels, Galaxy's Edge
notes.suggested: add the canon Darth Maul comic appearance (~32 BBY) if non-screen media are ever tracked
notes.detail: His earliest chronological canon appearance is Marvel's Darth Maul comic, set around 32 BBY — which is what makes a pre-Clone Wars column start defensible at all. If the timeline only plots film/TV/park content, this is fine as-is, but it is the source for the earliest point on his life.

notes.current: "...provided crucial aid and assistance during pivotal moments in the Clone Wars, Galactic Civil War, and First Order-Resistance War."
notes.suggested: soften the First Order-Resistance War claim
notes.detail: His sequel-era canon material (Batuu / Smugglers Run) shows him running a transport-and-smuggling business and lending out the Falcon, not aiding at 'pivotal moments' of the war. The Clone Wars and Galactic Civil War halves of the sentence hold up; the third does not.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Morgan_.description: Morgan Elsbeth was a Force-sensitive human female Nightsister of Dathomir who rose from the ashes of her clan's destruction to become one of the Galactic Empire's most important shipwrights and, later, the driving force behind Grand Admiral Thrawn's return from exile. Born on Dathomir before the Clone Wars, she survived the massacre of the Nightsisters — in which her mother was killed — and spent years as a wanderer and survivor, allying briefly with the Mountain Clan before her ruthlessness and talent for industry brought her into the Empire's service, where she helped construct the Imperial Navy and became a devoted follower of Thrawn. By the New Republic era she ruled the walled city of Calodan on Corvus as its magistrate, terrorizing its people with a regime that wore the chimaera emblem of Thrawn's 7th Fleet; there she offered a spear of pure beskar to the Mandalorian Din Djarin as payment to kill the former Jedi Ahsoka Tano, only to be defeated by Tano herself. Freed from New Republic custody by the mercenaries Baylan Skoll and Shin Hati, Elsbeth pursued a map to a distant galaxy, built the hyperspace ring Eye of Sion, and reached the world of Peridea, where the Great Mothers formally accepted her into the Nightsister order and granted her the gift of shadows and the Blade of Talzin. Fighting to buy Thrawn time to escape back to the known galaxy, she was struck down by Ahsoka Tano, dying with the hope of restoring Dathomir's culture unfulfilled.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Morgan_.timeline:
Before the Clone Wars - Birth on Dathomir

Morgan Elsbeth was born on Dathomir into the Nightsister clan sometime before the Clone Wars. Canon gives no year for her birth.

~19 BBY - Survivor of the Nightsister Massacre

Elsbeth witnessed the destruction of her people during the Separatist assault on Dathomir, in which her mother was killed. She survived alone amid the ruins of her clan.

~19 BBY - Wandering and the Mountain Clan

Left without a home, Elsbeth wandered Dathomir and eventually fell in with the Mountain Clan, who took her in before she turned on those who wronged her.

Reign of the Empire - Imperial Shipwright

Elsbeth's skill as a starship designer and industrialist made her instrumental in the construction of the Imperial Navy, and she became a dedicated follower of Grand Admiral Thrawn, whose chimaera emblem her forces later wore.

9 ABY - Magistrate of Calodan

By the New Republic era, Elsbeth ruled the walled city of Calodan on the planet Corvus, stripping the world bare and terrorizing its citizens with her guards and the mercenary Lang.

9 ABY - The Beskar Spear

She hired the Mandalorian Din Djarin, offering a spear of pure beskar as payment to kill the former Jedi Ahsoka Tano, who had come to Corvus hunting information about Thrawn.

9 ABY - Defeat on Corvus

Ahsoka Tano liberated Calodan and defeated Elsbeth in single combat, demanding to know the location of Grand Admiral Thrawn before taking her into custody.

9 ABY - Rescued by Baylan Skoll and Shin Hati

While being transported by the New Republic, Elsbeth was freed by the mercenaries Baylan Skoll and Shin Hati, whom she had employed, and resumed her search for Thrawn.

9 ABY - The Star Map and the Eye of Sion

Elsbeth recovered an ancient star map pointing to a distant galaxy and oversaw construction of the enormous hyperspace ring known as the Eye of Sion above Seatos.

9 ABY - Journey to Peridea

After a confrontation with Ahsoka Tano at Seatos, Elsbeth launched the Eye of Sion and crossed to Peridea, the ancestral world of the Dathomiri witches, where she found Thrawn in exile.

9 ABY - Accepted as a Nightsister

The Great Mothers formally inducted Elsbeth into the Nightsister order, granting her the gift of shadows and the Blade of Talzin.

9 ABY - Death on Peridea

During the Battle of Peridea, Elsbeth stayed behind at the Great Mothers' fortress to hold off Ahsoka Tano and allow Thrawn to escape. Declaring her sacrifice was for Dathomir rather than the Empire, she was killed by Tano.

birth.source: Wookieepedia's Morgan Elsbeth article gives no birth year — only "born on Dathomir prior to the Clone Wars"; the StarWars.com databank entry likewise gives no date. No canon source (film, series, or reference book) pins a year.

death.source: Wookieepedia (Morgan Elsbeth; Battle of Peridea) — killed by Ahsoka Tano on Peridea during the Ahsoka series, which canon dates to 9 ABY.

notes.suggested: Yes
notes.detail: Wookieepedia describes her as "a Force-sensitive human female Nightsister." She is a Nightsister who is granted the gift of shadows and wields the Blade of Talzin on Peridea, and Tales of the Empire shows her connection to Dathomiri magick. This is the clearest factual error in the entry.

notes.suggested: Ahsoka, The Mandalorian, Tales of the Empire
notes.detail: Elsbeth is the protagonist of the first three episodes of Star Wars: Tales of the Empire (2024), which covers her survival of the Nightsister massacre, her time with the Mountain Clan, and her rise under Thrawn. StarWars.com's databank lists it among her appearances.

Correctly flagged. No canon source gives Elsbeth a birth year — Wookieepedia says only "born on Dathomir prior to the Clone Wars." 48 BBY would make her about 29 at the Nightsister massacre and 57 at her death, which is broadly consistent with her depiction in Tales of the Empire, but it remains unsupported. Keep startYearUnknown true.

Wookieepedia's infobox classes her as Human, but the StarWars.com databank calls her a Dathomirian "born as a Nightsister on Dathomir." Human is the safer label (Nightsisters of Talzin's clan were human), but the two official sources are not phrased identically — worth knowing if a reader queries it.

notes.suggested: Magistrate Elsbeth
notes.detail: She is referred to in dialogue and in the StarWars.com databank title as "Magistrate Morgan Elsbeth," which is the name most viewers know her by from The Mandalorian.

notes.current: "Morgan Elsbeth was a Nightsister who was instrumental in the construction of the Imperial Navy... offered it as payment to the Mandalorian bounty hunter Din Djarin for him to kill the former Jedi Ahsoka Tano."
notes.detail: The existing text predates the Ahsoka series and Tales of the Empire — it stops at Corvus and omits her escape from New Republic custody, the Eye of Sion, the voyage to Peridea, her formal induction as a Nightsister with the Blade of Talzin, and her death at Ahsoka's hands. It also omits her Force sensitivity. Replace with the updated summary.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Kit_Fis.description: Kit Fisto was a Force-sensitive Nautolan male from the ocean world of Glee Anselm who rose to the rank of Jedi Master and served on the Jedi High Council during the final years of the Galactic Republic. Amphibious, famously good-humored, and regarded as one of the Order's finest lightsaber duelists — equally deadly on land and underwater — he fought in the Battle of Geonosis in 22 BBY that opened the Clone Wars and went on to serve as a Jedi General in the Grand Army of the Republic. Over the course of the war he hunted General Grievous to the Vassek system, where he lost his former Padawan Nahdar Vebb to the cyborg general and only narrowly escaped himself; he later led Republic forces in the underwater campaign on Mon Cala to protect Prince Lee-Char and restore the planet's throne. In 19 BBY, after Anakin Skywalker exposed Supreme Chancellor Sheev Palpatine as the Sith Lord Darth Sidious, Fisto joined Mace Windu, Agen Kolar and Saesee Tiin in the attempt to arrest him; Sidious cut down all three of Windu's companions within seconds, and Fisto died in the Chancellor's office on Coruscant, one of the last Jedi to fall before Order 66 destroyed the Order.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Kit_Fis.timeline:
Unknown date - Birth on Glee Anselm

Kit Fisto was born a Nautolan on the ocean world of Glee Anselm. No canon source gives a birth year; he was clearly born decades before the Clone Wars, as he was a seasoned Jedi Master by 22 BBY.

Before 22 BBY - Jedi Master and mentor

Fisto trained in the Jedi Order and attained the rank of Jedi Master, earning a reputation as an exceptional swordsman. He took the Mon Calamari Nahdar Vebb as his Padawan learner.

22 BBY - Battle of Geonosis

Fisto was among the roughly two hundred Jedi who followed Mace Windu to Geonosis to rescue Anakin Skywalker, Obi-Wan Kenobi and Padmé Amidala. He survived the arena battle that began the Clone Wars and became a Jedi General of the Grand Army of the Republic.

22 BBY - Hunt for Nute Gunray and the lair of Grievous

Pursuing the escaped Viceroy Nute Gunray, Fisto tracked a Separatist shuttle to a fortress on the third moon of Vassek and reunited with Nahdar Vebb, now a Jedi Knight. The pair instead found General Grievous's private lair. Fisto fought Grievous nearly to a standstill, but Vebb was killed and Fisto was forced to flee with clone commander Fil's squad decimated behind him.

22-19 BBY - Service on the Jedi High Council

Fisto sat on the Jedi High Council during the war, weighing in on the Order's strategy, on the growing entanglement of the Jedi with the Republic military, and on matters such as Ahsoka Tano's trial and Anakin Skywalker's standing.

~21 BBY - The war for Mon Cala

When the Separatists and the Quarren Isolation League overthrew the Mon Calamari monarchy, Fisto led Republic clone forces in the underwater campaign, using his amphibious nature to fight alongside Anakin Skywalker, Ahsoka Tano and Padmé Amidala to protect the young Prince Lee-Char until Gungan reinforcements turned the tide.

19 BBY - The attempt to arrest Palpatine

After Anakin Skywalker revealed that Supreme Chancellor Palpatine was the Sith Lord Darth Sidious, Fisto joined Mace Windu, Agen Kolar and Saesee Tiin in confronting the Chancellor in his office on Coruscant to place him under arrest.

19 BBY - Death on Coruscant

Sidious drew his lightsaber and killed Kolar and Tiin almost instantly. Fisto held out only seconds longer before he too was struck down, leaving Windu alone against the Sith Lord moments before Order 66 was issued.

birth.source: No canon source states Kit Fisto's birth year. The Wookieepedia article gives no birth date in its infobox (its date field covers only his death in 19 BBY), and no canon reference work (Star Wars Encyclopedia-type guides, StarWars.com Databank) assigns him one. The 55 BBY figure sometimes cited comes from Legends-era material and is not canon.

death.source: Wookieepedia "Kit Fisto" / StarWars.com Databank: killed by Darth Sidious in the Chancellor's office on Coruscant in 19 BBY, during Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith.

notes.suggested: Keep flagged as a guess, but consider moving it earlier (roughly 55-50 BBY)
notes.detail: Canon states no birth year for Fisto, so keeping the guess flag is correct. However, 45 BBY makes him only ~23 at Geonosis and ~26 at his death, which is hard to square with his being a Jedi Master who had already trained Nahdar Vebb to near-Knighthood. Legends material (non-canon) placed his birth around 55 BBY, which fits the on-screen character far better. If the column position is meant to be plausible, an earlier guess is better.

notes.suggested: Verify or remove
notes.detail: Fisto's Clone Wars presence is in the TV series (notably "Lair of Grievous" and the season 4 Mon Cala arc). I could not confirm an appearance in the 2008 theatrical The Clone Wars film; the Jedi Council scenes there do not clearly include him. Worth double-checking before presenting it as an appearance.

notes.suggested: Consider checking Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi for Jedi Council appearances
notes.detail: Tales of the Jedi features multiple Jedi High Council chamber scenes in the late Republic era; if Fisto is animated among the Council members there, it belongs in seenIn. Flagging rather than asserting — I could not verify it from a primary source.

notes.suggested: Add Rank: Jedi Master / Jedi General, and Jedi High Council member
notes.detail: His Council seat and General rank are among the most defining facts about him and are already implied in the description but not tracked as structured metadata.

notes.current: Current description does not mention his death or his apprentice
notes.suggested: Mention Nahdar Vebb and his death at the hands of Darth Sidious
notes.detail: The existing text stops at his Council membership. His two most notable narrative beats — losing his Padawan Nahdar Vebb to General Grievous, and dying in the attempt to arrest Palpatine — are missing.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Han_Sol.description: Han Solo was a human smuggler from the Core World of Corellia who rose to become a general and one of the most vital leaders of the Alliance to Restore the Republic during the Galactic Civil War. Orphaned into the criminal underworld of Corellia, he escaped his homeworld, briefly served as an Imperial cadet, and forged a lifelong friendship with the Wookiee Chewbacca, who swore him a life debt. Alongside his prized ship the Millennium Falcon — won from Lando Calrissian — he became famed for completing the Kessel Run in under twelve parsecs while working for gangsters such as Jabba the Hutt. Drawn into the Rebellion after agreeing to transport Luke Skywalker and Obi-Wan Kenobi to Alderaan, he helped rescue Princess Leia Organa, aided in the destruction of the first Death Star at the Battle of Yavin, and fought through Hoth, Bespin, and the Battle of Endor. He later married Leia and fathered Ben Solo, but their son's fall to the dark side as Kylo Ren shattered the family. In 34 ABY, Han was killed by his own son aboard Starkiller Base while trying to bring him home.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Han_Sol.timeline:
32 BBY - Birth on Corellia

Han is born in a rough district of the Core World Corellia during the final years of the Galactic Republic, growing up as a scrumrat in the planet's criminal underworld.

~10 BBY - Escape, the Empire, and the Kessel Run

Han flees Corellia, briefly serves as an Imperial cadet where a recruitment officer gives him the surname "Solo," meets Chewbacca, wins the Millennium Falcon from Lando Calrissian, and completes the legendary Kessel Run in under twelve parsecs.

0 BBY - Chartering passage on Tatooine

A smuggler indebted to Jabba the Hutt, Han agrees to fly Luke Skywalker, Obi-Wan Kenobi, and the droids to Alderaan aboard the Falcon from Mos Eisley.

0 BBY - Rescue of Leia and the Battle of Yavin

Drawn aboard the Death Star, Han helps rescue Princess Leia, then returns to cover Luke's trench run, enabling the destruction of the battle station at the Battle of Yavin.

3 ABY - Hoth and Bespin

Now committed to the Rebellion, Han fights at the Battle of Hoth, flees through an asteroid field, and is betrayed at Cloud City, where the Empire freezes him in carbonite and hands him to the bounty hunter Boba Fett.

4 ABY - Rescue and the Battle of Endor

Rescued from Jabba's palace by his friends, Han is promoted to general and leads the strike team that disables the second Death Star's shield generator on Endor, helping secure the Rebellion's decisive victory.

~5 ABY - Marriage and family

In the years after Endor, Han marries Leia Organa and they have a son, Ben Solo. Han's later estrangement from Leia follows Ben's fall to the dark side.

34 ABY - Death on Starkiller Base

Reunited with the Resistance, Han confronts his son — now Kylo Ren — on Starkiller Base in an attempt to bring him home. Ren kills his father, and Han falls to his death.

birth.source: Wookieepedia 'Han Solo' article states he was born in 32 BBY on Corellia; corroborated by multiple canon references.

death.source: Killed by Kylo Ren on Starkiller Base in 34 ABY during Episode VII: The Force Awakens.

notes.suggested: Episode VII: The Force Awakens (killed by his son Kylo Ren on Starkiller Base)
notes.detail: Correct as-is; optional added specificity on cause/place of death for display.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Bib_For.description: Bib Fortuna was a male Twi'lek from Ryloth who rose from the criminal underworld to become the majordomo and chief of staff of the Hutt crime lord Jabba Desilijic Tiure. Born into a prominent Twi'lek clan with deep ties to organized crime — much to the disgust of his politically minded cousin Beezer Fortuna — he worked as a slave trader preying on his own species before entering Jabba's service. For decades he ran the day-to-day operations of Jabba's Palace on Tatooine, screening visitors, managing the court and its entertainments, and handling his master's business, all while privately loathing the Hutt and coveting his throne. He was present in Jabba's box at the Boonta Eve Classic podrace on Tatooine in 32 BBY, remained at the Hutt's side through the Clone Wars and the rise of the Empire, and in 4 ABY admitted Luke Skywalker into the palace during the Rebel effort to rescue Han Solo — an intrusion that ended with Jabba's death aboard his sail barge over the Great Pit of Carkoon. In the power vacuum that followed, Fortuna at last claimed the palace and Jabba's territory for himself, ruling as a minor crime lord until 9 ABY, when the bounty hunter Boba Fett, returned from the Sarlacc, shot him dead and took the throne.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Bib_For.timeline:
Date Unknown - Birth on Ryloth

Bib Fortuna was born a male Twi'lek on Ryloth, into a prominent clan with close ties to the criminal underworld. Canon gives no birth year; he was clearly an adult by 32 BBY.

Date Unknown - Slave trader in the underworld

Before entering Hutt service, Fortuna worked as a slave trader who trafficked in members of his own species, a trade his cousin Beezer Fortuna resented the family for.

Date Unknown - Becomes Jabba's majordomo

Fortuna took service with Jabba Desilijic Tiure on Tatooine, eventually becoming his majordomo and chief of staff and running Jabba's Palace day to day.

32 BBY - The Boonta Eve Classic

Fortuna attended the Boonta Eve Classic podrace in Jabba's private box at the Mos Espa Grand Arena, the race won by the young slave Anakin Skywalker.

~19 BBY - Serving Jabba under the new Empire

In the aftermath of the Clone Wars and the founding of the Galactic Empire, Fortuna continued to receive visitors and conduct business on Jabba's behalf at the palace, including dealings with mercenaries and clone deserters passing through Tatooine.

4 ABY - Admitting Luke Skywalker

When Luke Skywalker came to the palace to free Han Solo, Fortuna — pressed by a Jedi mind trick — brought him before Jabba, against the Hutt's wishes.

4 ABY - The death of Jabba the Hutt

Jabba was killed aboard his sail barge at the Great Pit of Carkoon during the botched execution of Skywalker, Solo and their companions. Fortuna survived the collapse of Jabba's criminal empire.

~4 ABY to 9 ABY - Crime lord of Jabba's Palace

With his master dead, Fortuna finally seized the throne he had long coveted, ruling the palace and a diminished remnant of Jabba's Tatooine holdings.

9 ABY - Killed by Boba Fett

Boba Fett, having escaped the Sarlacc and recovered his armor, entered the palace with Fennec Shand, shot Fortuna dead on his dais, and claimed the throne for himself.

birth.source: Wookieepedia's Bib Fortuna article gives no birth date in its infobox or biography; no canon source (film, series, or reference book) states a birth year or age for him. The 57 BBY figure our entry uses is a positioning guess, not canon.

death.source: Wookieepedia, "Bib Fortuna": "In 9 ABY, Fortuna was killed by bounty hunter Boba Fett, who took over his throne." Depicted in the post-credits scene of The Mandalorian "Chapter 16: The Rescue" (that scene is itself titled "The Book of Boba Fett") and recapped in The Book of Boba Fett "Chapter 1: Stranger in a Strange Land."

notes.suggested: The Mandalorian
notes.detail: Fortuna's on-screen death is the post-credits scene of The Mandalorian "Chapter 16: The Rescue" (2020). That scene is captioned "The Book of Boba Fett," so the current value is defensible, but the death occurs in the series our timeline lists as The Mandalorian; The Book of Boba Fett only replays it as a flashback recap in "Chapter 1: Stranger in a Strange Land." If the field drives an app link, it will send users to the wrong show.

Confirmed: canon states no birth year or age for Bib Fortuna anywhere — not in the films, series, or reference works. Keeping startYearUnknown: true is correct. The 57 BBY figure is a reasonable placeholder (he is an adult by 32 BBY and serves Jabba for decades), but it should never be presented as canon.

notes.suggested: Add Occupation: Majordomo / crime lord, and Affiliation: Jabba's criminal empire
notes.detail: His defining trait is his role rather than his species; other underworld entries would benefit from the same fields.

notes.current: Does not mention his pre-Jabba career or his period as crime lord in his own right
notes.suggested: Note that he was a Twi'lek slave trader before entering Jabba's service, and that he ruled the palace himself for roughly five years
notes.detail: Wookieepedia records that Fortuna trafficked in members of his own species before working for Jabba, and that he secretly hated the Hutt and wanted his throne — which is what makes the 4 ABY to 9 ABY gap in our column meaningful rather than dead time.

notes.suggested: Track cousin Beezer Fortuna as a relation
notes.detail: Beezer Fortuna, a politically minded Twi'lek who resented the family's underworld ties, is canon and appears in The Clone Wars-era material; a relations link would be useful if the timeline supports one.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Mace_Wi.description: Mace Windu was a revered human Jedi Master and one of the most powerful members of the Jedi High Council during the final decades of the Galactic Republic. Born on Haruun Kal, he rose to become the Order's foremost warrior, a master of the Force and creator of the rare lightsaber combat form Vaapad, wielding a distinctive amethyst-bladed lightsaber. He served for a time as Master of the Order and, during the Clone Wars, as a senior Jedi General in the Grand Army of the Republic. A staunch defender of Jedi tradition and deeply wary of Anakin Skywalker and the growing shadow over the Republic, Windu ultimately uncovered Supreme Chancellor Palpatine as the Sith Lord Darth Sidious. He nearly defeated the Sith in single combat, but was betrayed by Anakin and killed, a turning point that sealed the fall of the Jedi Order and the rise of the Galactic Empire.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Mace_Wi.timeline:
72 BBY - Birth on Haruun Kal

Mace Windu is born on the humid jungle world of Haruun Kal, home to the Korunnai people, and is identified as strong in the Force.

~70 BBY - Taken into the Jedi Order

Discovered to be Force-sensitive, the young Windu is brought to the Jedi Temple on Coruscant to be trained as a Jedi.

~40 BBY - Rise to Jedi Master and the High Council

Windu becomes one of the most accomplished Jedi of his generation, earning a seat on the Jedi High Council and developing the aggressive lightsaber form known as Vaapad.

32 BBY - The Phantom Menace

As a senior Council member, Windu helps question the captured Sith threat and is present when Qui-Gon Jinn presents Anakin Skywalker; he is skeptical of training the boy but consents after Qui-Gon's death.

22 BBY - Attack of the Clones and the Battle of Geonosis

Windu leads a Jedi strike team to rescue Obi-Wan Kenobi, Anakin, and Padmé on Geonosis, personally slaying the bounty hunter Jango Fett as the Clone Wars erupt.

22–19 BBY - Jedi General in the Clone Wars

Windu serves as a general in the Grand Army of the Republic, leading campaigns across the galaxy and serving at times as Master of the Order while balancing his duties on the Council.

19 BBY - Revenge of the Sith: exposing Darth Sidious

After Anakin reveals that Chancellor Palpatine is the Sith Lord Darth Sidious, Windu leads a team of Jedi Masters to arrest him and defeats him in a duel in the Chancellor's office.

19 BBY - Death on Coruscant

As Windu prepares to end the Sith threat, Anakin intervenes and severs his hand; Sidious then unleashes Force lightning and hurls Windu from the window to his death, a betrayal that marks Anakin's turn to the dark side.

birth.source: Wookieepedia gives 72 BBY, derived from the Star Wars Encyclopedia booklet 'Mace Windu and Other Users of the Force,' which states he was born 40 years before the Battle of Naboo (32 BBY per Star Wars: Timelines).

death.source: Killed during Revenge of the Sith (19 BBY), confronting Darth Sidious; Anakin Skywalker severs his hand and Sidious hurls him from a window to his death.

notes.suggested: 72 BBY (confirmed)
notes.detail: Automatic check: birth year in data.json disagrees with the year Claude verified. Wookieepedia gives 72 BBY, derived from the Star Wars Encyclopedia booklet 'Mace Windu and Other Users of the Force,' which states he was born 40 years before the Battle of Naboo (32 BBY per Star Wars: Timelines).

Current canon establishes Mace Windu's birth as 72 BBY, per the Star Wars Encyclopedia booklet 'Mace Windu and Other Users of the Force,' which states he was born 40 years before the Battle of Naboo (32 BBY per Star Wars: Timelines). We assert 80 BBY as known, which is incorrect. Note the year is derived from a reference-book age, so it could be flagged with a slight ambiguity, but 72 BBY is the accepted canon figure — 80 BBY has no canon basis.
Once startYear is corrected to the true birth year, no separate birthYear field is needed, but the underlying data should reflect 72 BBY.

notes.suggested: Human (Korun)
notes.detail: Canon and Legends identify Windu as a Korun human — a human ethnic group native to Haruun Kal. Optional refinement; 'Human' is not wrong.

19 BBY death is correct and canon. Worth knowing: because his body was never recovered on-screen and creators (notably Samuel L. Jackson and Dave Filoni) have publicly speculated he could have survived the fall, some fans treat his death as unconfirmed. Canon currently lists him as deceased in 19 BBY, so our entry is accurate as-is.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Landoni.description: Landonis Balthazar "Lando" Calrissian is a human smuggler, gambler, entrepreneur, and sabacc player who rose to become Baron Administrator of Cloud City on Bespin and, later, a general in the Rebel Alliance. Born on Socorro, he was the original owner of the Millennium Falcon before losing the freighter to Han Solo in a game of sabacc. Charming and self-interested but ultimately loyal, he is best known for his fraught reunion with Han at Cloud City—where he was coerced by Darth Vader into betraying his friends before turning against the Empire—and for piloting the Falcon during the Battle of Endor that destroyed the second Death Star. Decades later, having lived as a recluse after the First Order abducted his infant daughter, he answered the call to rally reinforcements for the Battle of Exegol, helping secure the Resistance's final victory over the Sith Eternal.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Landoni.timeline:
~43 BBY - Birth on Socorro

Lando Calrissian is born on the desert world of Socorro. Canon gives this date only as an approximation, derived from his stated age of 78 during the war against the First Order.

~10 BBY - Losing the Millennium Falcon

An established smuggler and gambler, Lando owns the Millennium Falcon and joins Han Solo, Chewbacca, and Qi'ra on the Kessel heist. He loses the Falcon to Han in a game of sabacc on Numidian Prime and parts ways to resume his own ventures.

~5 BBY - Schemes on Lothal

Having eased back from full-time smuggling, Lando runs assorted schemes, including a small operation tied to Lothal, and crosses paths with the Ghost crew and Ezra Bridger during the early rebellion.

3 ABY - Betrayal and reversal at Cloud City

As Baron Administrator of Cloud City on Bespin, Lando is forced by Darth Vader and the Empire to lure Han Solo and his companions into a trap. Regretting the betrayal, he turns against the Empire, helps rescue Leia and the others, and joins the hunt to recover the carbonite-frozen Han.

4 ABY - General at the Battle of Endor

Commissioned a general in the Rebel Alliance, Lando pilots the Millennium Falcon into the second Death Star and destroys its reactor core during the Battle of Endor, helping bring down the Galactic Empire.

35 ABY - Rallying the fleet at Exegol

Living as a recluse after the First Order stole his daughter years earlier, Lando aids the Resistance and, alongside Chewbacca, leads a massive citizens' fleet to the Battle of Exegol, contributing to the defeat of the Sith Eternal. Afterward he sets out to help Jannah find her home and family.

birth.source: Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary lists his birth as approximately 43 BBY; consistent with his stated age of 78 in TROS (35 ABY), i.e. c. 43 BBY. Wookieepedia dates it 'c. 43 BBY'.

death.source: Lando survives all canon appearances through The Rise of Skywalker (35 ABY); no death is recorded in canon.

notes.current: Birth of 43 BBY presented as a KNOWN/confirmed date (startYearUnknown absent)
notes.suggested: Set startYearUnknown: true (or mark as estimated)
notes.detail: Canon only gives Lando's birth as approximately 43 BBY (The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary; Wookieepedia lists 'c. 43 BBY'). It is derived from his stated age of 78 in TROS, not a firmly stated year, so the app should not present it as fact. Note also the Story Group's Matt Martin has stated Lando is older than Han (born 32 BBY), which 43 BBY satisfies.

notes.suggested: Consider adding The Book of Boba Fett? (No) — but note his voice/animated appearances
notes.detail: The listed appearances are all valid. Optional additions the timeline could track: Lando also appears in Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker prominently and in various canon comics/novels; the current list is a reasonable subset of on-screen media.

notes.suggested: Add note that Lando has a daughter abducted by the First Order
notes.detail: A canon detail worth tracking: The Rise of Skywalker era material establishes Lando's daughter was taken by the First Order as a child, which explains his reclusive years — relevant context for his 35 ABY events and speculation linking him to Jannah.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Saw_Ger.description: Saw Gerrera was a human male freedom fighter from Onderon whose four-decade war against tyranny made him both a founding figure of the rebellion and its most feared extremist. During the Clone Wars he and his younger sister Steela led the Onderon rebels against the Separatist-installed king Sanjay Rash, trained in insurgency by Jedi sent secretly by the Republic — Anakin Skywalker, Obi-Wan Kenobi, Ahsoka Tano and Captain Rex — and their victory restored King Ramsis Dendup to the throne, though Steela was killed in the fighting. When the Republic became the Galactic Empire, Saw simply kept fighting, building the Partisans into a militia whose brutal, uncompromising tactics alienated even sympathetic rebels such as Mon Mothma and Luthen Rael and eventually saw him expelled from the nascent Alliance. He rescued and raised Galen Erso's daughter Jyn after her mother's death, only to abandon her as a teenager, and he investigated Imperial atrocities from the emptied hives of Geonosis to the kyber mines of Jedha. Broken in body and dependent on breathing apparatus, Saw ran his final insurgency from the Catacombs of Cadera on Jedha, where he confirmed the truth of the Death Star through Galen's holographic message before choosing to stand his ground and die in the destruction of Jedha City.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Saw_Ger.timeline:
~42 BBY - Birth on Onderon

Saw is born on the Inner Rim world of Onderon, elder brother to Steela Gerrera. Canon never states his birth year; he is a grown man by the late Clone Wars and visibly middle-aged by his death.

~20 BBY - The Onderonian rebellion begins

After Separatist-backed King Sanjay Rash deposes Ramsis Dendup, Saw, Steela and Lux Bonteri form a rebel cell in the jungles outside Iziz.

~20 BBY - Trained by the Jedi

The Jedi Council covertly sends Anakin Skywalker, Obi-Wan Kenobi, Ahsoka Tano and Captain Rex to train the Onderon rebels in guerrilla warfare and sabotage.

~20 BBY - Capture and rescue

Saw is captured and tortured by droid forces after acting against orders; the rebels break him out during an operation in Iziz.

~20 BBY - Liberation of Onderon and Steela's death

Armed with Republic-supplied rocket launchers, the rebels destroy the Separatist droid presence and free King Dendup, but Steela falls to her death during the final battle — a loss that hardens Saw permanently.

19 BBY - The Empire rises

The Clone Wars end with the proclamation of the Galactic Empire, and Onderon's liberation gives way to Imperial occupation. Saw refuses to stand down.

~18 BBY - Insurgency with Clone Force 99

Now leading an early anti-Imperial fighting force on Onderon, Saw crosses paths with the renegade clones of the Bad Batch, warning them that neutrality is not an option and eventually abandoning his camp to take the fight elsewhere.

13 BBY - Rescuing Jyn Erso

Answering Galen and Lyra Erso's plea, Saw arrives on Lah'mu after Krennic's raid and takes the orphaned eight-year-old Jyn Erso into his care, raising her as a soldier.

~5 BBY - Abandoning Jyn

Fearing that her identity as Galen Erso's daughter makes her a liability — and her a liability to him — Saw leaves the sixteen-year-old Jyn behind to fend for herself.

5 BBY - Dealings with Luthen Rael

Saw negotiates with the rebel broker Luthen Rael for weapons and intelligence, while rejecting Luthen's push to unite the galaxy's fractured rebel cells under a common command.

~3 BBY - "Revolution is not for the sane"

Operating from a hidden fuel depot, Saw takes on the young Wilmon Paak and delivers his creed on the intoxicating madness of rebellion, exposing himself to rhydonium fumes that further ruin his lungs.

2 BBY - The mystery of Geonosis

Hunting for the truth behind the Empire's secret construction project, Saw meets the crew of the Ghost above Geonosis and discovers the near-total extermination of the Geonosian species, plus evidence of a massive kyber crystal shipment.

~1 BBY - The Partisans on Jedha

Following the kyber trail, Saw bases his Partisans in the Catacombs of Cadera and wages a violent street war against Imperial convoys in the Holy City, alienating the Rebel Alliance leadership with his methods.

0 BBY - Interrogating Bodhi Rook

Saw's Partisans capture the defecting Imperial cargo pilot Bodhi Rook, who carries Galen Erso's message; Saw subjects him to the mind-shredding Bor Gullet before accepting his story.

0 BBY - Reunion with Jyn Erso

Rebel intelligence brings Jyn back to Jedha. Saw, now frail and reliant on a breathing apparatus, apologises for abandoning her and shows her Galen's holographic confession revealing the Death Star's deliberate flaw.

0 BBY - Death in the destruction of Jedha City

When the Death Star fires a single-reactor blast at the Holy City, Saw refuses to flee, telling Jyn to save the Rebellion and "save the dream." He is killed as the shockwave engulfs the catacombs.

death.source: Wookieepedia "Saw Gerrera" and StarWars.com Databank: Saw chose to remain in Jedha City and was killed by the shockwave of the Death Star's single-reactor-ignition test, during the events of Rogue One (0 BBY).

notes.suggested: Keep -42, keep the guess flag
notes.detail: Canon gives no birth year for Saw Gerrera — Wookieepedia's article carries no dated birth, and no reference book pins one down. 42 BBY is a reasonable placement (an adult combat leader by ~20 BBY, middle-aged and physically wrecked by 0 BBY), and the existing 'guess' flag is the correct handling. Do not promote it to a known date.

notes.current: endYear 0, endYearEvent "Rogue One: A Star Wars Story"
notes.suggested: No change
notes.detail: Confirmed: Saw dies in the destruction of Jedha City by the Death Star's single-reactor test in 0 BBY, days before the Battle of Yavin. Note that some secondary sites date the Jedha siege to 1 BBY; the destruction itself and Saw's death are 0 BBY.

notes.current: Ends with his reputation as an extremist recognized by the Empire and the New Republic
notes.suggested: Add his role as Jyn Erso's guardian and the founding of the Partisans
notes.detail: The current text stops at the Clone Wars and a vague gesture at his later career. His two most defining canon acts — rescuing and then abandoning Jyn Erso, and leading the Partisans on Jedha — are missing entirely.

notes.suggested: Add Affiliation: Onderon rebels / Partisans (Rebel Alliance, expelled)
notes.detail: Saw's defining organisational identity is the Partisans, the splinter militia the Alliance disowned. Worth surfacing given how often he is confused for a mainline Alliance figure.

notes.current: The Clone Wars, The Bad Batch, Star Wars Rebels, Andor, Rogue One
notes.suggested: No change — all five verified
notes.detail: All five listed appearances are real. If you ever track episode-level data, note he appears in both Andor seasons (Luthen dealings in season one, the Wilmon rhydonium arc in season two), not just one.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Darth_S.description: Sheev Palpatine, secretly the Sith Lord Darth Sidious, was a human politician from Naboo who rose to become Supreme Chancellor of the Galactic Republic and ultimately Emperor of the first Galactic Empire. Trained in the dark side by Darth Plagueis, he cultivated dual identities — the affable public senator Palpatine and the shadowy Sith master Sidious — using both to engineer the galaxy's collapse. He manipulated the Trade Federation's blockade of Naboo to win the chancellorship, orchestrated the Clone Wars from both sides, and used the conflict as a pretext to purge the Jedi Order through Order 66 and declare himself Emperor in 19 BBY. He ruled the galaxy through fear and the dark side until he was cast down by Darth Vader at the Battle of Endor in 4 ABY. Yet Sidious cheated death: having prepared cloned bodies and a hidden cult on Exegol, he returned decades later, only to be finally destroyed by Rey and the combined spirits of the Jedi in 35 ABY. He is remembered as the galaxy's greatest villain and the architect of the Republic's fall and the Empire's rise.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Darth_S.timeline:
84 BBY - Birth on Naboo

Sheev Palpatine is born into an aristocratic family in Theed, the capital of Naboo.

~65 BBY - Apprenticeship to Darth Plagueis

As a young man, Palpatine is discovered and taken as the Sith apprentice of Darth Plagueis, becoming Darth Sidious and learning the ways of the dark side while building a political career.

~52 BBY - Enters Naboo politics

Palpatine establishes himself in public life, eventually representing Naboo as its senator in the Galactic Senate on Coruscant.

~32 BBY - Murder of Darth Plagueis

Following the Rule of Two, Sidious kills his master Plagueis in his sleep, becoming the reigning Sith Lord and taking his own apprentice, Darth Maul.

32 BBY - The Phantom Menace

Sidious orchestrates the Trade Federation's blockade and invasion of Naboo, using the ensuing crisis to have Chancellor Valorum ousted and to be elected Supreme Chancellor of the Republic.

22 BBY - Attack of the Clones and the Clone Wars begin

Manipulating the Separatist crisis through his apprentice Count Dooku, Palpatine is granted emergency powers as the Clone Wars erupt, secretly commanding both the Republic and Separatist forces.

22–19 BBY - The Clone Wars

Palpatine prolongs the galaxy-spanning war to consolidate power, erode the Senate, and set the Jedi Order up for destruction.

19 BBY - Revenge of the Sith

Sidious turns Anakin Skywalker to the dark side as Darth Vader, issues Order 66 to exterminate the Jedi, reorganizes the Republic into the Galactic Empire, and declares himself Emperor.

0 BBY - A New Hope

Emperor Palpatine rules through the Death Star and his apprentice Vader as the Rebel Alliance destroys the battle station at Yavin.

3 ABY - The Empire Strikes Back

The Emperor directs Vader to hunt down Luke Skywalker, sensing the young Jedi's potential and plotting to turn him.

4 ABY - Death at the Battle of Endor

Aboard the second Death Star, Palpatine attempts to corrupt Luke, but Vader turns on his master and hurls him into the reactor shaft, killing the Emperor at the Battle of Endor.

~5–35 ABY - Secret return on Exegol

Having transferred his essence into cloned bodies prepared on the hidden Sith world of Exegol, a weakened Sidious survives and builds the Sith Eternal cult and a secret armada while orchestrating events from the shadows.

35 ABY - The Rise of Skywalker

Palpatine reveals his return and lures his granddaughter Rey to Exegol. Drawing on the strength of all the Jedi, Rey turns his own Force lightning against him, destroying Sidious and his cult once and for all.

birth.source: Star Wars: Timelines (2023) and The Rise and Fall of the Galactic Empire (2024) date his birth to 84 BBY in Theed, Naboo. Note: older canon sources (Clone Wars Character Encyclopedia, etc.) gave 82 BBY.

death.source: The Rise of Skywalker (2019): Palpatine is destroyed permanently at Exegol in 35 ABY. (He first 'died' at the Battle of Endor in 4 ABY but returned via cloning.)

notes.suggested: Keep 84 BBY, but be aware of the 82 BBY conflict
notes.detail: 84 BBY is supported by the most recent canon reference books (Star Wars: Timelines, 2023; The Rise and Fall of the Galactic Empire, 2024), which state he was born in Theed. However, earlier canon sources (e.g. the Clone Wars Character Encyclopedia) placed his birth at 82 BBY. The 84 BBY value is the current best canon and matches our entry, so no change is needed — just noting the unresolved older figure.

notes.current: Only the 35 ABY Exegol death (Rise of Skywalker) is recorded
notes.suggested: Consider noting the 4 ABY Endor 'first death'
notes.detail: Palpatine died twice in canon: first at the Battle of Endor in 4 ABY (Return of the Jedi), then permanently at Exegol in 35 ABY. Our endYear of 35 ABY is correct for the column's end, but the 4 ABY death is a major milestone the entry does not surface.

notes.current: seenIn omits Episode III: Revenge of the Sith
notes.suggested: Add Episode III: Revenge of the Sith
notes.detail: Revenge of the Sith is arguably Palpatine's single most important on-screen appearance (Order 66, becoming Emperor) yet it is missing from seenIn, while Episodes I, II, IV, V, VI and IX are listed. Also worth considering: Rebels and Tales of the Empire feature him. This is his most conspicuous seenIn gap.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Asajj_V.description: Asajj Ventress was a Dathomirian warrior whose life spanned nearly every role the galaxy could offer: Nightsister, Jedi Padawan, Sith-trained assassin, Separatist commander, bounty hunter, and ultimately a redeemed heroine. Born on Dathomir into a Nightsister clan, she was given away as a child and endured slavery before being taken as an apprentice by a stranded Jedi. She rose to prominence during the Clone Wars as Count Dooku's deadly dark-side assassin, dueling Anakin Skywalker, Obi-Wan Kenobi, and Ahsoka Tano. After Dooku betrayed her, she returned to her Nightsister kin, survived General Grievous's massacre of them, and reinvented herself as a bounty hunter. Her arc culminated when she aided the fallen Jedi Quinlan Vos and sacrificed herself to save him from Dooku's Force lightning — though Nightsister magic later restored her to life, allowing her to resurface in the years after the Clone Wars.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Asajj_V.timeline:
50 BBY - Birth on Dathomir

Asajj Ventress is born into a clan of Nightsister witches on the planet Dathomir.

~49 BBY - Given away as a child

As a young child, Ventress is handed over to the criminal Hal'Sted and taken offworld, where she ends up enslaved. (Elements of this early backstory, including her time on Rattatak, originate in Legends material.)

~40 BBY - Trained by a stranded Jedi

After her master's death, she is discovered and trained in the ways of the Force by the marooned Jedi Ky Narec, whom she later loses to violence — a loss that drives her toward the dark side.

~22 BBY - Assassin for Count Dooku

By the outbreak of the Clone Wars, Ventress serves Count Dooku as a dark-side assassin and Separatist commander, clashing repeatedly with Anakin Skywalker, Obi-Wan Kenobi, and Ahsoka Tano.

~21 BBY - Betrayed by Dooku

Fearing her growing power, Dooku orders her killed. Surviving the betrayal, Ventress returns to Dathomir and rejoins Mother Talzin and the Nightsisters, plotting revenge.

~21 BBY - Massacre of the Nightsisters

General Grievous leads a droid army that slaughters the Nightsister clan. Ventress survives but is left without her people, becoming a wandering bounty hunter.

~20 BBY - Aiding Ahsoka Tano

Now a bounty hunter, Ventress crosses paths with Ahsoka Tano and helps clear the framed Padawan's name during Ahsoka's flight from the Jedi Order.

~19 BBY - Dark Disciple and sacrifice

Ventress joins the Jedi Quinlan Vos in a mission to assassinate Dooku, falling in love with him. She sacrifices herself, shielding Vos from Dooku's Force lightning, and appears to die.

~18 BBY - Restored to life

Through Nightsister magic she is resurrected, and in the years following the Clone Wars she resurfaces alive, appearing during the era of The Bad Batch.

birth.source: Wookieepedia and Star Wars reference sources give her birth as 50 BBY on Dathomir; matches our stored value.

death.source: She is killed by Dooku's Force lightning in the novel Dark Disciple (~19 BBY), but is resurrected by Nightsister magic and appears alive in The Bad Batch (~18 BBY) and Tales of the Underworld (2025). She therefore has no permanent canon death year; her fate remains open.

notes.suggested: Dathomirian (Zabrak)
notes.detail: Canon (Wookieepedia infobox, StarWars.com Databank) classifies Ventress as a Dathomirian — a near-human Zabrak subspecies native to Dathomir. Listing only 'Zabrak' is imprecise; 'Dathomirian' is the more accurate canon species.

notes.suggested: Keep endYearUnknown true, but note the Dark Disciple event
notes.detail: Ventress is killed in the 2015 novel Dark Disciple around 19 BBY, then resurrected by Nightsister magic. Because she survives (confirmed by her appearances in The Bad Batch and 2025's Tales of the Underworld), keeping the column open is correct, but the maintainer should be aware that a well-known 'death' exists in Dark Disciple that was later retconned by resurrection.

notes.current: The Clone Wars (TV series), The Bad Batch
notes.suggested: Consider adding Tales of the Underworld and the novel Dark Disciple
notes.detail: Ventress is a central character of the canon novel Dark Disciple and of the 2025 series Tales of the Underworld, which explains how she survived her death. Neither is tracked in seenIn.

notes.current: Describes Hal'Sted, Rattatak, and Jedi Knight Ky Narec as her early history
notes.suggested: Flag these elements as Legends-derived / uncertain in canon
notes.detail: The detailed slavery-on-Rattatak and training-by-Ky-Narec backstory originates in Legends (Clone Wars multimedia project / Star Wars: Republic comics). Canon confirms she was born a Nightsister on Dathomir and was given away as a child, but the Rattatak and Ky Narec specifics are not firmly established in current canon and should be treated cautiously.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Rose_Ti.description: Rose Tico is a human woman born in 11 ABY on the Otomok-system world of Hays Minor, whose people were exploited and brutalized by the First Order — an experience that fueled her lifelong hatred of the regime. She and her older sister Paige both joined the Resistance, where Rose served as a flight engineer and maintenance technician. She is best known for her role during the war against the First Order: after Paige died as a bomber gunner at the Battle of D'Qar, Rose partnered with the former stormtrooper Finn on a covert mission to the casino city of Canto Bight to find a codebreaker who could disable the First Order's hyperspace tracker. Though the plan failed after they were betrayed by the slicer DJ, Rose fought in the Battle of Crait, where she saved Finn's life by stopping him from a suicidal charge. She continued serving the Resistance through the final campaign against the resurgent Sith Eternal, remaining at the base during the Battle of Exegol, and survived the war.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Rose_Ti.timeline:
11 ABY - Birth on Hays Minor

Rose Tico is born on Hays Minor in the Otomok system, a world stripped and poisoned by the First Order, alongside her older sister Paige.

~34 ABY - Joins the Resistance

Rose and Paige enlist with the Resistance, Rose working as a flight engineer and maintenance technician while Paige serves as a bomber gunner.

34 ABY - Death of Paige at the Battle of D'Qar

During the Resistance evacuation of D'Qar, Paige Tico is killed manning her bomber against the First Order dreadnought, leaving Rose grieving her sister.

34 ABY - Meeting Finn and the Canto Bight mission

Rose stops the former stormtrooper Finn from deserting the fleet, and the two set out for the casino city of Canto Bight to recruit a codebreaker capable of disabling the First Order's hyperspace tracker.

34 ABY - Betrayal and capture

Rose, Finn, and BB-8 instead enlist the slicer DJ, who betrays them to the First Order after their infiltration of the Supremacy fails.

34 ABY - Battle of Crait

In the ground battle on the mineral world of Crait, Rose saves Finn's life by ramming his speeder to stop his suicidal charge, and afterward shares a moment with him before escaping aboard the Millennium Falcon.

35 ABY - The war against the Sith Eternal

Rose continues to serve the Resistance during its final campaign, remaining at the base to help plan the assault on Exegol against the resurgent Emperor Palpatine's fleet.

35 ABY - Victory and survival

Rose survives the Battle of Exegol and the defeat of the First Order and Sith Eternal, celebrating the Resistance's triumph.

birth.source: Wookieepedia (Rose Tico) states she was born in 11 ABY on Hays Minor in the Otomok system.

death.source: Rose Tico survives the sequel trilogy; canon records no death for her.

notes.current: startYear 11, no explicit birthYear field, presented as known
notes.suggested: Add birthYear: 11 (or confirm 11 ABY) with confidence noted
notes.detail: Wookieepedia confirms Rose was born in 11 ABY, so the claimed-known birth date is correct. No change needed to the value; just confirming it checks out.

notes.suggested: Keep endYearUnknown true
notes.detail: Rose Tico does not die in canon; she survives the war against the First Order and Sith Eternal (35 ABY) and is alive at the end of The Rise of Skywalker. The 50 ABY column end is correctly flagged as a rendering bound, not a death.

notes.current: Only Episodes VIII and IX listed
notes.suggested: Optionally note additional canon appearances
notes.detail: Rose also appears in canon comics and reference material (e.g. the Age of Resistance – Rose Tico one-shot and Poe Dameron / DJ tie-ins). Not required for the timeline, but worth knowing the film list is not her full canon footprint.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Leia_Sk.description: Leia Skywalker Organa Solo was a princess, senator, revolutionary, and military leader who became one of the most pivotal figures in the fight against tyranny across three galactic conflicts. The biological daughter of Padmé Amidala and Anakin Skywalker (later Darth Vader) and twin sister of Luke Skywalker, she was born in 19 BBY and adopted at birth by Senator Bail Organa and Queen Breha Organa of Alderaan, becoming Princess Leia of the Core World's royal House of Organa. Serving in the Imperial Senate as a cover for her work with the growing rebellion, she was captured while carrying the stolen Death Star plans, witnessed the Empire destroy Alderaan, and was rescued in the events of A New Hope. She became a leading figure of the Rebel Alliance, helped bring down the Empire at the Battle of Endor, and married the smuggler Han Solo, with whom she had a son, Ben. In the New Republic era she founded and led the Resistance against the First Order as General Organa, and though a trained but largely unfulfilled Force-sensitive, she passed her Jedi knowledge to Rey before dying in 35 ABY on Ajan Kloss, becoming one with the Force as she reached out to her fallen son.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Leia_Sk.timeline:
19 BBY - Birth on Polis Massa

Leia is born alongside her twin brother Luke to Padmé Amidala, who dies shortly after childbirth. To hide the children from Darth Vader and the Emperor, Leia is adopted by Senator Bail Organa and Queen Breha Organa of Alderaan.

~10 BBY - Childhood adventure with Obi-Wan Kenobi

As a headstrong young princess, Leia is kidnapped and rescued by Obi-Wan Kenobi, forging an early connection with the exiled Jedi Master during the events of the Obi-Wan Kenobi series.

~5 BBY - Secret work for the rebellion

Following in her adoptive father's footsteps, Leia becomes a member of the Imperial Senate while covertly aiding the growing rebel movement against the Galactic Empire.

0 BBY - The Death Star plans and the fall of Alderaan

Entrusted with the stolen Death Star plans, Leia is captured by Darth Vader aboard the Tantive IV. She is forced to watch the Empire destroy Alderaan, then is rescued from the Death Star by Luke Skywalker, Han Solo, and Obi-Wan Kenobi in A New Hope.

3 ABY - Escape from Hoth and capture at Cloud City

After the Empire overruns Echo Base, Leia flees with Han Solo, and the two fall in love before being betrayed at Cloud City, where Han is frozen in carbonite, as depicted in The Empire Strikes Back.

4 ABY - Victory at Endor

Leia rescues Han from Jabba the Hutt, learns that Luke is her twin brother and Vader her father, and helps the Rebel Alliance destroy the second Death Star at the Battle of Endor in Return of the Jedi.

~5 ABY - Marriage to Han Solo

In the aftermath of the Empire's defeat, Leia continues to build the New Republic and marries Han Solo.

~5 ABY - Birth of Ben Solo

Leia and Han have a son, Ben, who is later trained by Luke before falling to the dark side as Kylo Ren.

~28 ABY - Founding the Resistance

Disillusioned with the New Republic's complacency toward the rising First Order, General Leia Organa founds and leads the Resistance.

34 ABY - The war against the First Order

Leia leads the Resistance through the destruction of the Hosnian system and the Starkiller Base crisis, survives an attack in the vacuum of space using the Force, and guides the battered remnants of her fleet to survival during the events of The Force Awakens and The Last Jedi.

35 ABY - Death on Ajan Kloss

Leia completes Rey's Jedi training and, in a final act, reaches out through the Force to her son Ben in an effort to turn him from the dark side. The effort costs her life, and she becomes one with the Force during the events of The Rise of Skywalker.

birth.source: Wookieepedia (Leia Skywalker Organa Solo) and canon reference material place her birth in 19 BBY, immediately following Revenge of the Sith; corroborated by search of the Wookieepedia article.

death.source: Wookieepedia: Leia dies in 35 ABY on Ajan Kloss during The Rise of Skywalker, becoming one with the Force.

notes.suggested: Alderaan (born on Polis Massa)
notes.detail: Alderaan is Leia's adopted/cultural homeworld and is the standard Wookieepedia listing, so it is not wrong to display. However, she was physically born on the asteroid medical facility Polis Massa. Worth a footnote if the app distinguishes birthplace from homeworld.

Verify Leia's inclusion in Star Wars Resistance. She is not a recurring or clearly featured character in that series; if the app requires an actual on-screen appearance, this entry may be an error and should be double-checked against the show's episode list.

notes.suggested: add birthYear: -19
notes.detail: Birth year (19 BBY) and startYear coincide here, so no separate birthYear field is strictly needed. Both dates we assert (19 BBY birth, 35 ABY death) are confirmed canon and require no change — the entry's dates are accurate.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/R5-D4.description: R5-D4, nicknamed "Red" by Luke Skywalker, is a red-and-white R5-series astromech droid built by Industrial Automaton — one of the last units off a budget production line notorious for malfunctions. Active on Tatooine as early as the era of the Clone Wars, when he was glimpsed rolling through the streets of Mos Espa, he later fell into the hands of a band of Jawas who wiped his memory and hawked him from their sandcrawler. His moment of galactic consequence came shortly before the Battle of Yavin: offered for sale to Owen Lars alongside R2-D2, R5-D4 blew his own motivator so that Lars would take R2-D2 instead, ensuring the astromech carrying the stolen Death Star plans reached Luke Skywalker. R5 survived the Imperial raid that destroyed the sandcrawler and its Jawa crew, and was still operational decades later in the New Republic era, working out of Peli Motto's hangar in Mos Eisley, where he projected a holomap of Tatooine for the Mandalorian Din Djarin. Motto later sold him to Djarin outright, and R5 accompanied the Mandalorian to Mandalore, surviving an Alamite ambush to confirm the ruined planet's atmosphere was breathable — a scan that opened the way for the Mandalorians' return home.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/R5-D4.timeline:
Unknown date - Manufacture by Industrial Automaton

R5-D4 is produced as one of the last units of the R5-series astromech line, a low-cost design plagued by defects and poor customer reception. Canon does not give a construction year.

~22 BBY - Seen in the streets of Mos Espa

R5-D4, then serving a master on Tatooine, rolls through Mos Espa around the time Anakin Skywalker returns to the planet in search of his mother.

~4 BBY - Acquired by Jawas and memory-wiped

The droid falls into the possession of a Jawa scavenger crew, who wipe his memory and press him into service as salvage stock aboard their sandcrawler.

0 BBY - The blown motivator

Jawas offer R5-D4 to Owen Lars at the Lars moisture farm. On R2-D2's advice, R5 deliberately blows his own motivator, causing Lars to buy R2-D2 — and the Death Star plans hidden in his memory — instead.

0 BBY - Survives the sandcrawler massacre
