Imperial stormtroopers hunting the missing droids destroy the sandcrawler and slaughter its Jawa crew. R5-D4 survives the attack.

9 ABY - In service at Peli Motto's hangar

Now working for Mos Eisley hangar manager Peli Motto, R5-D4 projects a holographic map of Tatooine to help Din Djarin locate the Tusken Raiders and a mudhorn-hunting job.

9 ABY - Continued work in Mos Eisley

R5 remains part of Motto's hangar operation during Boba Fett's rise on Tatooine, assisting with repairs and errands around Docking Bay 3-5.

9 ABY - Sold to Din Djarin

Motto sells the reluctant droid to Din Djarin — persuading him with the threat of being sold back to the Jawas — so the Mandalorian has an astromech for his journey to Mandalore.

9 ABY - Atmospheric survey of Mandalore

Sent out alone onto the ruined surface of Mandalore, R5 is attacked by an Alamite but escapes and confirms the atmosphere is breathable, allowing Djarin to explore the Mines of Mandalore and ultimately enabling the Mandalorians' return to their homeworld.

notes.current: 25 BBY, flagged as a guess (startYearUnknown: true)
notes.suggested: Keep the guess flag; a defensible bound is "by 22 BBY"
notes.detail: Canon never states R5-D4's construction date. The only hard constraint is his cameo in the streets of Mos Espa in Attack of the Clones (22 BBY), so he must predate that. 25 BBY is a reasonable positioning value — just make sure it stays flagged as a guess rather than becoming asserted fact.

notes.suggested: Keep endYearUnknown; last confirmed active 9 ABY
notes.detail: R5-D4 has no canon death or deactivation. His latest confirmed activity is The Mandalorian season 3 (9 ABY). The 50 ABY bound is fine as a rendering device but the column implies far more on-screen life than canon supports.

notes.current: "...survived an Imperial attack on the sandcrawler soon after, and went out to find the Rebellion."
notes.suggested: Drop or soften "went out to find the Rebellion"
notes.detail: The sandcrawler survival is canon, but R5-D4 setting out to join the Rebellion is a Legends-era beat (the old "Star Wars Tales"/Expanded Universe material). Current canon simply has him surviving and later resurfacing on Tatooine in New Republic times.

notes.current: Homeworld: Unknown; Species: Droid; Force Sensitive: No
notes.suggested: Add Manufacturer: Industrial Automaton; Model: R5-series astromech droid; Class: Astromech
notes.detail: For droids, manufacturer and model carry the information a homeworld would. R5-D4 is specifically noted as one of the last units of Industrial Automaton's troubled, cost-cut R5 line — the in-universe reason he is so failure-prone.

notes.current: Episode II, Episode IV, The Mandalorian, The Book of Boba Fett, Galaxy's Edge
notes.suggested: Note that his largest role is The Mandalorian season 3 (Chapters 17-18 and beyond), not just the season 1 cameo
notes.detail: His most substantial screen role is as Din Djarin's astromech in season 3, including the Mandalore atmospheric scan. Worth surfacing if the timeline distinguishes seasons or episode-level appearances.

notes.suggested: Verify or drop
notes.detail: R5-series units are ubiquitous at Black Spire Outpost/Droid Depot, but I could not confirm a canon source identifying R5-D4 himself as present at Galaxy's Edge. If this was inferred from a generic red R5 unit, it should be removed.

notes.suggested: Also "R5" / "Arfive"
notes.detail: Peli Motto and Din Djarin consistently call him "R5" (Arfive) in the New Republic era; "Red" is Luke Skywalker's one-off nickname from A New Hope.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/8D8.description: 8D8 was a masculine-programmed 8D-series smelter droid manufactured by the Roche Hive, originally built for ore processing before being lobotomized and reprogrammed into a torture-chamber assistant. In that role he served EV-9D9, the chief of Cyborg Operations in Jabba Desilijic Tiure's palace on Tatooine, where he helped brand, mutilate and terrorize the droids pressed into the Hutt's service — a role in which he was glimpsed heating a branding iron over a droid strapped to a rack when C-3PO and R2-D2 were processed in 4 ABY. Deactivated along with the rest of the palace's droid pool after Jabba's death above the Great Pit of Carkoon, 8D8 was reactivated years later by Boba Fett, who took over the palace and installed the droid as his majordomo. In that capacity 8D8 announced visiting dignitaries and tribute-bearers, translated for Fett in negotiations with Mos Espa's crime families and Hutt rivals, coordinated the daimyo's day-to-day affairs, and stood with Fett's people through the war against the Pyke Syndicate. Long-suffering and dryly acerbic, he habitually urged Fett to rule through fear and intimidation rather than the reciprocal loyalty his master preferred, and he retained an open resentment of the more sophisticated protocol and astromech droids he had once tormented.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/8D8.timeline:
Date unknown - Manufacture on Roche

8D8 was built by the Roche Hive as an 8D-series smelter droid, a 1.83-meter industrial unit intended for ore processing. Canon gives no construction date.

Date unknown - Lobotomized and repurposed

At some point before Jabba the Hutt's death, 8D8 was lobotomized and reprogrammed to serve EV-9D9 in the droid torture chamber beneath Jabba's palace on Tatooine, terrorizing and mutilating the palace's droid pool.

4 ABY - Processing C-3PO and R2-D2

When Luke Skywalker's droids were delivered to Jabba's palace, 8D8 worked alongside EV-9D9 in the torture chamber, branding the feet of a screaming Power droid as the newcomers were assigned their duties.

4 ABY - Deactivation after Jabba's death

Following Jabba's death at the Great Pit of Carkoon and the collapse of his criminal empire, the palace droids — including 8D8 — were shut down and left dormant.

9 ABY - Reactivated by Boba Fett

After Boba Fett killed Bib Fortuna and claimed the palace and the title of daimyo of Mos Espa, 8D8 was reactivated and made Fett's majordomo, announcing visitors and presenting tribute alongside Fennec Shand.

9 ABY - Serving the daimyo

8D8 translated for Fett in dealings with the Hutt twins, the mayor of Mos Espa's majordomo and the heads of the city's crime families, and repeatedly counseled his master that a crime lord must rule through fear rather than respect.

9 ABY - The war for Mos Espa

8D8 remained at Fett's side through the escalating conflict with the Pyke Syndicate, coordinating the daimyo's household and forces as fighting broke out in the streets of Mos Espa.

After 9 ABY - Continued service

8D8 survives the events of the war; canon records no destruction or deactivation, and his fate beyond this point is unknown.

notes.suggested: Keep flagged as a guess; no canon construction date exists
notes.detail: Canon never states when 8D8 was manufactured or how long he served in Jabba's palace. The 16 BBY value has no source behind it — correctly flagged, but maintainers should not let it drift into a 'known' date. If a positioning anchor is wanted, note only that he predates 4 ABY.

notes.suggested: Manufacturer: Roche Hive (or keep Roche but relabel)
notes.detail: Roche is the asteroid field / Verpine hive where the 8D-series was produced, not a 'homeworld' in the biological sense. For droids a 'Manufacturer' field is more accurate.

notes.suggested: Add Model: 8D-series smelter droid; add Gender: Masculine programming
notes.detail: The 8D-series designation is the single most distinctive fact about the character and is used across canon sources; the entry currently only says 'Droid'.

notes.suggested: Track that 8D8's role changed from torture-chamber assistant (4 ABY) to Boba Fett's majordomo (9 ABY)
notes.detail: The two appearances are five years and one employer apart; the entry's description mentions it but the timeline data has no event granularity for it.

notes.suggested: Consider clamping the column near 9 ABY
notes.detail: Correctly marked as not a death — 8D8 survives The Book of Boba Fett — but he has no canon appearance after 9 ABY, so drawing the column to 50 ABY implies far more continuity than canon supports.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Rex.description: Rex, born CT-7567, was a clone trooper of the Grand Army of the Republic, created on Kamino as a genetic replica of the bounty hunter Jango Fett. Rising to the rank of Clone Captain (and later Commander), he led the 501st Legion's Torrent Company during the Clone Wars, serving as second-in-command to Jedi General Anakin Skywalker and forging a close bond with Skywalker's Padawan Ahsoka Tano and with Marshal Commander Cody. Renowned for his bravery, tactical skill, and fierce loyalty to his men, Rex grew increasingly troubled by the clones' expendable treatment. During Order 66 the inhibitor chip in his brain forced him to turn on Ahsoka, but she removed it in time, allowing him to survive; the pair faked their deaths and became fugitives from the Empire. Years later he was found in self-imposed retirement on Seelos alongside fellow clones Wolffe and Gregor before joining the Rebellion. Rex ultimately fought in the Battle of Endor, making him one of the few clone troopers to survive the Clone Wars and live to see the fall of the Empire.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Rex.timeline:
32 BBY - Creation on Kamino

Rex is grown and decanted on the ocean world of Kamino, a clone of the bounty hunter Jango Fett, and given the designation CT-7567.

~22 BBY - The Clone Wars Begin

Rex serves as Captain of the 501st Legion's Torrent Company under Jedi General Anakin Skywalker, quickly earning a reputation for courage and dedication to his men.

~22 BBY - Partnership with Ahsoka Tano

Rex mentors and fights alongside Anakin's new Padawan, Ahsoka Tano, forming one of the war's most enduring friendships.

22 BBY to 19 BBY - Campaigns of the Clone Wars

Rex leads the 501st through countless front-line battles against the Confederacy of Independent Systems, working closely with Commander Cody and General Obi-Wan Kenobi, and grows disillusioned by the heavy losses among his brothers.

~19 BBY - Discovery of the Inhibitor Chips

The malfunction of clone trooper Tup leads Rex to help uncover the existence of the secret inhibitor chips implanted in every clone, foreshadowing Order 66.

19 BBY - Siege of Mandalore

Now a Commander, Rex leads the 501st alongside Ahsoka Tano to liberate Mandalore and capture Maul.

19 BBY - Order 66

Palpatine issues Order 66; Rex's inhibitor chip forces him to turn on Ahsoka, but she removes it in time. Together they survive the crash of their Star Destroyer and fake their deaths to escape the new Empire.

19 BBY - A Fugitive from the Empire

Considered killed in action, Rex goes into hiding, later removing the inhibitor chips from fellow clones Wolffe and Gregor.

~1 BBY - Retirement on Seelos

Rex, Wolffe, and Gregor live in a modified AT-TE walker on Seelos until the crew of the Ghost recruits Rex to the growing rebellion.

~1 BBY to 1 ABY - Service in the Rebellion

Rex fights alongside the Spectres and the Phoenix cell against the Empire, including the liberation of his adopted homeworld of Lothal.

4 ABY - Battle of Endor

Rex fights in the Battle of Endor, helping the Rebel Alliance destroy the second Death Star and bring down the Galactic Empire, surviving one of the war's decisive victories.

birth.source: Wookieepedia/StarWars.com databank: Rex was born (created) in 32 BBY on Kamino as a clone of Jango Fett.

death.source: Rex survives canon. He lives through Order 66, the Rebellion, and the Battle of Endor (4 ABY). No death is depicted or dated in canon.

notes.current: 30 (30 ABY, endYearUnknown=true, treated as a rendering bound)
notes.suggested: Keep endYearUnknown=true, but note his last confirmed canon activity is the Battle of Endor in 4 ABY
notes.detail: Rex does not die in canon and there is no basis for a 30 ABY endpoint. His latest confirmed on-screen contribution is fighting at the Battle of Endor (4 ABY), confirmed in the Star Wars Rebels finale epilogue narration ('Commander Rex' fought at Endor). The 30 ABY bound is fine as a pure rendering value but should not be read as any kind of canon date.

notes.current: The Clone Wars (movie), The Clone Wars (TV series), The Bad Batch, Star Wars Rebels
notes.suggested: Consider whether to note the Battle of Endor / Return of the Jedi connection
notes.detail: Dave Filoni has stated Rex is likely the bearded Rebel commando on the Endor strike team in Return of the Jedi, and the Rebels epilogue explicitly confirms he fought at Endor. Not a discrete 'seenIn' series, but a notable canon milestone the timeline may want to represent.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Sabine_.description: Sabine Wren is a human Mandalorian warrior, artist, and revolutionary from Clan Wren of House Vizsla, born in 21 BBY. A former cadet at the Imperial Academy on Mandalore, she defected after realizing the weapons she designed would be turned against her own people, and became "Spectre 5," the weapons and explosives expert of the Ghost crew during the early rebellion against the Galactic Empire. A gifted graffiti artist, her painted starbird came to inspire the symbol of the Alliance to Restore the Republic. She wielded the ancient Darksaber after taking it from Maul and trained with it under Kanan Jarrus despite having no notable Force sensitivity, ultimately surrendering it to Bo-Katan Kryze to unite the Mandalorian clans against Imperial rule. After the liberation of Lothal and the disappearance of Ezra Bridger and Grand Admiral Thrawn, she dedicated herself to finding her lost friend, later training as Ahsoka Tano's Padawan. In the Ahsoka era she helped track Thrawn's return and became stranded with Ahsoka in the distant galaxy of Peridea.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Sabine_.timeline:
21 BBY - Birth

Sabine Wren is born into Clan Wren, a Mandalorian family aligned with House Vizsla, with ties to the ancestral homeworld of Krownest.

~7 BBY - Imperial Academy cadet on Mandalore

As a teenager, Sabine trains at the Imperial Academy on Mandalore, where her talents as a weapons designer are recognized.

~6 BBY - Defection from the Empire

Realizing that the Imperial weapons she helped develop are meant to be used against fellow Mandalorians, Sabine defects, becoming estranged from her family and a target of the Empire.

~5 BBY - Joins the Ghost crew

Sabine joins the rebel cell aboard the Ghost as "Spectre 5," serving as its explosives and weapons expert and painting her art across their operations.

~4 BBY - Rise of the rebellion on Lothal

Sabine fights alongside the crew against Imperial forces, and her starbird artwork becomes an emblem of hope for the growing rebellion.

~3 BBY - Claiming the Darksaber

Sabine recovers the ancient Darksaber and, though not Force-sensitive, undertakes lightsaber training with Kanan Jarrus, embracing her role as a symbol for uniting Mandalore.

~3 BBY - Reunion with Clan Wren

Sabine returns to Krownest and helps free her father and reconcile with her family, rallying Clan Wren against the Empire's rule of Mandalore.

~2 BBY - Surrendering the Darksaber

To unite the Mandalorian clans, Sabine passes the Darksaber to Bo-Katan Kryze, affirming Bo-Katan's claim to lead their people.

~1 BBY - Liberation of Lothal

Sabine takes part in the final battle to free Lothal, during which Ezra Bridger vanishes into hyperspace with Grand Admiral Thrawn.

~5 ABY - The search for Ezra begins

After the fall of the Empire, Sabine sets out with Ahsoka Tano to find the missing Ezra Bridger, later becoming Ahsoka's Padawan.

~9 ABY - Hunt for Thrawn in the Ahsoka era

Sabine and Ahsoka investigate rumors of Thrawn's survival. After clashing with mercenaries Baylan Skoll and Shin Hati, Sabine helps reach the distant galaxy of Peridea, reunites with Ezra, and becomes stranded there with Ahsoka.

birth.source: Wookieepedia 'Sabine Wren' infobox and corroborating databases give her birth as 21 BBY (she is 16 at the start of Rebels, ~5 BBY).

death.source: Sabine survives Star Wars Rebels and the Ahsoka series; canon records no death. She is last seen alive, stranded in the galaxy of Peridea (~9 ABY).

notes.suggested: Minimal / latent (revealed in Ahsoka)
notes.detail: During Rebels Sabine was described as having no notable Force sensitivity, but the Ahsoka series (2023) establishes she can train as a Jedi Padawan and demonstrates latent Force ability. A flat 'No' is now inconsistent with canon; consider noting it as minimal or latent.

notes.suggested: Krownest (Clan Wren ancestral world); largely raised on Mandalore
notes.detail: Wookieepedia and other sources note Sabine was raised in part on Mandalore while regarding Clan Wren's ancestral world Krownest as her homeworld. Krownest is defensible but the Mandalore connection is worth acknowledging.

notes.suggested: Also add Star Wars: Forces of Destiny (and any live-action/animated cameo appearances)
notes.detail: Sabine appears in the Forces of Destiny shorts and other tie-in canon media; the seenIn list only plots the two primary series, which is acceptable but could be expanded.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Obi-Wan.description: Obi-Wan Kenobi, also known in his later years as Ben Kenobi, was a legendary human Jedi Master born in 57 BBY on Stewjon and taken to the Jedi Temple on Coruscant as a Force-sensitive child. Trained as the Padawan of Qui-Gon Jinn, he rose to become a Jedi Knight and later a Jedi Master and member of the Jedi High Council. He is best known for training Anakin Skywalker, serving as a Jedi General in the Grand Army of the Republic during the Clone Wars, and surviving Order 66 and the fall of the Republic. After Anakin's turn to the dark side as Darth Vader—whom Obi-Wan defeated on Mustafar—he went into exile on Tatooine to watch over the infant Luke Skywalker. Emerging years later to aid the Rebellion, he mentored Luke in the ways of the Force before sacrificing himself in a duel with Vader aboard the Death Star in 0 BBY, becoming one with the Force and continuing to guide Luke as a spectral presence.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Obi-Wan.timeline:
57 BBY - Birth on Stewjon

Obi-Wan Kenobi is born on the planet Stewjon. Discovered to be Force-sensitive, he is taken as an infant to the Jedi Temple on Coruscant to begin his training.

~44 BBY - Apprenticed to Qui-Gon Jinn

As a Jedi youngling nearing the age limit for selection, Obi-Wan is taken as the Padawan learner of Jedi Master Qui-Gon Jinn.

32 BBY - The Invasion of Naboo

During the crisis over Naboo, Obi-Wan and Qui-Gon encounter Anakin Skywalker. After Qui-Gon is slain by Darth Maul, Obi-Wan defeats the Sith and, honoring his master's dying wish, pledges to train Anakin. He is knighted and takes Anakin as his Padawan.

22 BBY - Outbreak of the Clone Wars

Investigating an assassination attempt on Padmé Amidala, Obi-Wan discovers the clone army on Kamino and the Separatist plot on Geonosis. The Battle of Geonosis begins the Clone Wars, in which he serves as a Jedi General.

22–19 BBY - Jedi General in the Clone Wars

Now a Jedi Master and Council member, Obi-Wan leads clone troopers across countless campaigns alongside Anakin and Ahsoka Tano, becoming one of the war's most celebrated commanders.

19 BBY - Duel on Mustafar and Order 66

Obi-Wan defeats General Grievous, then survives Order 66. After Anakin falls to the dark side and becomes Darth Vader, Obi-Wan defeats him in a duel on Mustafar, leaving him gravely wounded. He then delivers the newborn Luke Skywalker to the Lars family on Tatooine.

~19–0 BBY - Exile on Tatooine

Living as the hermit "Ben" Kenobi, Obi-Wan watches over Luke from afar while remaining in hiding from the Empire.

9 BBY - Rescue of Leia Organa

Drawn out of exile, Obi-Wan rescues a kidnapped young Leia Organa and confronts a resurgent Darth Vader, reaffirming his resolve to protect the Skywalker children.

0 BBY - Sacrifice aboard the Death Star

Reuniting with Luke, Obi-Wan begins his Jedi training and travels to the Death Star. In a final duel with Darth Vader, he allows himself to be struck down, vanishing into the Force so he can guide Luke as a spectral mentor.

3 ABY - Guidance on Hoth and beyond

As a Force spirit, Obi-Wan appears to Luke, urging him to seek out Yoda on Dagobah to complete his Jedi training.

birth.source: Wookieepedia 'Obi-Wan Kenobi' article and '57 BBY' article; widely established canon (born on Stewjon, taken to Jedi Temple as an infant)

death.source: Death aboard the first Death Star during his duel with Darth Vader in Episode IV: A New Hope, which is dated 0 BBY (year 0) in canon

notes.current: Does not list Obi-Wan Kenobi's Force-ghost appearance in Return of the Jedi's roster beyond the listed films
notes.suggested: Entry already lists Episode VI; verify it is intended to represent his Force-spirit appearance
notes.detail: Obi-Wan appears in Return of the Jedi only as a Force spirit (already in seenIn). His guidance role in The Empire Strikes Back (also listed) is likewise as a spirit. No correction needed, just confirming these are spirit appearances.

notes.suggested: 0 BBY (keep)
notes.detail: 0 BBY is correct: A New Hope is canonically the year 0. Flagging only because some sources loosely say '0 BBY/0 ABY'; the death is firmly at the ANH point, so 0 BBY as stored is accurate.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Phasma.description: Phasma was a human warrior from the nuclear-ravaged world of Parnassos who rose to become a captain in the First Order and commander of its stormtrooper legions. Born into the Scyre clan, she survived Parnassos's brutal wastelands to become her people's fiercest fighter before the arrival of the crashed First Order officer Brendol Hux gave her a path offworld — a path she seized by betraying her brother Keldo and her own clan. Rising through the First Order in her distinctive chromium armor, she is known for leading the assault on Jakku, for her ruthless efficiency, and for her rivalry with the defector Finn (FN-2187). She stood among the First Order's leadership alongside General Hux and Kylo Ren, and met her end during the Crait campaign aboard the Star Dreadnought Supremacy, falling into flames after a final duel with Finn.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Phasma.timeline:
6 ABY - Birth on Parnassos

Phasma is born on the harsh, post-apocalyptic world of Parnassos, growing up as a member of the Scyre clan.

~24 ABY - Warrior of the Scyre

Hardened by Parnassos's deadly environment, Phasma becomes the Scyre clan's most formidable fighter and a leader of its warriors, serving alongside her brother Keldo.

~28 ABY - Betrayal and departure from Parnassos

When First Order General Brendol Hux crash-lands on Parnassos, Phasma helps him reach his ship in exchange for passage offworld, betraying her brother Keldo and the remnants of her clan to secure her escape.

~28 ABY - Joining the First Order

Rescued from Parnassos, Phasma abandons her former life, rises through the First Order to become captain of its stormtrooper corps, and adopts her signature chromium armor.

34 ABY - Assault on Jakku and the Force Awakens

Phasma leads the First Order attack on Jakku, later reprimands the wavering trooper FN-2187 (Finn), and is captured aboard Starkiller Base, where she is forced at gunpoint to lower the base's shields before it is destroyed.

34 ABY - Star Wars Resistance

Around the events of the Force Awakens, Phasma appears in operations against the Resistance, reinforcing the First Order's tightening grip on the galaxy.

34 ABY - Death aboard the Supremacy

During the First Order's pursuit of the Resistance, Phasma confronts Finn aboard the Star Dreadnought Supremacy. After a fierce duel the deck gives way beneath her, and she falls into the flames below, ending her life during the events of The Last Jedi.

birth.source: Star Wars: Timelines reference book establishes Phasma's birth in 6 ABY on Parnassos (same age as fellow Scyre Siv); cited on her Wookieepedia article.

death.source: Dies aboard the Supremacy in Episode VIII: The Last Jedi (34 ABY); treated as canonical death on Wookieepedia and confirmed by cast/crew.

notes.suggested: Captain Phasma
notes.detail: Her rank-title 'Captain Phasma' is the name used in most marketing and reference material; worth surfacing as an alt title.

notes.suggested: Add Affiliation: First Order (formerly Scyre clan)
notes.detail: Her First Order allegiance is her defining trait and is not currently captured in metadata.

notes.current: Lists Star Wars Resistance, TFA, TLJ
notes.suggested: Consider noting the 'Captain Phasma' (2017) Marvel comic
notes.detail: The four-issue comic bridges TFA and TLJ, showing how she survived Starkiller Base's destruction. Not required for a screen-focused timeline, but it is her only depicted appearance between the two films.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Jabba_D.description: Jabba Desilijic Tiure, better known as Jabba the Hutt, was a Hutt gangster and crime lord who ran a vast criminal empire from his palace on the Outer Rim desert world of Tatooine. A member of the powerful Grand Hutt Council and the Desilijic clan, Jabba controlled much of the galaxy's piracy, slaving, spice-running, and smuggling operations, employing bounty hunters and enforcers to protect his interests. He is remembered for sponsoring the Boonta Eve podrace, for his dealings with smugglers such as Han Solo during the Galactic Civil War, and for holding both Han Solo (frozen in carbonite) and Princess Leia Organa captive. He met his end during the rescue of Han Solo, when Leia strangled him with the very chain he had used to enslave her aboard his sail barge over the Great Pit of Carkoon.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Jabba_D.timeline:
600 BBY - Birth

Jabba Desilijic Tiure is born into the influential Desilijic clan of Hutts, a species originating from Nal Hutta.

~32 BBY - Sponsoring the Boonta Eve Classic

An established crime lord on Tatooine, Jabba presides over the Boonta Eve podrace won by the young Anakin Skywalker, underscoring his dominance over the desert world.

22 BBY - The rescue of his son Rotta

During the Clone Wars, Jabba's son Rotta is kidnapped in a Separatist plot. Anakin Skywalker and Ahsoka Tano recover the Huttlet, and a grateful Jabba grants the Republic passage through Hutt-controlled space.

~22-19 BBY - Power during the Clone Wars

Jabba wields influence from the Grand Hutt Council and weathers threats to his family and holdings, including the treachery of Ziro the Hutt.

~1 BBY - Bounty on Han Solo

After the smuggler Han Solo jettisons a spice shipment to avoid capture, Jabba places a bounty on his head, sending bounty hunters after him.

0 BBY - Operations at the time of the Battle of Yavin

Jabba continues to run his smuggling and enforcement network out of Tatooine, confronting Han Solo over unpaid debts before Solo leaves on the mission to destroy the Death Star.

3 ABY - Han Solo delivered in carbonite

Following the events on Cloud City, the bounty hunter Boba Fett brings Han Solo, frozen in carbonite, to Jabba's Palace, where the smuggler is displayed as a trophy.

4 ABY - Death over the Great Pit of Carkoon

During the rescue of Han Solo, Jabba captures Luke Skywalker, Leia Organa, and their allies. Aboard his sail barge above the Sarlacc's pit, Leia strangles Jabba with the chain of her enslavement, killing him as the rescue erupts into battle.

birth.source: Wookieepedia / canon reference Star Wars: Absolutely Everything You Need to Know states Jabba was born 600 BBY (aged 604 at death in 4 ABY).

death.source: Return of the Jedi (4 ABY); Jabba killed by Leia Organa aboard his sail barge over the Great Pit of Carkoon.

notes.current: Episode V: The Empire Strikes Back
notes.suggested: Remove Episode V; consider whether Episode IV should note Special Edition-only appearance
notes.detail: Jabba does not physically appear in The Empire Strikes Back (he is only referenced). His appearance in A New Hope exists only in the Special/Digitally Remastered editions (added CGI scene at Docking Bay 94), not the original theatrical cut. His seenIn list should reflect that he appears in TPM, The Clone Wars film and series, ANH (Special Edition), and ROTJ.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Yoda.description: Yoda was a diminutive, green-skinned member of an unnamed and mysterious species who became the most revered Jedi of the modern age, serving as Grand Master of the Jedi Order and as a member of the Jedi High Council for centuries. Born around 896 BBY, he began instructing younglings roughly a century later and trained generations of Jedi over some 800 years, including Count Dooku and, indirectly, nearly every Jedi of the late Republic. Renowned less for his lightsaber prowess — though he was among the greatest duelists alive — than for his wisdom, humility and profound connection to the living Force, he guided the Order through the Naboo crisis, reluctantly led the Grand Army of the Republic as a general during the Clone Wars, and was ultimately deceived along with the rest of the Council by Darth Sidious. After Order 66 he dueled the newly declared Emperor in the Senate chamber, failed to defeat him, and accepted exile in the swamps of Dagobah, where he spent two decades learning from Qui-Gon Jinn's spirit how to retain his identity after death. In 3 ABY he trained Luke Skywalker in the ways of the Force, and in 4 ABY, at the age of 900, he died and became one with the Force — returning years later as a Force spirit to counsel Luke a final time on Ahch-To.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Yoda.timeline:
~896 BBY - Birth

Yoda is born, a member of a species whose name and homeworld are never revealed in canon.

~796 BBY - Begins training Jedi

About a century into his life, Yoda takes up the instruction of younglings, a vocation he will continue for roughly 800 years.

~232 BBY - Grand Master during the High Republic

By the height of the High Republic Era, Yoda serves as Grand Master of the Jedi Order, mentoring younglings and traveling the frontier alongside Jedi such as Master Zia and the initiates of Tenoo.

~132 BBY - Counsel after the Brendok tragedy

In the final years of the High Republic, Vernestra Rwoh brings Yoda word of the deaths of Indara, Torbin and Kelnacca, concealing the truth of what happened on Brendok.

~102 BBY - Trains Dooku

Yoda instructs the young Dooku, who will become one of the Order's most formidable Jedi before falling to the dark side.

32 BBY - The Phantom Menace

Yoda tests the young Anakin Skywalker and senses grave danger in the boy's fear; after Qui-Gon Jinn's death he reluctantly permits Obi-Wan Kenobi to train Anakin and recognizes that a Sith Lord has returned.

22 BBY - Attack of the Clones and the outbreak of war

Yoda leads the clone army to Geonosis to rescue the Jedi strike team, rescues Anakin and Obi-Wan from Dooku, and duels his former apprentice — the first battle of the Clone Wars.

22–19 BBY - The Clone Wars

Serving as a general of the Republic, Yoda leads campaigns such as the defense of Rugosa, survives an assassination attempt on Vjun, defends the Jedi Temple against Cad Bane's schemes, and grows increasingly troubled by the Order's role in the war.

~19 BBY - Journey to the living Force

Guided by the voice of Qui-Gon Jinn, Yoda undertakes a pilgrimage to the priestesses of Serenno's ancient world and to Moraband, confronting Darth Bane's shade and learning that he can retain his consciousness after death.

19 BBY - Order 66 and the duel with Sidious

Yoda survives the clone betrayal on Kashyyyk with the aid of Chewbacca and Tarfful, returns to Coruscant, and duels Darth Sidious in the Senate chamber. Unable to defeat the new Emperor, he withdraws.

19 BBY - Exile on Dagobah

Accepting that the Jedi have failed, Yoda goes into hiding in the swamps of Dagobah, continuing his studies with Qui-Gon's spirit and awaiting a new hope.

~2 BBY - Guiding Ezra Bridger

Through the Force, Yoda speaks to Ezra Bridger in the Jedi Temple on Lothal and later at the Temple's Force nexus, warning him of the perils of vengeance and pointing him toward Malachor.

3 ABY - Training Luke Skywalker

Luke Skywalker arrives on Dagobah at Obi-Wan's urging. Yoda tests him, reluctantly accepts him as a student, raises his X-wing from the bog, and warns him against leaving to confront Vader on Bespin.

4 ABY - Death and becoming one with the Force

At 900 years old, Yoda tells a returned Luke that Vader is his father and that another Skywalker remains, then dies and vanishes into the Force.

34 ABY - Return as a Force spirit

On Ahch-To, Yoda appears to a despairing Luke, calls down lightning on the ancient Jedi tree, and reminds him that failure is the greatest teacher — prompting Luke's final stand.

birth.source: Wookieepedia "Yoda" article, citing the canon reference book Star Wars Timelines (2023), which places his birth in 896 BBY; consistent with him being 900 years old at his death in 4 ABY (stated in Return of the Jedi).

death.source: Return of the Jedi (4 ABY) depicts Yoda's death on Dagobah; Wookieepedia's Yoda article dates it to 4 ABY, shortly before the Battle of Endor.

notes.suggested: Remove "Episode IV: A New Hope"
notes.detail: Yoda does not appear in A New Hope in any form — his first appearance in the saga is The Empire Strikes Back (3 ABY). Obi-Wan mentions the Jedi, but Yoda is neither seen nor heard.

notes.suggested: Add "Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi"
notes.detail: Yoda appears in the Tales of the Jedi shorts, notably in Ahsoka's arc ("Life and Death" and "Practice Makes Perfect"), which our list does not track.

notes.current: -/4 with endYearEvent "Episode VI: Return of the Jedi"
notes.suggested: Keep 4 ABY as the death year, but consider extending the drawn column (or adding a marker) to 34 ABY
notes.detail: 4 ABY is the correct death year, but Yoda's last canon appearance is as a Force spirit in The Last Jedi (34 ABY) — which our own seenIn list already includes. As drawn, the column ends 30 years before an appearance we plot, the same tension the entry already handles for other Force-spirit characters.

notes.suggested: No change; flag provenance
notes.detail: 896 BBY is correct but derives from a single reference book, Star Wars Timelines (2023), rather than from any film or series. On screen only his age of 900 in Return of the Jedi is given, and that figure is itself approximate in dialogue. Worth knowing that this is book-only precision.

notes.current: Homeworld: Unknown; Species: Yoda's Species; Force Sensitive: Yes
notes.suggested: Add "Rank: Grand Master of the Jedi Order" and optionally "Masters/Apprentices: Dooku, Luke Skywalker"
notes.detail: Homeworld "Unknown" and the species entry are both correct — canon deliberately withholds both. But Yoda's defining role, Grand Master of the Jedi Order (held at least as far back as 232 BBY during the High Republic), is not captured anywhere in the metadata.

notes.current: "...he played integral roles in the Clone Wars, the rebirth of the Jedi through Luke Skywalker, and unlocking the path to immortality."
notes.suggested: Note that Yoda learned the path from Qui-Gon Jinn's spirit rather than unlocking it himself
notes.detail: In canon the technique of retaining consciousness after death was discovered by Qui-Gon Jinn, who taught it to Yoda (and through him Obi-Wan) — Yoda was a student of it, not its discoverer. The current phrasing carries a Legends-era implication.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/R2-D2.description:
"

R2-D2, pronounced \"Artoo-Detoo\" and commonly called Artoo, is an R2-series astromech droid built by Industrial Automaton and given masculine programming. Small, stubborn and improbably brave, he served a long chain of masters — the Royal Engineers of Naboo, Queen and Senator Padmé Amidala, Anakin Skywalker, Bail Organa, Princess Leia Organa, Luke Skywalker and finally the Resistance — and, uniquely among droids, was never given a full memory wipe, allowing decades of accumulated experience to shape a wilful, resourceful and often sardonic personality. He repaired the shield generator of Queen Amidala's royal starship during the Trade Federation blockade of Naboo in 32 BBY, flew as Anakin Skywalker's astromech throughout the Clone Wars, witnessed the fall of the Republic and the birth of the Empire, and carried both the stolen Death Star plans and Leia's plea to Obi-Wan Kenobi to Tatooine in 0 BBY, setting the Galactic Civil War's decisive events in motion. He went on to help destroy the first Death Star, accompany Luke to Dagobah, aid the rescue of Han Solo and the victory at Endor, serve at Luke's Jedi temple, and decades later supply the missing piece of the map that led the Resistance to the exiled Luke Skywalker. His fate after the Battle of Exegol in 35 ABY is not recorded in canon; he is last seen alive and operational.

"
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/R2-D2.timeline:
"

Prior to 32 BBY - Manufacture

R2-D2 is built by Industrial Automaton as an R2-series astromech droid with masculine programming. Canon gives no construction date, only that he existed before 32 BBY.

32 BBY - Service with the Royal Engineers of Naboo

R2-D2 is in the possession of Naboo's Royal Engineers and assigned as a maintenance droid aboard Queen Padmé Amidala's Royal Starship.

32 BBY - Repairing the Royal Starship

During the escape from the Trade Federation blockade of Naboo, R2-D2 is the only astromech to survive repairing the ship's shield generator under fire, allowing the vessel to reach Tatooine. Amidala honors him personally.

32 BBY - Meeting Anakin Skywalker

On Tatooine, R2-D2 meets the slave boy Anakin Skywalker and his protocol droid C-3PO, beginning the galaxy's longest droid partnership.

32 BBY - Battle of Naboo

R2-D2 accompanies Anakin in the starfighter that destroys the Droid Control Ship above Naboo, helping end the occupation.

22 BBY - Separatist Crisis

Now in Padmé Amidala's service as a senator, R2-D2 travels with her to Naboo and Tatooine and follows Anakin and Obi-Wan Kenobi to Geonosis, where he and C-3PO help rescue them from the execution arena.

22 BBY - The Clone Wars begin

R2-D2 becomes Anakin Skywalker's personal astromech, flying with him aboard his Jedi starfighter for the duration of the war.

~22 BBY - Lost and recovered

R2-D2 is lost in battle and sold to the scavenger Gha Nachkt, who delivers him to General Grievous; Anakin, unwilling to lose a droid carrying unwiped strategic data, mounts a rescue and recovers him from Grievous' listening post.

21 BBY - The Senate hostage crisis

During Cad Bane's raid on the Senate Building, R2-D2 aids the response, but Bane erases the droid's record of the building's ventilation shafts — the only memory wipe, partial at that, R2-D2 ever receives.

19 BBY - Rescue over Coruscant

R2-D2 accompanies Anakin and Obi-Wan aboard the Invisible Hand to rescue Chancellor Palpatine, holding off battle droids and guiding the crippled cruiser's crash landing.

19 BBY - Fall of the Republic

R2-D2 travels to Mustafar with Padmé and witnesses Anakin's fall to the dark side, then escapes aboard the Tantive IV. C-3PO's memory is wiped to protect the secret of the Skywalker twins; R2-D2's is not.

19 BBY to 0 BBY - Service to House Organa

R2-D2 enters the service of Senator Bail Organa of Alderaan, retaining full knowledge of the Jedi, the Clone Wars and the Skywalkers throughout the Imperial era.

~5 BBY - Missions for the rebellion

Working covertly for Bail Organa, R2-D2 and C-3PO cross paths with the Spectres rebel cell, including a weapons-acquisition mission that leads to the crew's first encounter with the growing Rebel Alliance.

1 BBY - Yavin 4

R2-D2 is present at the Rebel Alliance's Massassi base during the mobilization that follows the theft of the Death Star plans from Scarif.

0 BBY - Carrying the Death Star plans

Aboard the Tantive IV over Tatooine, Princess Leia Organa loads the Death Star schematics and a plea for help into R2-D2, who escapes by pod with C-3PO and seeks out Obi-Wan Kenobi.

0 BBY - Battle of Yavin

Bought by the Lars family, R2-D2 brings Luke Skywalker into the war, helps rescue Leia from the Death Star, and rides in Luke's X-wing during the trench run that destroys the battle station.

3 ABY - Hoth, Dagobah and Bespin

R2-D2 serves with Rogue Squadron on Hoth, then flies with Luke to Dagobah for his training under Yoda and on to Cloud City, where he restores the damaged Millennium Falcon's hyperdrive to allow their escape.

4 ABY - Jabba's palace and Endor

R2-D2 plays his part in the rescue of Han Solo on Tatooine, concealing Luke's lightsaber aboard the sail barge, then joins the strike team on Endor and is injured shorting the shield bunker's doors during the battle that destroys the second Death Star.

After 4 ABY - The New Republic era

R2-D2 remains with Luke Skywalker through his search for Jedi lore and the founding of his Jedi temple.

9 ABY - Reunion with the Skywalker legacy

R2-D2 accompanies Luke when he answers the call aboard Moff Gideon's cruiser to take Grogu as an apprentice, and later escorts the young Jedi at Luke's temple as he chooses between the Jedi path and Din Djarin.

~28 ABY - Destruction of the temple

After Ben Solo's fall and the razing of Luke's academy, a grieving R2-D2 is left behind and eventually enters a self-imposed low-power mode at the Resistance base on D'Qar.

34 ABY - The map to Luke Skywalker

Roused by BB-8's arrival, R2-D2 awakens and produces the missing portion of the star map, revealing Ahch-To and sending Rey to find Luke Skywalker.

34 ABY - Ahch-To and Crait

R2-D2 travels with the Resistance during the flight from the First Order, replaying Leia's original message to persuade Luke to help, and survives the evacuation of Crait.

35 ABY - The war against the Final Order

R2-D2 supports the Resistance from Ajan Kloss through the Battle of Exegol, restoring C-3PO's wiped memory afterward. He is last recorded alive and active; canon gives no death.

"

birth.source: Wookieepedia's canon R2-D2 article gives no manufacture year — it states only that he was an R2-series astromech droid built by Industrial Automaton prior to 32 BBY, by which time he was in the service of the Royal Engineers of Naboo. No canon source (film, series, or reference book) pins a construction date.

death.source: No canon source records R2-D2's destruction or permanent deactivation. He is alive and active as of the Battle of Exegol in 35 ABY (Episode IX: The Rise of Skywalker), the latest canon point at which he appears.

notes.suggested: Keep 45 BBY only as a positioning guess; canon supports no more than "prior to 32 BBY"
notes.detail: Canon never states when R2-D2 was manufactured. Wookieepedia's canon article says only that he was built by Industrial Automaton prior to 32 BBY, when he was already serving the Royal Engineers of Naboo. The 45 BBY figure has no canon basis (Legends material sometimes places R2 as a much older unit), so the existing 'this is a guess' flag is correct and should stay.

notes.suggested: Manufacturer: Industrial Automaton (factories on Nubia); Naboo is where he served, not where he was built
notes.detail: R2-D2 is a droid, not a native of a homeworld. Canon establishes Industrial Automaton as his manufacturer, with its R-series factories on Nubia. Naboo is where he was first seen in service (Royal Engineers / Queen Amidala's Royal Starship). Listing 'Homeworld: Naboo' overstates canon; either relabel it 'Place of Service' or replace it with a Manufacturer/Model field.

notes.suggested: Add Model: R2-series astromech droid; Manufacturer: Industrial Automaton
notes.detail: These are the two facts canon actually asserts about his origin, and they're the analogue of species/homeworld for a droid entry.

notes.suggested: No change needed to the flag; last canon appearance is 35 ABY
notes.detail: Correctly flagged. R2-D2 has no canon death or deactivation; he survives the Battle of Exegol in 35 ABY. Worth noting the column extends 15 years past any canon appearance, which may mislead readers into assuming continued canon activity.

notes.current: Mentions Cad Bane's partial memory wipe as the only exception to never being wiped
notes.suggested: Clarify: Bane erased only R2's record of the Senate building's ventilation shafts during the 21 BBY hostage crisis
notes.detail: The current wording ('a partial wipe of select information by Cad Bane') is accurate but vague; the specific detail is that Bane wiped R2's memory of the ventilation system schematics he had just recorded. Also worth adding the contrast that C-3PO was memory-wiped in 19 BBY while R2 was not — that asymmetry is the reason R2 alone retains the whole saga's continuity.

notes.current: 16 titles listed; Star Wars Rebels and The Book of Boba Fett present
notes.suggested: Consider whether Galaxy's Edge belongs in a canon appearance list
notes.detail: Every listed film/series appearance checks out (including the Rogue One cameo on Yavin 4, the Rebels appearances beginning with 'Droids in Distress', The Mandalorian season two finale with Luke, and the Book of Boba Fett scene where he escorts Grogu). 'Galaxy's Edge' is a theme-park land rather than a narrative work, so it sits oddly alongside the films and series — flagging for consistency, not accuracy.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Babu_Fr.description: Babu Frik was an Anzellan male droidsmith who lived during the New Republic Era and the war between the First Order and the Resistance. Tiny in stature and highly accented in his speech, he maintained a workshop in the mountain city of Kijimi, where he worked as a black-market droidsmith among the Spice Runners of Kijimi. Renowned as one of the finest droidsmiths in the galaxy, he could reprogram or modify virtually any droid regardless of the security measures protecting its systems. He is best known for his role in the search for Emperor Palpatine, when the Resistance brought the protocol droid C-3PO to him so he could bypass the droid's programming restrictions and translate a forbidden inscription in the Sith language, a procedure that wiped C-3PO's memory. A fan-favorite introduced in Star Wars: Episode IX The Rise of Skywalker, Babu Frik became emblematic of his species, the Anzellans.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Babu_Fr.timeline:
~50 BBY - Birth

Babu Frik, an Anzellan, is born. His exact birth year is not stated in canon; it is derived from a reference-book statement that he was 85 years old during the events of The Rise of Skywalker (35 ABY).

By 35 ABY - Droidsmith of Kijimi

Babu Frik establishes himself as a black-market droidsmith with a workshop in the city of Kijimi, working among the Spice Runners of Kijimi and earning a reputation for being able to modify or reprogram nearly any droid.

35 ABY - Reprogramming C-3PO

During the Resistance's search for the Sith wayfinder, Poe Dameron leads Rey and the others to Babu Frik on Kijimi. Because C-3PO is forbidden by his programming to translate the Sith text held in his memory bank, Babu Frik bypasses those restrictions to read the inscription aloud, an act that erases the protocol droid's memory.

35 ABY - Survival on Kijimi

After aiding the Resistance, Babu Frik survives the events surrounding the final campaign against the Sith Eternal. Canon records no death for him.

birth.source: Derived: Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary states Babu Frik was 85 years old during the events of The Rise of Skywalker (35 ABY); 35 ABY minus 85 years = ~50 BBY. Canon gives an age, not an explicit birth year.

death.source: Babu Frik survives the events of The Rise of Skywalker (35 ABY). Canon records no death for him.

notes.current: startYearUnknown is absent (so the 50 BBY birth is presented as known fact)
notes.suggested: Set startYearUnknown: true and treat the birth as ~50 BBY (an estimate)
notes.detail: The 50 BBY birth is not stated in canon. It is derived from Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary, which gives his age as 85 during TROS (35 ABY): 35 ABY - 85 = ~50 BBY. Because it comes from an approximate age rather than a stated birth year, the app should flag it as a guess/estimate rather than assert it as known.

notes.suggested: Optionally note: 85 years old during The Rise of Skywalker (per the TROS Visual Dictionary)
notes.detail: The only chronological anchor canon provides is this stated age, which is the basis for the entire birth-year estimate. Worth recording so the estimate's provenance is clear.

notes.current: seenIn lists only Episode IX: The Rise of Skywalker
notes.suggested: Keep as-is; do not add The Mandalorian
notes.detail: The Mandalorian season 3 (set c. 9 ABY) features Anzellans of Babu Frik's species repairing IG-11 on Nevarro, prompting speculation that one is Babu Frik. They are unnamed and not confirmed by canon or Wookieepedia to be him, so leaving seenIn limited to TROS is correct. Flagging in case a future source identifies him.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Ben_Sol.description: Ben Solo, better known as the dark warrior Kylo Ren, was a human male Force-sensitive who served as master of the Knights of Ren and, ultimately, Supreme Leader of the First Order before returning to the light shortly before his death. Born in 5 ABY on the Core World of Chandrila to General Han Solo and Princess Leia Organa, he was the heir to the Skywalker bloodline and possessed immense raw power in the Force. Trained as a Jedi apprentice by his uncle, Luke Skywalker, during the New Republic Era, he was manipulated across his youth by the resurrected Darth Sidious and Sidious's creation, Supreme Leader Snoke, who lured him to the dark side and drove him to destroy Luke's fledgling temple. As Kylo Ren he hunted the last Jedi, killed his own father Han Solo, slew Snoke to seize command of the First Order, and clashed repeatedly with the scavenger Rey — with whom, unknown to either, he shared a rare dyad in the Force. Confronted by the memory of his father and Rey's compassion, Ben Solo rejected the dark side at the Battle of Exegol in 35 ABY, helped defeat the reborn Emperor, and gave his own life to revive Rey, ending the Skywalker line.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Ben_Sol.timeline:
5 ABY - Birth on Chandrila

Ben Solo is born on the Core World of Chandrila to Han Solo and Leia Organa on the very day the Galactic Empire surrendered to the New Republic, ending the Galactic Civil War.

~28 ABY - Fall of Luke's Temple

As a young apprentice at Luke Skywalker's Jedi temple, Ben is preyed upon by Snoke and Sidious. A moment of fear from Luke drives him fully to the dark side; he destroys the temple, takes fellow students as the Knights of Ren, and becomes Kylo Ren.

34 ABY - Hunt for the Map to Skywalker

Now a commander of the First Order, Kylo Ren captures the pilot Poe Dameron and the droid BB-8 on Jakku while searching for a map to the exiled Luke Skywalker.

34 ABY - Death of Han Solo

Aboard Starkiller Base, Kylo Ren confronts his father Han Solo on a bridge and kills him in an attempt to cast off the light. He is then wounded in a duel with Rey in the surrounding forest.

34 ABY - The First Battle with Rey

Overpowered by the untrained scavenger Rey, Kylo Ren is scarred and forced to flee as Starkiller Base collapses, marking the beginning of his obsession with her.

34 ABY - Supreme Leader of the First Order

Drawn into a Force-bond with Rey, Kylo Ren brings her before Snoke, then kills Snoke and battles the Praetorian Guard alongside her. He proclaims himself Supreme Leader of the First Order but fails to turn Rey to his side.

35 ABY - Alliance with the Resurrected Sidious

Kylo Ren travels to Exegol and pledges himself to the returned Darth Sidious, taking command of the Sith Eternal's Final Order fleet while continuing to pursue Rey across the galaxy.

35 ABY - Redemption of Ben Solo

After Rey wounds and then heals him, and confronted by a memory of his father, Kylo Ren casts away his lightsaber and reclaims his identity as Ben Solo, rejecting the dark side for good.

35 ABY - Death at the Battle of Exegol

Ben Solo joins Rey against Sidious on Exegol. After the Emperor's final defeat, he uses the Force to revive a fallen Rey, sacrificing his own life force and passing away as the last of the Skywalker bloodline.

birth.source: Wookieepedia (Ben Solo) and multiple canon sources: born in 5 ABY on Chandrila the day the Empire surrendered to the New Republic.

death.source: Canon sources place his death at the Battle of Exegol in 35 ABY (The Rise of Skywalker), where he sacrifices himself to revive Rey.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Cassian.description: Cassian Jeron Andor was a Rebel intelligence officer, spy, and pilot who became one of the most important operatives of the Alliance to Restore the Republic. Born as a boy named Kassa on the remote, mining-scarred world of Kenari, he was orphaned as a child and taken to the industrial planet Ferrix, where he was raised by Clem and Maarva Andor and grew into a cynical scavenger and thief. Recruited by the rebel spymaster Luthen Rael, Cassian was radicalized through the Aldhani garrison heist, a brutal wrongful imprisonment on Narkina 5, and the Imperial atrocities he witnessed — including the massacre on Ghorman — transforming from a disillusioned criminal into a committed and ruthless rebel agent operating under the codename Fulcrum and numerous cover identities. He is best known for leading the Rogue One team that infiltrated the Imperial data vault on Scarif to steal the Death Star plans, an act that gave the Rebellion its first true chance to destroy the battle station. Cassian was killed in the Death Star's bombardment of Scarif, sacrificing his life alongside Jyn Erso to ensure the plans reached the Alliance.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Cassian.timeline:
33 BBY - Birth on Kenari

Cassian is born on the isolated planet Kenari, where he lives among a tribe of children as a boy named Kassa.

~26 BBY - Orphaned and taken to Ferrix

After a Republic vessel crashes on Kenari, the young Kassa is taken in by salvagers Clem and Maarva Andor, who give him the name Cassian and a forged identity claiming Fest as his birthplace, raising him on the industrial world of Ferrix.

5 BBY - Killing on Morlana One and recruitment

While searching for his lost sister, Cassian kills two corporate security officers, drawing the attention of rebel spymaster Luthen Rael, who recruits him into the fledgling rebellion.

5 BBY - The Aldhani heist

Cassian joins a rebel cell to raid an Imperial garrison payroll vault on Aldhani, a daring robbery that helps galvanize open resistance to the Empire.

5 BBY - Imprisonment on Narkina 5

Arrested on a trumped-up charge, Cassian is sentenced to a brutal Imperial labor prison on Narkina 5, from which he engineers a mass escape.

5 BBY - The Ferrix uprising

Returning home for Maarva's funeral, Cassian witnesses her recorded call to resistance spark an uprising against Imperial forces and fully commits himself to the rebel cause.

~2 BBY - The Ghorman Massacre

Now an established rebel operative, Cassian is present as the Empire slaughters civilian protesters on Ghorman, an atrocity that hardens his resolve and deepens the Alliance's coordinated resistance.

~1 BBY - Rebel intelligence operative

Cassian carries out assassinations, extractions, and espionage missions for the Alliance under the codename Fulcrum and a series of cover identities, becoming a trusted agent of Rebel Intelligence.

0 BBY - Rogue One and the Battle of Scarif

Cassian leads the volunteer Rogue One team to infiltrate the Imperial archive on Scarif and steal the Death Star plans; after transmitting them to the fleet, he and Jyn Erso are killed in the Death Star's bombardment of the planet.

birth.source: Wookieepedia (Cassian Jeron Andor) gives his birth as 33 BBY on Kenari; corroborated by web sources.

death.source: Killed at the Battle of Scarif during Rogue One, 0 BBY (Wookieepedia; Rogue One events).

notes.suggested: Kenari (Fest as forged/claimed birthplace)
notes.detail: The Andor series retconned Cassian's origin: he was born as 'Kassa' on Kenari and raised on Ferrix. Fest is only the falsified birthplace on his forged documents, not his true homeworld. Wookieepedia lists Kenari as his origin.

Cassian did not meaningfully 'serve in the Confederacy of Independent Systems during the Clone Wars' (he was a young child, ~11–14, during the Clone Wars); this reflects a loose reading of his Rogue One line about fighting since age six. The listed aliases (Aach, Joreth Sward) are not his established current-canon cover names; documented aliases include Clem, Keef Girgo, Varian Skye, Ronni Googe, and the codename Fulcrum.

notes.detail: His childhood name Kassa and codename Fulcrum are notable secondary identities worth surfacing.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Beru_Wh.description: Beru Whitesun Lars was a human female moisture farmer from the desert world of Tatooine who, with her husband Owen Lars, raised Luke Skywalker as their own. Born into a family that had farmed moisture on Tatooine for generations, she was living and working at the Lars homestead by the time of the Clone Wars, where she met Anakin Skywalker and Padmé Amidala when Anakin returned home searching for his mother, Shmi Skywalker Lars. After the fall of the Republic in 19 BBY, Obi-Wan Kenobi delivered the infant Luke — Anakin and Padmé's son — into Beru and Owen's care, and the couple spent the next two decades raising the boy in obscurity under the Galactic Empire, with Beru often serving as the softer counterweight to Owen's protective sternness and quietly recognizing that Luke's restlessness would eventually carry him off-world. Their family life was strained by the secret of Luke's parentage and by the lurking danger of Imperial and Inquisitorial attention on Tatooine. In 0 BBY, stormtroopers hunting the droids R2-D2 and C-3PO tracked them to the Lars homestead and killed Beru and Owen, burning the farm; finding their remains severed Luke's last tie to Tatooine and set him on the path to joining the Rebel Alliance.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Beru_Wh.timeline:
Unknown date - Birth on Tatooine

Beru Whitesun was born into a Tatooine moisture-farming family. Canon has never established her birth year; the frequently quoted 47 BBY figure is Legends-only.

~22 BBY - Life at the Lars homestead

By the eve of the Clone Wars, Beru was part of the Lars household alongside Owen Lars, his father Cliegg, and Cliegg's wife Shmi Skywalker Lars.

22 BBY - Meeting Anakin Skywalker and Padmé Amidala

When Anakin came home in search of his mother, Beru welcomed him and Padmé, offering them food and shelter. Shmi died of her injuries after Anakin recovered her from a Tusken camp, and Beru attended her funeral.

~22-19 BBY - Marriage to Owen Lars

Beru married Owen and took the Lars name, continuing to work the moisture farm through the years of the Clone Wars.

19 BBY - Taking in Luke Skywalker

Following the fall of the Republic and Anakin's turn to the dark side, Obi-Wan Kenobi brought the newborn Luke to the homestead. Beru cradled the child as she and Owen looked out over the twin suns, beginning their life as his guardians.

~10 BBY - Raising Luke under the Empire

Beru helped bring up a young Luke on the farm, tempering Owen's insistence that the boy be kept ignorant of his heritage and hidden from Obi-Wan, who watched from a distance.

~10 BBY - Danger comes to the homestead

When the Inquisitor Reva hunted Luke to draw out Obi-Wan, Beru armed herself and fought to defend her nephew, holding the farm until Obi-Wan and Owen drove the threat off.

~0 BBY - A restless nephew

As Luke chafed at farm life and longed to leave for the Academy, Beru urged Owen to accept that the boy could not be kept on Tatooine forever.

0 BBY - Death at the Lars homestead

Imperial stormtroopers traced R2-D2 and C-3PO to the farm and killed Beru and Owen, torching the homestead. Luke returned to find their bodies and left Tatooine with Obi-Wan, ultimately joining the Rebel Alliance.

birth.source: Canon gives no birth year for Beru. The widely-cited 47 BBY comes from Legends material (the Legends Wookieepedia article and the old Databank/Star Wars Character Encyclopedia Legends entries), not from current canon.

death.source: Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope, which the canon timeline places in 0 BBY; Beru and Owen are killed by Imperial stormtroopers at the Lars homestead while searching for R2-D2 and C-3PO.

notes.suggested: Keep flagged as a guess; consider -47 to match the most commonly cited figure
notes.detail: Canon does not state Beru's birth year. 47 BBY is a Legends-era figure (Legends Wookieepedia / older Databank material). Your "guess" flag is the correct handling — just be aware there is no canon date to promote it to, and if you want the guess to align with the number fans will recognize, 47 BBY is that number.

notes.current: Includes "Episode III: Revenge of the Sith" and "Obi-Wan Kenobi"
notes.suggested: No change needed to those two — but note Revenge of the Sith is a silent, single-shot appearance
notes.detail: Flagging only for completeness: her Revenge of the Sith appearance is the closing twin-suns shot with no dialogue. All four listed appearances are real and correct; no removals needed.

notes.suggested: Add a family/relations fact, e.g. "Spouse: Owen Lars" and "Ward: Luke Skywalker"
notes.detail: Her entire significance is relational — wife of Owen, aunt/guardian of Luke, daughter-in-law by marriage into the Lars family. The timeline shows none of that in metadata even though the description depends on it.

notes.suggested: Consider "Beru Whitesun Lars", with altTitle "Beru Whitesun"
notes.detail: Her full canon name (and the Wookieepedia article title you already link) is Beru Whitesun Lars; her maiden name Whitesun is used in canon and would be a natural altTitle, which is currently empty.

notes.current: Existing paragraph ends at her death and Luke leaving Tatooine
notes.suggested: Mention her role in the Obi-Wan Kenobi series
notes.detail: The current description predates the 2022 Obi-Wan Kenobi series, where Beru has a substantial role — including arming herself to defend Luke from Reva — even though you already list that series in seenIn. The prose and the appearance list are out of sync.

Verification caveat for the maintainer: Wookieepedia (starwars.fandom.com) returned HTTP 402 to every fetch attempt during this pass, so the canon article's infobox could not be read directly. The death year (0 BBY, A New Hope) is solid from the film itself; the absence of a canon birth year is based on the fact that every retrievable citation of 47 BBY traces to Legends sources. Worth a re-check when the article is reachable.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/C1-10P.description: C1-10P, better known as "Chopper" or simply "Chop," is a cantankerous masculine C1-series astromech droid built by Industrial Automaton and one of the longest-serving members of the Ghost crew. Originally a navigational astromech in the Galactic Republic Navy during the Clone Wars, he was shot down over Ryloth in a Y-wing and salvaged by a young Hera Syndulla, who rebuilt him from mismatched parts — the origin of his patchwork frame and famously abrasive personality. Chopper aided the Free Ryloth movement, then became Hera's inseparable companion aboard the VCX-100 light freighter Ghost, maintaining the ship and serving as gunner, slicer, saboteur and reluctant undercover agent for the Spectres rebel cell alongside Kanan Jarrus, Ezra Bridger, Garazeb Orrelios and Sabine Wren. Despite a well-earned reputation for pranks, grumbling and outright malice toward strangers, he proved decisive again and again — infiltrating Imperial facilities, flying stolen craft, and surviving the rebellion's hardest losses. The Rebel Alliance's Atollon headquarters, Chopper Base, was named in his honor. He fought through the liberation of Lothal, was present at Yavin 4 during the Scarif crisis and at the Battle of Endor, later joined Sabine Wren's long search for the missing Ezra Bridger, and remained at Hera's side into the New Republic era.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/C1-10P.timeline:
~22 BBY - Built by Industrial Automaton

C1-10P is manufactured as a C1-series astromech droid; canon never states his construction date, only that he was in service by the Clone Wars.

~22 BBY - Service with the Republic Navy

Chopper serves as a navigational astromech aboard Republic starfighters during the Clone Wars.

~20 BBY - Shot down over Ryloth

His Y-wing is shot down during the fighting on Ryloth. Badly damaged and abandoned in the wreck, he is found by a young Hera Syndulla, who repairs him with whatever parts she can scavenge.

~20 BBY - Joins the Free Ryloth movement

Chopper becomes Hera's constant companion, assisting the Twi'lek resistance against Separatist and later Imperial occupation.

~19 BBY - Aiding Hera against the early Empire

In the first year of the Galactic Empire, Chopper helps Hera on Ryloth during operations against Imperial forces occupying the world, including efforts to free captured members of the Syndulla family.

~11 BBY - Crew of the Ghost

Chopper serves aboard Hera's VCX-100 light freighter Ghost as she meets Kanan Jarrus during the events on Gorse, becoming the ship's permanent — and permanently grumpy — maintenance droid.

5 BBY - The Spectres on Lothal

Chopper is part of the rebel cell that harasses Imperial operations on Lothal and takes in the young street thief Ezra Bridger, flying missions, slicing Imperial systems and pulling pranks on the crew throughout.

4 BBY - Phoenix Squadron

The Spectres join the wider rebel network; Chopper takes part in supply raids, rescues and infiltrations, often disguised or posing as an Imperial droid.

3 BBY - Chopper Base

The rebels establish a headquarters on Atollon, nicknamed Chopper Base in his honor after he helps clear the site. He survives the Empire's devastating assault there.

2 BBY - Undercover and in the field

Chopper repeatedly infiltrates Imperial installations, including a stint aboard an Imperial vessel where his rough manners nearly expose the crew.

1 BBY - Liberation of Lothal

Chopper takes part in the final campaign to free Lothal, aboard the Ghost as Ezra Bridger vanishes into hyperspace with Grand Admiral Thrawn and the purrgil.

0 BBY - Yavin 4

Chopper is present at the Rebel Alliance's Massassi base on Yavin 4 during the crisis that leads to the theft of the Death Star plans at Scarif.

4 ABY - Battle of Endor

Chopper serves aboard the Ghost as the Alliance fleet destroys the second Death Star and breaks the Empire.

~5 ABY - Searching for Ezra

After the war, Chopper accompanies Sabine Wren as she sets out to find the missing Ezra Bridger.

~9 ABY - The New Republic era

Still at Hera Syndulla's side, now a New Republic general, Chopper assists in the effort to track Thrawn's return and recover Ezra, remaining active and unmistakably cantankerous.

birth.source: No canon source states Chopper's construction date. StarWars.com Databank calls him "a beat-up, patched together astromech droid dating back to the Clone Wars"; the only firm bound is that he was manufactured by Industrial Automaton and was already in Republic Navy service by the Clone Wars (22-19 BBY), so he was built by 22 BBY at the latest. (Wookieepedia's C1-10P article was unreachable during this check - HTTP 402 - so the infobox could not be read directly; a widely-repeated '32 BBY' figure appears only on unofficial mirror wikis and is not sourced to canon.)

death.source: Chopper does not die in canon. He is last seen alive in the Ahsoka series (c. 9 ABY) serving with Hera Syndulla and the New Republic.

notes.suggested: Remove, or use "Unknown (manufactured by Industrial Automaton)"
notes.detail: Lasan is Garazeb Orrelios's homeworld, not Chopper's. Canon never states where Chopper was built; he is a C1-series astromech from Industrial Automaton and was first encountered on Ryloth. This looks like a data-entry bleed from the Zeb entry.

notes.suggested: Add Manufacturer: Industrial Automaton; Model: C1-series astromech droid; Class: Astromech; Gender: Masculine programming
notes.detail: For droids these are the meaningful identity facts, and they are all firmly established in canon (Rebels, Databank).

notes.suggested: Keep the guess flag; ~22 BBY or earlier is the defensible bound
notes.detail: Canon gives no construction date. The only hard constraint is that he was serving the Republic Navy during the Clone Wars (22-19 BBY), so he existed by 22 BBY and plausibly well before. 30 BBY is a reasonable rendering position but should stay flagged; the '32 BBY' figure circulating on non-Wookieepedia fan wikis has no canon source. Note: Wookieepedia's C1-10P article returned HTTP 402 during this verification, so the infobox could not be read first-hand.

notes.suggested: Keep as-is
notes.detail: Correct handling - Chopper never dies in canon and is alive c. 9 ABY in Ahsoka. Worth noting he is one of the few characters whose column genuinely should be open-ended.

notes.current: The Bad Batch, Star Wars Rebels, Rogue One, Ahsoka
notes.suggested: Consider adding Star Wars: Forces of Destiny (and note the novel A New Dawn, 11 BBY, if you track books)
notes.detail: The listed four are all real appearances (Bad Batch S2 Ryloth arc; Rogue One cameo on Yavin 4 in 0 BBY, where he and Hera are paged over the base PA). Chopper also appears in the animated shorts Forces of Destiny and in the canon novel A New Dawn, which is his earliest post-Clone Wars appearance with Hera.

notes.current: "...active during the Clone Wars and the early years of the Galactic Empire"
notes.suggested: Extend through the Galactic Civil War and into the New Republic era
notes.detail: The stored description stops at the early Empire and predates Rogue One/Ahsoka. Chopper is active at Endor (4 ABY) and still serving with Hera around 9 ABY in Ahsoka.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Fennec_.description: Fennec Shand is a human female bounty hunter, elite mercenary and master assassin who built a fearsome reputation working for the galaxy's top crime syndicates, known for her marksmanship with a long-range sniper rifle, her cunning, and her willingness to change allegiance when the payout justifies it. She first rose to prominence in the years just after the Clone Wars, when she was hired to track the young clone Omega — a hunt that took her from Pantora across the galaxy and repeatedly pitted her against Clone Force 99, though she ultimately proved a more ambiguous figure than a straightforward villain in that pursuit. Decades later, during the New Republic era, she was hunted on Tatooine by the novice bounty hunter Toro Calican and the Mandalorian Din Djarin and left for dead in the Dune Sea, only to be recovered and rebuilt with cybernetic implants by Boba Fett, to whom she then pledged her loyalty. As Fett's partner she helped him reclaim Jabba the Hutt's palace and install himself as daimyo of Mos Espa, fought in the war against the Pyke Syndicate, and joined Din Djarin's crew in the rescue of Grogu from Moff Gideon's cruiser. She is not known to have died in canon.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Fennec_.timeline:
Unknown - Birth

Canon does not record when or where Fennec Shand was born. By the end of the Clone Wars she was already an experienced operator, so any birth year on the timeline is an estimate for positioning only.

~19 BBY - Hired to hunt Omega

In the first months of the Galactic Empire, Shand was engaged by Kaminoan scientist Nala Se to recover the young clone Omega, beginning a long pursuit of the child and her protectors in Clone Force 99.

~19 BBY - Betrayal on Pantora

Shand won Omega's trust on Pantora before turning on her, fighting off Wrecker and chasing the girl across the city rooftops. Omega escaped back to Hunter, and Shand vowed to continue the hunt.

~19 BBY - Pursuit across the Outer Rim

She continued tracking Clone Force 99, at one point capturing Omega and delivering her toward Kamino, while quietly withholding information about the girl's true value from other interested parties.

~18 BBY - The Bad Batch's final campaign

Working alongside Nala Se's interests, Shand remained entangled with Omega's fate through the Empire's cloning program, ultimately aiding rather than opposing the effort to free the clones from Imperial custody.

9 ABY - Hunted on Tatooine

Now a wanted assassin with bounties from every major syndicate, Shand was tracked across the Dune Sea by the rookie bounty hunter Toro Calican and Din Djarin. Calican shot her and left her for dead in the sand.

9 ABY - Rebuilt by Boba Fett

A mysterious armored figure — Boba Fett — found her dying body and had her wounds replaced with cybernetics, saving her life. In return she pledged herself to Fett's service.

9 ABY - Recovery of Boba Fett's armor

Shand accompanied Fett to Tython, where he bargained with Din Djarin for the return of his Mandalorian armor and then covered the Mandalorian in the fight against Imperial forces.

9 ABY - Rescue of Grogu

She joined Din Djarin's assembled crew — Boba Fett, Cara Dune and Bo-Katan Kryze — in boarding Moff Gideon's light cruiser to free the child Grogu from Imperial captivity.

9 ABY - Seizing Jabba's palace

With Fett, Shand stormed the Hutt palace on Tatooine and helped install him as the new daimyo of Mos Espa, serving as his enforcer and chief strategist.

9 ABY - War with the Pyke Syndicate

Shand fought in the street battle for Mos Espa against Pyke forces alongside Fett, Din Djarin and the Mods, and afterward eliminated the crime bosses who had betrayed them.

After 9 ABY - Fate unknown

Shand survives every appearance recorded in canon to date; no death has been depicted, and her column on the timeline is open-ended rather than terminated by a death year.

notes.suggested: keep as a guess
notes.detail: Canon gives no birth year, age or birthplace for Fennec Shand; the databank entry and every screen source are silent. Flagging it as a guess is the correct handling — do not promote it to a known date. If anything, 39 BBY makes her ~48 in The Mandalorian (9 ABY) and ~20 during The Bad Batch, which reads young for how established an operator she already is in 19 BBY; a birth in the mid-to-late 40s BBY would fit better, but neither is canon.

notes.suggested: no change
notes.detail: Correct — she survives all known appearances. Her apparent death in The Mandalorian 'Chapter 5: The Gunslinger' is undone at the end of that episode when Boba Fett recovers her; she should never be recorded as dying in 9 ABY.

notes.current: Description covers only the Pantora incident from The Bad Batch and ends with 'vowed to continue her hunt for the girl.'
notes.suggested: Use the new full-career summary
notes.detail: The existing text is a single-episode plot recap of The Bad Batch and omits her entire Mandalorian / Book of Boba Fett arc, her cybernetic reconstruction, and her role as Boba Fett's partner and enforcer on Tatooine — which is what she is best known for.

notes.suggested: Species: Human (cyborg from 9 ABY)
notes.detail: After being gut-shot on Tatooine in 9 ABY, Boba Fett had her rebuilt with a cybernetic abdominal replacement; her cyborg status is a defining trait post-Mandalorian season 1.

Sourcing caveat: the Wookieepedia article (starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Fennec_Shand) could not be retrieved during this pass — the host returned HTTP 402 to every fetch attempt. Verification here rests on the official StarWars.com databank entry plus the episodes themselves. The date conclusions (no canon birth year, no death) are robust, but a maintainer should re-check the Wookieepedia infobox if it later becomes reachable, in case a reference book has since supplied an age.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Mon_Mot.description: Mon Mothma is a human politician from Chandrila who rose from the Galactic Senate to become the founding leader of the Alliance to Restore the Republic and the first Chancellor of the New Republic. She came to prominence during the Separatist Crisis and Clone Wars as an outspoken advocate for peace and a critic of Supreme Chancellor Palpatine's accumulation of executive power, allied with senators such as Bail Organa and Padmé Amidala. Under the Empire she publicly served as the Chandrilan senator while secretly financing and organizing the growing rebellion — a double life dramatized in the series Andor — before openly breaking with the Empire to lead the Rebel Alliance. Her leadership guided the destruction of both Death Stars and the fall of the Empire at Endor, after which she established the New Republic and served as its Chancellor. She remains one of the most enduring figures of the galaxy's transition from Republic to Empire to Republic, and no canon work depicts her death.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Mon_Mot.timeline:
~48 BBY - Birth on Chandrila

Mon Mothma is born into a prominent political family on the planet Chandrila. Wookieepedia places her birth around 48 BBY, derived from canon references to her age when elected to the Senate.

~32 BBY - Elected to the Galactic Senate

At roughly sixteen years of age, Mothma becomes one of the youngest senators to represent Chandrila in the Galactic Senate, beginning a decades-long political career.

~24 BBY - Marriage to Perrin Fertha

Around this time Mothma enters an arranged Chandrilan marriage to Perrin Fertha, a union that later becomes politically and personally strained as her covert activities grow.

22 BBY - The Separatist Crisis and Clone Wars

As the Republic fractures and war breaks out with the Confederacy of Independent Systems, Mothma emerges as a leading voice for peace and diplomacy alongside Senators Bail Organa and Padmé Amidala.

~19 BBY - Opposing Palpatine's power

Mothma joins senators pushing back against the Supreme Chancellor's expanding emergency powers, laying the groundwork for organized political resistance as the Clone Wars end and the Empire is declared.

~5 BBY - Secret financier of the rebellion

Publicly serving as Chandrila's senator in the Imperial Senate, Mothma secretly funnels funds and coordinates disparate rebel cells, navigating Imperial surveillance while maintaining a respectable political facade.

~1 BBY - Break with the Empire

Her covert activities exposed and her position untenable, Mothma delivers a defiant speech against Imperial atrocities and flees the Senate to openly commit to armed rebellion.

0 BBY - Leading the Rebel Alliance

As a founder and leader of the Alliance to Restore the Republic, Mothma helps direct the effort to obtain the Death Star plans and endorses the mission that leads to the Battle of Scarif.

4 ABY - Fall of the Empire

Mothma leads the Alliance through the campaign culminating in the Battle of Endor and the destruction of the second Death Star and the death of the Emperor.

~4 ABY - Founding the New Republic

With the Empire in retreat, Mothma proclaims the restoration of the Republic and becomes the first Chancellor of the New Republic, moving the seat of government to Chandrila.

~9 ABY - Chancellor of the New Republic

Mothma continues to serve as Chancellor, guiding the fledgling New Republic, and appears in this role during the events of the Ahsoka era.

birth.source: Wookieepedia dates her birth to ~48 BBY, derived from Andor (she was 16 when elected senator) cross-referenced with Queen's Shadow (elected ~4 years before that novel's 28 BBY setting, i.e. ~32 BBY at age 16 → born ~48 BBY). An older source, Rogue One: The Ultimate Visual Guide, gives 46 BBY.
