death.source: Mon Mothma does not die in any canon work. She is alive in her latest chronological appearance, the Ahsoka series (~9 ABY), still serving as New Republic Chancellor. No canon death year exists.

notes.suggested: 48 BBY (estimated)
notes.detail: Automatic check: birth year in data.json disagrees with the year Claude verified. Wookieepedia dates her birth to ~48 BBY, derived from Andor (she was 16 when elected senator) cross-referenced with Queen's Shadow (elected ~4 years before that novel's 28 BBY setting, i.e. ~32 BBY at age 16 → born ~48 BBY). An older source, Rogue One: The Ultimate Visual Guide, gives 46 BBY.

notes.suggested: ~48 BBY (flag as estimated: set startYearUnknown true)
notes.detail: Current canon (Wookieepedia) places Mon Mothma's birth around 48 BBY, derived from Andor establishing she was 16 when elected senator and Queen's Shadow dating that election to ~32 BBY. The 46 BBY we present as fact comes only from the older Rogue One: The Ultimate Visual Guide. Because the year is derived, it should not be shown as a hard known date.

Two canon figures conflict: ~48 BBY (narrative derivation from Andor + Queen's Shadow, favored by Wookieepedia) vs 46 BBY (Rogue One: The Ultimate Visual Guide). Worth flagging the conflict rather than asserting either as definitive.

notes.current: Homeworld Chandrila, Species Human, Force Sensitive No
notes.suggested: optionally add Gender: Female
notes.detail: All listed metadata is correct. She is a human female Chandrilan; species, homeworld, and non-Force-sensitivity all check out.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Bail_Pr.description: Bail Prestor Organa was a human male from Alderaan who served as his world's senator, Viceroy and First Chairman, and became one of the founding architects of the rebellion against the Galactic Empire. A principled voice for diplomacy in the Galactic Senate during the twilight of the Republic, he sat on the Loyalist Committee alongside Padmé Amidala and Mon Mothma, resisted Chancellor Palpatine's steady accumulation of emergency powers throughout the Clone Wars, and was among the signatories of the Petition of the Two Thousand. He witnessed the execution of Order 66 firsthand at the Jedi Temple, helped Obi-Wan Kenobi and Yoda escape into hiding, and afterward adopted Padmé's newborn daughter Leia, raising her as a princess of Alderaan with his wife, Queen Breha Organa. Over the next two decades he used Alderaan's wealth and his own senatorial cover to fund and shelter dissident cells — supporting the Lothal rebels, recruiting Ahsoka Tano as an intelligence coordinator under the codename Fulcrum, and working with Mon Mothma to knit scattered insurgencies into the Alliance to Restore the Republic. He was killed in 0 BBY when the Death Star destroyed Alderaan, shortly after sending Leia to Tatooine to find Obi-Wan Kenobi.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Bail_Pr.timeline:
~67 BBY - Birth on Alderaan

Bail Prestor Organa is born into Alderaan's ruling House of Organa. Canon never states the year outright; 67 BBY comes from reference-book material and should be read as an estimate.

~32 BBY - Enters the Galactic Senate

Organa takes up Alderaan's seat in the Galactic Senate around the time of the Naboo crisis, beginning decades of service as a reformist and advocate of diplomacy.

22 BBY - The Loyalist Committee and the Military Creation Act

As separatist pressure mounts, Organa serves on Chancellor Palpatine's Loyalist Committee with Padmé Amidala and Mas Amedda, and is present in the Chancellor's office when emergency powers are granted and the Clone Wars begin.

22-19 BBY - The Clone Wars

Throughout the war Organa leads humanitarian relief efforts, negotiates for neutral worlds such as Mandalore and Christophsis, and works with Amidala and Mon Mothma to oppose bills expanding military spending and executive authority.

19 BBY - The Petition of the Two Thousand

Organa joins Amidala, Mon Mothma and other senators in formally demanding that Palpatine return his wartime powers to the Senate — an act that plants the seed of the future Rebel Alliance.

19 BBY - Order 66 and the fall of the Republic

Organa rides to the burning Jedi Temple and narrowly escapes clone troopers, then rescues Yoda and Obi-Wan Kenobi aboard the Tantive IV. He hears Palpatine proclaim the Galactic Empire before the applauding Senate.

19 BBY - Adopting Leia

After Padmé Amidala dies giving birth on Polis Massa, Organa takes the infant Leia home to Alderaan, where he and Breha raise her as their daughter and heir, concealing her true parentage.

~18-10 BBY - Quiet resistance

Serving the Imperial Senate as a loyal-seeming statesman, Organa funnels Alderaanian resources to displaced peoples, shelters hunted Jedi and dissidents, and quietly seeks out insurgent groups.

~9 BBY - Leia's kidnapping and Obi-Wan's return

When ten-year-old Leia is abducted by mercenaries working for the Inquisitorius, Organa calls on Obi-Wan Kenobi to recover her, and is later forced to send a desperate transmission to Tatooine as the hunt closes in on his family.

~5-2 BBY - Building the Alliance

Organa recruits Ahsoka Tano to coordinate rebel intelligence as Fulcrum, supplies the Phoenix Cell and the Lothal rebels, and works with Mon Mothma to unify scattered cells into the Alliance to Restore the Republic.

0 BBY - Yavin 4 and the mission to Kenobi

With news of the Death Star confirmed, Organa breaks openly with the Empire at the Alliance council on Yavin 4, pledges Alderaan's support, and sends Leia to Tatooine to bring Obi-Wan Kenobi into the war.

0 BBY - Death in the destruction of Alderaan

Grand Moff Tarkin orders the Death Star to destroy Alderaan as a demonstration of Imperial power. Bail and Breha Organa die with their world and its people.

birth.source: Wookieepedia's Bail Prestor Organa article and mirrors give 67 BBY, a figure that traces to reference-book material (The Official Star Wars Fandom/Fact File lineage) rather than to a film or series. No canon film, show or novel states his birth year outright, so 67 BBY is best treated as an approximation. Keeping startYearUnknown: true is correct.

death.source: Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope — Grand Moff Tarkin destroys Alderaan with the Death Star in 0 BBY, killing Bail and Breha Organa along with the planet's population; confirmed by the StarWars.com Databank entry for Bail Organa and Wookieepedia.

notes.suggested: Keep -67 and keep the unknown flag
notes.detail: 67 BBY is the figure Wookieepedia and its mirrors carry, but it derives from reference-book/Fact File material rather than any canon film, series or novel. No canon work states Bail's birth year or age. Flagging it as a guess is the correct call — worth an internal comment so nobody 'upgrades' it to known later.

notes.current: Attack of the Clones, The Clone Wars, Revenge of the Sith, Rebels, Obi-Wan Kenobi, Rogue One
notes.suggested: Add Star Wars: The Bad Batch
notes.detail: Senator Bail Organa is a recurring speaking character in The Bad Batch (voiced by Phil LaMarr), notably aiding Omega and the squad and appearing in the Senate storyline around 18 BBY. This is a real gap in the appearance list.

notes.suggested: Add Titles: Senator of Alderaan / Viceroy and First Chairman; Family: wife Breha Organa, adopted daughter Leia Organa
notes.detail: Bail's defining relationships — Breha and Leia — are the single most useful facts about him for a timeline reader and are currently untracked. His viceroy/first chairman title also distinguishes him from an ordinary senator.

notes.suggested: Keep, optionally note the cause: destruction of Alderaan by the Death Star
notes.detail: The death year 0 BBY is correct and confirmed on screen, but the entry doesn't record that he dies off-screen in the Alderaan detonation rather than in any depicted scene — useful context if the UI shows a cause of death.

notes.current: '...believed that a full-scale Galactic Civil War was inevitable if the Empire was to be toppled and replaced by a New Republic.'
notes.suggested: Mention his adoption and raising of Leia Organa
notes.detail: The existing description omits the single thing Bail is most known for in the saga: adopting Padmé's daughter Leia in 19 BBY and raising her as a princess of Alderaan. It also omits his role in rescuing Yoda and Obi-Wan after Order 66.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Jar_Jar.description: Jar Jar Binks was a Gungan from the swamps of Naboo whose bumbling good intentions repeatedly placed him at the center of galactic history. Banished from Otoh Gunga for his clumsiness, he was rescued by Jedi Master Qui-Gon Jinn during the Trade Federation's invasion of Naboo in 32 BBY and served as the guide who brought the Jedi and Queen Padmé Amidala to his people, brokering the Gungan-Naboo alliance that broke the occupation; he was promoted to Bombad General for his accidental heroics at the Battle of Naboo. Elevated to Junior Representative for Naboo in the Galactic Senate, he was serving in Senator Amidala's place in 22 BBY when he proposed granting Supreme Chancellor Palpatine emergency powers — the motion that created the Grand Army of the Republic and set the Republic on the path to becoming the Galactic Empire. Through the Clone Wars he undertook improbable missions to Rodia, Aleen, Bardotta and his own homeworld, often paired with Jedi such as Anakin Skywalker, Obi-Wan Kenobi and Mace Windu, and he was among the mourners at Padmé Amidala's funeral in 19 BBY. Under the Empire he faded from politics, and by 5 ABY he was living in Theed as a shunned clown and street performer, entertaining refugee children while adults blamed him for the Empire's rise.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Jar_Jar.timeline:
~52 BBY - Birth on Naboo

Jar Jar Binks is born a Gungan on the planet Naboo. Canon never states a birth year; the commonly quoted 52 BBY is a Legends-era figure and should be treated as an estimate.

Before 32 BBY - Exile from Otoh Gunga

His clumsiness and repeated accidents lead Boss Rugor Nass to banish him from the Gungan city of Otoh Gunga, leaving him to scavenge alone in the Naboo swamps.

32 BBY - Meeting Qui-Gon Jinn

During the Trade Federation invasion, Jar Jar is nearly crushed by an invasion transport and saved by Qui-Gon Jinn, whom he pledges a life debt to and guides, with Obi-Wan Kenobi, to Otoh Gunga.

32 BBY - Journey to Tatooine and Coruscant

Traveling with Queen Padmé Amidala's party, he is stranded on Tatooine, where the group meets young Anakin Skywalker, before continuing to Coruscant to petition the Senate.

32 BBY - Brokering the Gungan-Naboo alliance

Returning to Naboo, Jar Jar leads the Queen to the Gungan sacred place, where Amidala's plea unites the two peoples against the Trade Federation.

32 BBY - Battle of Naboo and promotion to Bombad General

Named a general by Boss Nass, he blunders his way through the Gungan Grand Army's clash with the droid army on the Great Grass Plains, contributing to the liberation of Naboo.

Between 32 and 22 BBY - Junior Representative in the Senate

Jar Jar becomes Naboo's Junior Representative in the Galactic Senate, serving alongside Padmé Amidala after she leaves the throne to become senator.

22 BBY - Motion granting Palpatine emergency powers

Standing in for Amidala after an assassination attempt, he moves that the Senate grant Supreme Chancellor Palpatine emergency powers, enabling the creation of the clone army and the outbreak of the Clone Wars.

22 BBY - Mission to Rodia

Mistaken for a Jedi while accompanying Amidala on Rodia, "Bombad Jedi" Jar Jar helps free her from Nute Gunray's grasp and keeps the Rodians from defecting to the Separatists.

22 BBY - Clone Wars campaigns

He serves in a string of unlikely wartime episodes, including the rescue of Kit Fisto's forces alongside Obi-Wan Kenobi and the effort to stop Nuvo Vindi's Blue Shadow Virus on Naboo.

21 BBY - Relief mission to Aleen

Sent with clone commander and droids C-3PO and R2-D2 on a humanitarian mission to the quake-stricken world of Aleen, Jar Jar helps restore the planet's balance.

20 BBY - The Naboo doppelganger plot

When the Separatist scientist Rish Loo manipulates Boss Lyonie to turn the Gungans against Theed, Jar Jar impersonates the Boss and rallies the Gungan army back to Naboo's side.

20 BBY - Mission to Bardotta

At the personal request of Queen Julia, with whom he shares a romance, Jar Jar and Mace Windu investigate the disappearance of the Bardottan Dagoyan Masters and stop the Frangawl Cult.

19 BBY - Padmé Amidala's funeral

After the fall of the Republic and the proclamation of the Galactic Empire, Jar Jar walks in the funeral procession for Padmé Amidala in Theed.

19 BBY to 5 ABY - Outcast under the Empire

Blamed by many for the Empire's rise and shunned once more by his own people, he leaves politics behind and lives quietly on Naboo.

5 ABY - Clown of Theed

Performing as a clown in the Plaza of the Catalan, Jar Jar befriends Mapo, a boy burned and orphaned by Imperial bombing, and agrees to train him as a performer — the last canon glimpse of his life.

birth.source: Canon sources never state Jar Jar Binks' birth year. The frequently cited 52 BBY comes from Legends-era reference material (e.g. the old Star Wars Encyclopedia/databank lineage), not current canon. Canon only establishes he is an adult Gungan by the Invasion of Naboo in 32 BBY.

death.source: No canon death. He is alive in 5 ABY in the canon novel Star Wars: Aftermath: Empire's End (Chuck Wendig), living as an outcast street performer in Theed.

notes.suggested: ~52 BBY (still flagged as a guess)
notes.detail: Canon never gives Jar Jar a birth year, so keeping startYearUnknown: true is correct. However, the long-standing Legends figure is 52 BBY, and using that rather than 60 BBY keeps the column consistent with the number most fans and reference works cite. Either way it must stay flagged as a guess.

notes.suggested: 5 (5 ABY), endYearUnknown: true
notes.detail: The canon novel Star Wars: Aftermath: Empire's End (Chuck Wendig, 2017) shows Jar Jar alive in 5 ABY on Naboo, working as a street-performing clown in Theed and taking on the burned refugee boy Mapo as an apprentice. The 8 BBY cutoff understates his documented lifespan by 13 years; the column should run at least to 5 ABY. He has no canon death.

notes.current: Episode I, Episode II, The Clone Wars (TV series), Episode III
notes.suggested: Add a note or entry for Aftermath: Empire's End (novel)
notes.detail: His only post-Imperial canon appearance is in a novel rather than a film or series. If the timeline only plots screen media, that is fine, but the 5 ABY event is worth surfacing since it is the character's canonical epilogue.

notes.current: Describes him only through the Invasion of Naboo and his Senate role
notes.detail: The existing summary omits his single most historically consequential act: proposing that the Senate grant Palpatine emergency powers in 22 BBY, which created the clone army and enabled the Republic's fall. It also omits his post-Empire fate as an ostracized street performer.
notes.suggested: Add the emergency-powers motion and the 5 ABY Theed epilogue.
Add "Titles: Bombad General, Junior Representative of Naboo"
notes.detail: Both are canon ranks he held (granted by Boss Rugor Nass in 32 BBY and by Naboo's government before 22 BBY respectively) and are useful distinguishing facts for the entry.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Luke_Sk.description: Luke Skywalker was a legendary human Jedi Master, hero of the Rebel Alliance, and a central figure in the Galactic Civil War against the Galactic Empire. Born in 19 BBY as the son of Anakin Skywalker (Darth Vader) and Padmé Amidala, and the twin brother of Leia Organa, he was raised in obscurity as a moisture farmer on Tatooine. Drawn into the conflict after acquiring the droids R2-D2 and C-3PO, he trained under Obi-Wan Kenobi and Yoda, destroyed the first Death Star at the Battle of Yavin, confronted his father, and ultimately redeemed Anakin at the Battle of Endor, bringing about the Emperor's downfall. In the decades that followed he sought to restore the Jedi Order, but the fall of his nephew Ben Solo to the dark side drove him into self-imposed exile on Ahch-To. He re-emerged during the war against the First Order, aiding the Resistance at Crait through an extraordinary Force projection before becoming one with the Force in 34 ABY. Remembered as one of the greatest Jedi in galactic history, his legend endured and later inspired a new generation.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Luke_Sk.timeline:
19 BBY - Birth on Polis Massa

Luke is born to Padmé Amidala and Anakin Skywalker shortly after the end of the Clone Wars, moments after his twin sister Leia. Separated from his sister to hide them from their fallen father, he is taken by Obi-Wan Kenobi to Tatooine.

19 BBY - Raised on Tatooine

Luke is placed in the care of his step-relatives Owen and Beru Lars, growing up as a moisture farmer while Obi-Wan watches over him from a distance.

0 BBY - Meeting the droids and Obi-Wan

Acquiring R2-D2 and C-3PO, Luke discovers Leia's plea for help, meets Obi-Wan Kenobi, receives his father's lightsaber, and leaves Tatooine after the Empire murders his aunt and uncle.

0 BBY - Destruction of the Death Star

Aided by the Force and Han Solo, Luke fires the shot that destroys the first Death Star at the Battle of Yavin, becoming a hero of the Rebel Alliance.

3 ABY - Training with Yoda

After the Rebel defeat on Hoth, Luke travels to Dagobah to train as a Jedi under Master Yoda. At Cloud City he duels Darth Vader, loses his hand, and learns that Vader is his father.

4 ABY - Redemption of Anakin at Endor

Luke surrenders to Vader and confronts Emperor Palpatine aboard the second Death Star. Refusing the dark side, he turns his father back to the light; Anakin destroys the Emperor, and Luke witnesses the Rebel victory at the Battle of Endor.

~28 ABY - Rebuilding the Jedi Order

In the years after the war, Luke works to restore the Jedi, training a new generation of apprentices, including his nephew Ben Solo.

~28 ABY - Fall of the temple and exile

After a moment of fear and Ben's turn to the dark side, Luke's temple is destroyed and his students scattered. Blaming himself, Luke exiles himself to the ancient Jedi world of Ahch-To.

34 ABY - Rey and the last lesson

Rey arrives on Ahch-To seeking Luke's help. Reluctant at first, he is eventually persuaded by Yoda's spirit to pass on the Jedi legacy.

34 ABY - Sacrifice at Crait and death

Luke projects himself across the galaxy to confront Kylo Ren and buy time for the Resistance to escape Crait. The immense effort drains him, and he peacefully becomes one with the Force on Ahch-To.

35 ABY - Return as a Force spirit

Luke appears to Rey as a Force ghost, lifting his old X-wing from the sea and encouraging her to face Palpatine, aiding the final victory over the Sith.

birth.source: Wookieepedia 'Luke Skywalker' — born 19 BBY on Polis Massa, twin of Leia, shortly after Empire Day / the end of the Clone Wars.

death.source: Wookieepedia 'Luke Skywalker' — died 34 ABY on Ahch-To, becoming one with the Force after projecting himself across the galaxy to Crait (Episode VIII: The Last Jedi).

notes.current: Episode IX: The Rise of Skywalker
notes.suggested: Episode VIII: The Last Jedi
notes.detail: Luke dies (becomes one with the Force) in The Last Jedi in 34 ABY after his Crait projection. In The Rise of Skywalker he appears only as a Force ghost, so the death-year event should point to The Last Jedi, not The Rise of Skywalker. The endYear of 34 ABY is correct.

notes.suggested: Tatooine (born on Polis Massa)
notes.detail: Luke's homeworld is reasonably listed as Tatooine (where he was raised), but he was actually born on the asteroid facility Polis Massa. Worth noting for accuracy; not necessarily a change to the displayed homeworld.

notes.suggested: Add Species detail / relations: son of Anakin Skywalker & Padmé Amidala, twin of Leia Organa
notes.detail: The entry tracks no family relations. Luke's parentage (Anakin/Padmé) and twin (Leia) are defining canon facts worth surfacing in metadata.

notes.current: SPOILER ALERT: Luke is the son of Anakin.
notes.suggested: Remove the informal 'SPOILER ALERT' line and General Han Solo phrasing
notes.detail: The old description is casual/dated (the 'SPOILER ALERT' aside, calling Han a 'General revolutionary'). A cleaner canon summary is provided in the description field.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Ki-Adi-.description: Ki-Adi-Mundi was a Cerean Jedi Master from the planet Cerea, distinguished by his species' towering binary brain and elongated cranium, who served on the Jedi High Council across an extraordinary span of galactic history — from the golden age of the High Republic through the fall of the Galactic Republic. He was already a seated Council member during the crisis on Brendok and the hunt for the Sith assassin Mae roughly a century before the Clone Wars, and he was still serving on the Council when Qui-Gon Jinn presented Anakin Skywalker to the Jedi after the Invasion of Naboo, famously insisting that the Sith had been extinct for a millennium. He fought in the Battle of Geonosis that opened the Clone Wars and became a Jedi General in the Grand Army of the Republic, leading clone forces at Geonosis's second campaign, Dantooine, Quell and elsewhere, and taking part in the Council's deepening suspicion of Chancellor Palpatine. Ki-Adi-Mundi died in 19 BBY on the crystalline world of Mygeeto, shot down by the Galactic Marines he commanded when Palpatine issued Order 66 and began the Great Jedi Purge.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Ki-Adi-.timeline:
Unknown date - Birth on Cerea

Ki-Adi-Mundi was born on Cerea, a world of the Mid Rim. Canon has never established the year; he was already an adult Jedi Master more than a century before the Clone Wars.

132 BBY - Serving on the Jedi High Council during the High Republic

Mundi sat on the Jedi High Council on Coruscant during the final century of the High Republic, taking part in the Order's response to the murders of Jedi Master Indara and others and the investigation into the fugitive Mae.

132 BBY - The Brendok inquiry

He questioned Master Sol and the Council's handling of the events surrounding the witches of Brendok, and was among those who debated how much to disclose to the Senate as the Order's public standing came under strain.

32 BBY - The Naboo crisis and the return of the Sith

As a Council member, Mundi heard Qui-Gon Jinn's report of a Sith attacker on Tatooine and declared it impossible, insisting the Sith had been extinct for a millennium. He was present when the Council reluctantly permitted Anakin Skywalker's training after Qui-Gon's death.

22 BBY - Battle of Geonosis

Mundi joined Mace Windu's task force to rescue Obi-Wan Kenobi, Anakin Skywalker and Padmé Amidala on Geonosis, surviving the arena battle that ignited the Clone Wars.

22 BBY - Jedi General of the Grand Army

Commissioned as a Jedi General, Mundi took command of clone troopers — later the Galactic Marines under Commander Bacara — and led Republic offensives against the Separatist Alliance.

22–20 BBY - Campaigns of the Clone Wars

He fought across the galaxy during the war, including the second Geonosis campaign against Poggle the Lesser's droid foundries, actions on Dantooine and Quell, and the defense of Republic worlds against Separatist incursions.

19 BBY - Council suspicions of the Chancellor

In the war's final year Mundi joined Council debate over Palpatine's refusal to relinquish emergency powers and the growing conviction that the Sith Lord controlled the Senate.

19 BBY - Death on Mygeeto

Leading the Galactic Marines through the crystalline cityscape of Mygeeto, Mundi was cut down by Commander Bacara and his own clone troopers the moment Palpatine issued Order 66, becoming one of the first casualties of the Great Jedi Purge.

birth.source: Canon has never given Ki-Adi-Mundi a birth year. The often-cited 93 BBY (and the 92 BBY Topps card figure) comes solely from Legends material invalidated in 2014. The only hard canon constraint is that he is a grown Jedi Master seated on the High Council in The Acolyte (132 BBY), so he must have been born well before 132 BBY — long-lived even by Cerean standards.

death.source: Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith — Mundi is gunned down by Commander Bacara and the Galactic Marines on Mygeeto when Order 66 is issued in 19 BBY.

notes.suggested: Keep -172 but keep the guess flag; there is no canon birth year
notes.detail: Canon has never stated Ki-Adi-Mundi's birth year. The widely circulated 93 BBY (and a 92 BBY Topps card) are Legends-only and were invalidated in 2014. His appearance as a seated Council Master in The Acolyte (132 BBY) means he must have been born well before 132 BBY, so a guess in the 170s BBY is defensible — but it is genuinely a guess, and your flag is correctly set. Do not let anyone 'correct' it to 93 BBY.

notes.current: Describes him as 'a Jedi who was active during the High Republic' and a Council member in the Republic's last decades
notes.suggested: Note explicitly that he sat on the Jedi High Council as early as 132 BBY and was among the longest-serving Council members in canon
notes.detail: The Acolyte establishes him on the Council roughly a century before The Phantom Menace, which is the single most notable and most-discussed fact about the character in current canon. It also creates a well-known tension with his 'the Sith have been extinct for a millennium' line in Episode I. Worth surfacing.

notes.suggested: Add Rank: Jedi Master / Jedi High Council member, and Affiliation: Jedi Order, Galactic Republic (Jedi General, Galactic Marines)
notes.detail: His command of the Galactic Marines under Commander Bacara is what ties directly to his death on Mygeeto and is not currently tracked.

notes.current: The Acolyte, Episode I, Episode II, The Clone Wars (TV series), Episode III
notes.suggested: Consider adding The Clone Wars (2008 film)
notes.detail: Mundi appears in the theatrical Star Wars: The Clone Wars film that opens the series, if your timeline plots that title separately from the TV series.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Qi%27ra.description: Qi'ra is a human woman born on Corellia who rose from the streets of Coronet City to become one of the most powerful crime lords in the galaxy. As a child she was a scrumrat in the White Worms gang under Lady Proxima, where she grew up alongside — and fell in love with — Han Solo. Separated from Han during a failed escape at the Coronet Spaceport, she was sold into slavery and ultimately came under the criminal syndicate Crimson Dawn, becoming the trusted lieutenant of Dryden Vos. Reunited briefly with Han during the Kessel Run heist, she chose ambition over their relationship, killing Vos and answering to the syndicate's true master, Maul. After Maul's death she rose to lead Crimson Dawn as "Lady Qi'ra," waging a shadow war against the Sith and the Empire before the syndicate collapsed, after which she passed her legacy to the young Cadeliah and vanished into hiding. She is known for her cunning, her survival instinct, and her tragic history with Han Solo.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Qi%27ra.timeline:
~31 BBY - Birth on Corellia

Qi'ra is born on the shipbuilding world of Corellia and grows up in the impoverished, dangerous streets of Coronet City.

~13 BBY - Life as a White Worms scrumrat

She becomes a scrumrat in Lady Proxima's White Worms gang, rising to the rank of Head Girl. During this time she and fellow orphan Han Solo become close and fall in love.

~10 BBY - Escape attempt and separation from Han

Han and Qi'ra try to flee Corellia using a stolen vial of coaxium, but they are separated at the Coronet Spaceport. Han escapes offworld, promising to return for her, while Qi'ra is captured.

~10 BBY - Sold into Crimson Dawn

Passed through slavery, Qi'ra eventually enters the service of Crimson Dawn and becomes the trusted lieutenant of Dryden Vos, the syndicate's public face.

~10 BBY - The Kessel Run and reunion with Han

Qi'ra reunites with Han during a coaxium heist. When Vos is exposed as a traitor to the syndicate, she kills him and reveals her allegiance to Crimson Dawn's true master. She parts ways with Han, keeping his ship and choosing her own path in the underworld.

~10 BBY - Answering to Maul

Qi'ra makes contact with Maul, the shadowy leader of Crimson Dawn, and departs for Dathomir to join him, cementing her rise within the organization.

~3 ABY - Leader of Crimson Dawn

Following Maul's death, Qi'ra takes control of Crimson Dawn as its ruling "Lady," orchestrating the War of the Bounty Hunters and launching a covert campaign against the Sith and the Galactic Empire.

~3 ABY - Collapse of Crimson Dawn and hiding

Her scheme to strike at Emperor Palpatine unravels when the Knights of Ren turn against her, and Crimson Dawn falls apart. Qi'ra bequeaths her remaining resources to the young Cadeliah and disappears into hiding, her ultimate fate left unrecorded.

birth.source: Wookieepedia "Qi'ra" article lists her birth as 31 BBY (also catalogued on the 31 BBY year page).

death.source: Qi'ra survives the events of the Crimson Dawn comics; after the collapse of the syndicate she bequeaths her resources to Cadeliah and goes into hiding. Canon records no death.

notes.suggested: Remove this field (she is not Force-sensitive)
notes.detail: Qi'ra is not a Force-sensitive character in canon. She is a skilled fighter and crime lord with no Force abilities. Nothing in the Solo film or the Crimson Dawn comics attributes Force sensitivity to her. This is a factual error.

notes.suggested: Keep endYearUnknown true; consider extending her active-story range to ~3 ABY
notes.detail: endYearUnknown is correct — Qi'ra does not die in canon; she goes into hiding after Crimson Dawn's collapse. However, her story extends well past Solo: the comic events (War of the Bounty Hunters, Crimson Reign, Hidden Empire) place her as Crimson Dawn's leader around 3 ABY. The 50 ABY bound is fine as a rendering limit, but the app currently implies her only appearance is Solo.

notes.suggested: Note her major comic appearances if the timeline tracks comics
notes.detail: Solo is her only live-action appearance, so seenIn is accurate for a screen-only timeline. But her most significant story role — leading Crimson Dawn — occurs in the comics (War of the Bounty Hunters, Crimson Reign, Hidden Empire). Worth knowing that the current entry captures only her origin, not her arc as a galactic power.

notes.current: Ends with Han/Qi'ra separation at Coronet Spaceport; describes only her White Worms youth
notes.suggested: Extend to cover her Crimson Dawn rise and leadership
notes.detail: The old description stops at her childhood and the spaceport separation, omitting her entire defining arc: joining Crimson Dawn, killing Dryden Vos, serving Maul, and later ruling the syndicate. It undersells the character significantly.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Finn.description: Finn, formerly designated FN-2187 ("Eight-Seven"), is a Force-sensitive human male and former First Order stormtrooper who deserted and defected to the Resistance during the First Order–Resistance War. Taken from his family as an infant and raised from birth to be an obedient soldier, he experienced a crisis of conscience during a First Order massacre on Jakku, refusing to fire on civilians and fleeing the regime. Initially reluctant to commit to a cause he feared was doomed, he was drawn into the conflict through his friendships with the pilot Poe Dameron and the scavenger Rey. Over the course of the war he rescued Poe, helped destroy Starkiller Base, survived a lightsaber duel with Kylo Ren, joined the strike team at Canto Bight and the Battle of Crait, and ultimately rose to become a general of the Resistance. During the campaign against the resurrected Emperor Palpatine he came to recognize his own connection to the Force, bonded with fellow deserter Jannah, and helped win the decisive Battle of Exegol.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Finn.timeline:
11 ABY - Birth

Finn is born during the New Republic Era. As an infant he is taken from his family by the First Order to be raised as a soldier.

~11 ABY - Conscription as FN-2187

Separated from his parents, the child is stripped of a name, designated FN-2187, and trained from birth in the First Order's stormtrooper program.

34 ABY - Defection at the Battle of Jakku

Deployed on his first combat mission, FN-2187 refuses to fire on the villagers of Tuanul during a massacre. Unwilling to serve, he frees captured Resistance pilot Poe Dameron and helps him escape, and Poe gives him the name "Finn."

34 ABY - Meeting Rey and joining the fight

Shot down over Jakku, Finn meets the scavenger Rey and BB-8. After the First Order attacks Takodana, he reconnects with the Resistance and reluctantly agrees to help.

34 ABY - Assault on Starkiller Base

Posing as an expert to rescue Rey, Finn helps the Resistance strike team infiltrate Starkiller Base. He duels Kylo Ren with Luke Skywalker's lightsaber, is gravely wounded, and falls into a coma.

34 ABY - The mission to Canto Bight

Awakening from his coma aboard the Raddus, Finn partners with mechanic Rose Tico on a covert mission to Canto Bight to disable the First Order fleet's hyperspace tracker.

34 ABY - Battle of Crait

During the last stand on Crait, Finn attempts to sacrifice himself against a First Order siege cannon, but Rose stops him, and he escapes with the surviving Resistance.

35 ABY - General of the Resistance

As the war intensifies against the resurrected Emperor Palpatine, Finn becomes a leader within the Resistance and searches for a Sith wayfinder alongside Rey, Poe, and their allies.

35 ABY - Meeting Jannah and sensing the Force

Finn encounters Jannah, a fellow former stormtrooper who deserted, and on the wreckage of the second Death Star he grows convinced that his instincts are guided by the Force.

35 ABY - Battle of Exegol

Finn leads Resistance ground forces aboard Palpatine's Sith fleet, helping to destroy a Star Destroyer's navigation tower and secure victory at Exegol. He survives the war.

birth.source: Wookieepedia 'Finn' article states he was born in 11 ABY during the New Republic Era; consistent with his being a young adult at the time of The Force Awakens (34 ABY).

death.source: Finn survives the entire sequel trilogy, including the Battle of Exegol in The Rise of Skywalker (35 ABY). Canon records no death for him.

notes.suggested: Add an occupation/affiliation fact such as "Resistance general / former First Order stormtrooper"
notes.detail: Finn's defining identity is his arc from stormtrooper FN-2187 to Resistance general. Homeworld 'Unknown' is correct — canon only says he was taken from his family as an infant and never establishes a birth planet.

notes.current: startYear 11 ABY presented as known, no separate birthYear field
notes.suggested: No change needed — 11 ABY is confirmed
notes.detail: Wookieepedia states Finn was born in 11 ABY, so presenting the date as known is accurate.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Poe_Dam.description: Poe Dameron is a human pilot and military leader, widely regarded as the best starfighter pilot in the Resistance. Born on Yavin 4 to Rebel Alliance veterans Kes Dameron and Shara Bey, he grew up idolizing his mother's flying and, as a rebellious teenager, briefly ran with the Spice Runners of Kijimi alongside Zorii Bliss before turning his life around. He joined the New Republic Defense Fleet, commanding Rapier Squadron, but grew frustrated with the Republic's inaction against the First Order and defected to General Leia Organa's Resistance. Flying his customized T-70 X-wing "Black One" under the callsign Black Leader, he became one of Leia's most trusted operatives, leading the assault that destroyed Starkiller Base. After a hard lesson in the cost of reckless heroism during the evacuation of D'Qar and the Battle of Crait, he matured into a leader, was named acting General, and ultimately co-commanded the Resistance fleet with Finn at the Battle of Exegol, helping bring down the resurrected Emperor Palpatine.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Poe_Dam.timeline:
2 ABY - Birth on Yavin 4

Poe Dameron is born on the jungle moon of Yavin 4 to Rebel Alliance pilot Shara Bey and Pathfinder sergeant Kes Dameron, growing up on their homestead near a Force-sensitive tree gifted by Luke Skywalker.

~18 ABY - Running with the Spice Runners of Kijimi

As a rebellious teenager, Poe leaves home and falls in with the Spice Runners of Kijimi, forming a bond with Zorii Bliss before abandoning the criminal life to pursue a more honorable path.

~28 ABY - Service in the New Republic

Poe follows in his late mother's footsteps and joins the New Republic Defense Fleet, eventually rising to command Rapier Squadron.

~32 ABY - Defection to the Resistance

Disillusioned by the New Republic's refusal to confront the First Order's violations of the Galactic Concordance, Poe leaves the Defense Fleet and joins Leia Organa's Resistance, becoming one of her most trusted operatives.

~34 ABY - Missions with the Resistance

Flying under the callsign Black Leader in his T-70 X-wing Black One, Poe undertakes covert operations for the Resistance, crossing paths with figures tied to the events depicted in Star Wars Resistance.

34 ABY - The Force Awakens

Captured by the First Order while retrieving a map to Luke Skywalker, Poe escapes with the stormtrooper he names Finn, is reunited with his droid BB-8, and leads the successful starfighter assault that destroys Starkiller Base.

34 ABY - The Last Jedi

Poe's reckless heroism during the evacuation of D'Qar costs lives and gets him demoted by Leia and Vice Admiral Holdo; he learns the burden of leadership and helps the survivors escape the Battle of Crait.

35 ABY - The Rise of Skywalker

Named acting General by Leia, Poe leads the Resistance in confronting the resurrected Emperor Palpatine, revealing his spice-running past, and co-commands the fleet with Finn at the Battle of Exegol, ending the threat of the Final Order.

birth.source: Wookieepedia (Poe Dameron) states he was born on the tenth day of the fifth month, 2 ABY, on Yavin 4; corroborated by web sources.

death.source: Poe Dameron survives all canon appearances through The Rise of Skywalker (35 ABY); no death is recorded in canon.

notes.current: startYear 2 with no startYearUnknown, so 2 ABY is presented as a known birth year
notes.suggested: Keep 2 ABY, but note it corresponds to birthYear; the entry lacks a birthYear field even though startYear equals the birth year
notes.detail: The birth year (2 ABY) is correct and canon-confirmed, so presenting it as known is appropriate. No change to the value is needed; flagging only that there is no separate birthYear field.

notes.suggested: Yavin 4 (confirmed birthplace)
notes.detail: Yavin 4 is correct as his birthplace and childhood home per Wookieepedia. No change needed, but worth noting his strong later associations with Kijimi (spice runners) and D'Qar/Resistance bases in case homeworld display needs nuance.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Ahsoka_.description: Ahsoka Tano is a Togruta former Jedi and one of the most prominent figures spanning the fall of the Republic and the rise of the New Republic era. Discovered as a Force-sensitive child on her homeworld of Shili by Jedi Master Plo Koon, she trained at the Jedi Temple on Coruscant and, at the outbreak of the Clone Wars, was assigned by Grand Master Yoda as the Padawan of Anakin Skywalker, who nicknamed her "Snips." Over the course of the war she matured from an eager apprentice into a capable commander, but after being framed for a bombing of the Jedi Temple and cleared only after nearly being expelled, she chose to walk away from the Jedi Order. She survived Order 66, went into hiding, and later became a key organizer of the growing rebellion under the codename "Fulcrum," aiding rebel cells and confronting her fallen master, now Darth Vader. In the decades after the Empire's defeat she continued as a wandering ally of the New Republic, hunting the missing Grand Admiral Thrawn and mentoring Sabine Wren, becoming an iconic bridge between the prequel, original, and sequel eras of Star Wars.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Ahsoka_.timeline:
36 BBY - Birth on Shili

Ahsoka Tano is born on the Togruta homeworld of Shili.

~33 BBY - Discovered by Plo Koon

At around three years of age, her Force sensitivity is detected and Jedi Master Plo Koon brings her to the Jedi Temple on Coruscant for training.

22 BBY - Padawan to Anakin Skywalker

At the outbreak of the Clone Wars, Yoda assigns Ahsoka as Anakin Skywalker's apprentice; she joins him at the Battle of Christophsis, earning the nickname "Snips," and helps rescue Rotta the Huttlet to secure a Republic–Hutt alliance.

22–19 BBY - Commander in the Clone Wars

Ahsoka serves throughout the war, growing from a headstrong Padawan into a skilled commander alongside Anakin and Captain Rex.

19 BBY - Leaving the Jedi Order

Framed for the bombing of the Jedi Temple hangar and nearly expelled, Ahsoka is exonerated but, disillusioned with the Order, chooses to leave the Jedi.

19 BBY - Order 66 and the Siege of Mandalore

Reuniting with Rex during the Siege of Mandalore, she survives Order 66 when Rex's inhibitor chip is removed, and afterward fakes her death to go into hiding.

~18–4 BBY - Years in hiding and the rebellion

Living in obscurity, Ahsoka gradually becomes involved in early resistance to the Empire, eventually coordinating rebel cells.

~4–2 BBY - Fulcrum

Operating under the codename "Fulcrum," she supplies intelligence and support to the growing rebel network alongside the crew of the Ghost.

2 BBY - Confrontation on Malachor

Ahsoka faces Darth Vader and discovers he is her former master Anakin; she is presumed lost in the ruined Sith temple.

~1 BBY - The World Between Worlds

Ezra Bridger pulls Ahsoka out of Malachor through the World Between Worlds, saving her life.

~1 ABY - Searching for Ezra

After the Empire's fall at Endor, Ahsoka sets out to find the missing Ezra Bridger and the vanished Grand Admiral Thrawn.

9 ABY - The Mandalorian and Boba Fett era

Now a wandering warrior, Ahsoka appears on Corvus and later aids Din Djarin and Grogu, continuing her hunt for Thrawn.

9 ABY - The Ahsoka series

Ahsoka mentors Sabine Wren, journeys to a distant galaxy, and confronts the returned Grand Admiral Thrawn, working to prevent his return threatening the New Republic.

birth.source: Wookieepedia "Ahsoka Tano" article: born 36 BBY on Shili; consistent with being discovered by Plo Koon at age ~3 and being ~14 at the start of the Clone Wars in 22 BBY.

death.source: Canon gives no death year. Ahsoka survives every appearance and is alive and active in ~9 ABY (The Mandalorian, The Book of Boba Fett, Ahsoka). No death is depicted in canon.

notes.current: The Clone Wars (movie), The Clone Wars (TV series), Ahsoka, The Mandalorian, The Book of Boba Fett
notes.suggested: Add Star Wars Rebels (and optionally Tales of the Jedi)
notes.detail: Star Wars Rebels is one of Ahsoka's most significant appearances — it is where she operates as Fulcrum and confronts Darth Vader on Malachor. Its omission is a notable gap. Tales of the Jedi (2022) also features her prominently across her youth and post-Order 66 life.

notes.current: Ahsoka Tano was a former Jedi Padawan who, after the Clone Wars, helped establish a network of various rebel cells...
notes.suggested: Update to reflect she is a living, active character through at least 9 ABY (New Republic era), not only a Clone Wars figure.
notes.detail: The existing description stops at the Clone Wars-era rescue of Rotta and frames her in the past tense as if her story ended. Her major later arcs (Rebels, Malachor, hunting Thrawn, The Mandalorian, and the Ahsoka series) are unrepresented.

notes.suggested: Keep as a rendering bound; do not treat as a death.
notes.detail: This is correct as-is — Ahsoka does not die in canon and her fate remains open. Flagging only to confirm the 20 ABY value should never be surfaced as a death year. Her latest confirmed activity is ~9 ABY, so the column could arguably extend further, but there is no canonical endpoint.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Hunter.description: Hunter was a clone commando sergeant of the Grand Army of the Republic and the commanding officer of Clone Force 99, the experimental squad of genetically enhanced clones nicknamed "the Bad Batch." Grown on Kamino from the genetic template of Jango Fett, he carried a mutation that gave him extraordinarily heightened senses — able to track targets by scent, sound and electromagnetic fields — which made him the squad's scout and de facto leader alongside the super-strong Wrecker, the analytical Tech and the sharpshooter Crosshair. During the Clone Wars he led the Batch on high-risk operations, most notably the campaign on Anaxes where they recovered the long-missing ARC trooper Echo from Skako Minor and folded him into the squad. Because their aberrant genetics left them largely immune to the inhibitor chips, Hunter and most of his men did not carry out Order 66, and their refusal to fire on civilians turned them into fugitives from the new Galactic Empire. On Kamino he took responsibility for Omega, an unaltered female clone, and the pair became the emotional center of the squad as they scraped out a mercenary living, lost Crosshair to Imperial conditioning and later lost Tech entirely on Eriadu. Hunter's driving goal became finding Omega a safe home, which he found on the island world of Pabu, and after storming the Empire's cloning facility at Mount Tantiss to rescue her and the other captive clones, he settled there. Years later he let Omega go to join the Rebel Alliance, remaining on Pabu with Wrecker and a reconciled Crosshair.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Hunter.timeline:
~32 BBY - Grown on Kamino

Hunter is created on Kamino as part of the first generation of Jango Fett clones, one of a handful of "defective" units whose mutations were deemed advantageous. His alteration grants him superhumanly acute senses. Like all clones he is engineered to age at twice the normal rate.

~22 BBY - The Clone Wars begin

With the war between the Republic and the Confederacy underway, Hunter and his squadmates Wrecker, Tech and Crosshair are formed into Clone Force 99, named in honor of the malformed Kaminoan maintenance clone 99. Their unorthodox methods earn them a reputation for taking missions regular clones cannot.

~19 BBY - The Anaxes campaign

Assigned to assist Captain Rex, Anakin Skywalker and Ahsoka Tano on Anaxes, Hunter leads the Batch on a raid of a Separatist cyber center. He then agrees to Rex's mission to Skako Minor, where the squad rescues the presumed-dead ARC trooper Echo from Wat Tambor's captivity.

~19 BBY - Order 66 and the fall of the Republic

On Kaller, Hunter's squad witnesses clone troopers turn on Jedi General Depa Billaba. Their mutations leave them largely unaffected by the inhibitor chips, and Hunter shields the Padawan Caleb Dume rather than hunting him. Only Crosshair complies with the order.

~19 BBY - Desertion and Omega

Returning to Kamino under the new Empire, the Batch refuse to fire on civilians on Onderon and are imprisoned. Hunter takes in Omega, an unaltered clone girl, and leads the squad's escape — leaving behind Crosshair, whose chip is intensified by Imperial doctors. They begin a fugitive life running jobs for Cid on Ord Mantell.

~19 BBY - Return to Kamino

Lured back to Tipoca City by Crosshair, Hunter and the squad narrowly survive the Empire's orbital bombardment of the Kaminoan cloning facilities. Crosshair saves them but refuses to leave with them, and the clones' birthworld is destroyed.

~18 BBY - Finding Pabu

After breaking with Cid, the squad discovers the island world of Pabu. Hunter, increasingly focused on giving Omega a childhood rather than a war, decides it should become their home.

~18 BBY - The death of Tech

During a mission on Eriadu to help Clone Captain Howzer, Tech sacrifices himself so the others can escape, falling from a collapsing rail line. Shortly afterward Omega is captured by Imperial forces and taken to Mount Tantiss.

~18 BBY - Hunting for Tantiss

Hunter and Wrecker search relentlessly for the hidden Imperial facility, eventually joining forces with a defecting Crosshair after Omega helps him escape. Hunter is briefly captured himself before being rescued by Omega, Crosshair and Wrecker.

~18 BBY - Assault on Mount Tantiss

The Batch infiltrate Dr. Royce Hemlock's cloning complex on Weyland, free the captive clone children and Omega, and destroy the facility, ending the Empire's Necromancer cloning program. Hunter, Wrecker, Crosshair, Omega and Echo survive.

~18 BBY - Retirement on Pabu

Hunter settles on Pabu with Wrecker, Crosshair and Omega, laying down his armor and living the peaceful life he had fought to secure for her.

~5 BBY - Omega joins the Rebellion

Years later, a grown Omega tells Hunter that the coming fight against the Empire is hers, not his, and departs Pabu as a Rebel pilot. Hunter watches her go and remains on Pabu with his brothers; canon records nothing of his fate afterward.

birth.source: Wookieepedia's Hunter article was unreachable during this pass (fandom.com returned HTTP 402 to every fetch attempt), so this is derived from established canon rather than read off the page: the clone army was commissioned by Sifo-Dyas around 32 BBY and the first generation of Jango Fett clones — Clone Force 99 among them — was grown on Kamino from that point, aging at twice the normal rate so as to be combat-ready by the Battle of Geonosis in 22 BBY. Canon does not state a specific birth year for Hunter individually, so ~32 BBY is an inference, correctly flagged as a guess.

death.source: Hunter survives every canon appearance. He is alive at the end of Star Wars: The Bad Batch (2024), including the epilogue set years after the assault on Mount Tantiss, in which an adult Omega leaves Pabu to join the Rebellion while Hunter, Wrecker and Crosshair remain behind. No canon source depicts or states his death.

notes.suggested: roughly -5 (5 BBY), or at most -1 BBY, keeping endYearUnknown: true
notes.detail: Hunter does not die in canon, so endYearUnknown is right — but 30 ABY is an implausible rendering bound. Clones age at twice the normal rate; by the Bad Batch epilogue Hunter is already visibly elderly. Drawing his column ~48 years past his last canon appearance implies a lifespan no clone is shown to have. The last point at which he actually appears is the series' epilogue on Pabu, when an adult Omega leaves to join the Rebellion — best placed a few years before the Battle of Yavin.

notes.suggested: keep -32 and keep the guess flag
notes.detail: Canon never gives Hunter an individual birth year. 32 BBY is the standard inference from the clone army being commissioned around then and the first generation reaching maturity by Geonosis in 22 BBY. The guess flag is correct and should stay; do not promote this to a known date.

notes.current: Homeworld Kamino, Species Human, Clone Yes, Force Sensitive No
notes.suggested: add e.g. {"name": "Rank", "value": "Sergeant"} and {"name": "Unit", "value": "Clone Force 99 (the Bad Batch)"}
notes.detail: Hunter's defining facts are his sergeant's rank and his command of Clone Force 99; both are stated throughout The Bad Batch. The existing metadata is accurate as far as it goes — Kamino, human clone of Jango Fett, non-Force-sensitive all check out.

notes.current: Existing description stops at the Clone Wars and the squad roster.
notes.suggested: Extend through the Imperial era.
notes.detail: Our current text covers only Hunter's Clone Wars service and never mentions the events he is now best known for: surviving Order 66, becoming Omega's guardian, the destruction of Kamino, the losses of Crosshair and Tech, Pabu, and Mount Tantiss. It reads as though it was written before The Bad Batch aired.

Verification caveat the maintainer should know: starwars.fandom.com returned HTTP 402 on every fetch attempt during this pass, so the Wookieepedia article could not be read directly. The event sequence above comes from the episodes themselves and is reliable; the specific in-universe years (~19/~18 BBY for the Bad Batch seasons, ~5 BBY for the epilogue) are the community's standard placement rather than dates I could confirm on the page, and are marked with ~ accordingly. Worth a re-check when the source is reachable.

Secondary sources circulate the designation "CT-9901" for Hunter. I could not confirm this on Wookieepedia during this pass and it may originate from a fan wiki rather than canon — do not add it to the entry without verification.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/BB-8.description: BB-8, sometimes spelled Beebee-Ate and nicknamed BB, was a spherical BB-series astromech droid built in 29 ABY on Hosnian Prime. Distinguished by a free-rolling ball body topped with a domed head reminiscent of an R2-series unit, BB-8 was primarily white with orange and silver accents and a single black photoreceptor. He became the loyal companion of Resistance pilot Poe Dameron, accompanying him aboard his T-70 X-wing starfighter. BB-8 is best known for safeguarding a fragment of the map leading to the exiled Luke Skywalker after Poe entrusted it to him on Jakku, setting in motion the events of the war between the Resistance and the First Order. Plucky, resourceful, and fiercely loyal, he aided Rey, Finn, and the Resistance across the campaigns against the First Order and the resurrected Emperor Palpatine, surviving the conflict as one of the era's most beloved droids.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/BB-8.timeline:
29 ABY - Construction on Hosnian Prime

BB-8 is built as a BB-series astromech droid on Hosnian Prime and eventually enters the service of the Resistance.

~30s ABY - Companion to Poe Dameron

BB-8 becomes the astromech partner of Resistance pilot Poe Dameron, riding aboard his T-70 X-wing and serving alongside him during missions depicted in Star Wars Resistance.

34 ABY - The Search for Luke Skywalker

On Jakku, Poe entrusts BB-8 with a fragment of the map to the exiled Luke Skywalker. BB-8 escapes the First Order, encounters the scavenger Rey and the defector Finn, and helps deliver the map to the Resistance, playing a key role in the events of The Force Awakens.

34 ABY - The War Against the First Order

BB-8 accompanies the Resistance through its desperate flight from the First Order fleet and the stand on Crait, assisting Poe, Rey, and Finn during the events of The Last Jedi.

35 ABY - The Fall of the Final Order

BB-8 joins the final campaign against the resurrected Emperor Palpatine and the Sith Eternal fleet at Exegol, surviving the Resistance's victory during the events of The Rise of Skywalker.

birth.source: Wookieepedia BB-8 article: droid built in 29 ABY on Hosnian Prime

death.source: BB-8 does not die in canon; survives through The Rise of Skywalker (35 ABY)

notes.suggested: Hosnian Prime
notes.detail: Misspelling. Canon states BB-8 was built on Hosnian Prime in 29 ABY. Note 'homeworld' is really place of manufacture for a droid.

notes.suggested: Droid (BB-series astromech droid)
notes.detail: 'Droid' is fine, but the specific model is a BB-series astromech droid; worth noting for precision.

notes.suggested: no death; last appears 35 ABY (The Rise of Skywalker)
notes.detail: Correctly flagged as not a death. BB-8 survives the conflict against the First Order/Sith Eternal. The column bound could reasonably end around 35 ABY, his last confirmed canonical appearance, rather than 50 ABY.

notes.suggested: Owner/Master: Poe Dameron
notes.detail: BB-8's association with Poe Dameron is his defining trait and could be tracked as metadata.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Jacen_S.description: Jacen Syndulla is a Force-sensitive human/Twi'lek hybrid, the son of the rebel General Hera Syndulla and the late Jedi Knight Kanan Jarrus, and the grandson of the Twi'lek resistance leader Cham Syndulla. Born during the Galactic Civil War shortly after his father's sacrifice, he first appeared as an infant and toddler in the epilogue of Star Wars Rebels, glimpsed alongside his mother in the years following the Battle of Endor. He made his live-action debut as a boy in the Disney+ series Ahsoka, where he expressed a wish to become a Jedi like his father, hinting at inherited Force potential. Named by creator Dave Filoni as a nod to Jacen Solo of the old Expanded Universe, he represents the next generation of the Syndulla and Jarrus legacy.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Jacen_S.timeline:
1 BBY - Birth

Jacen Syndulla is born to Hera Syndulla and Kanan Jarrus during the Galactic Civil War, shortly after Kanan's death; he never knew his father, who sacrificed himself to save the Spectres.

~5 ABY - Seen in the Rebels epilogue

Following the Battle of Endor and the liberation of Lothal, a young Jacen is shown with his mother Hera as Sabine Wren departs to help find Ezra Bridger, marking his first appearance.

~9 ABY - Live-action debut in Ahsoka

Now a boy living with his mother, Jacen appears in the series Ahsoka, showing curiosity about the Jedi and Ezra Bridger and telling Hera he wants to become a Jedi—an early hint of his Force sensitivity.

birth.source: Wookieepedia (Jacen Syndulla) and corroborating references give his birth year as 1 BBY, born shortly after his father Kanan Jarrus's death during the Galactic Civil War.

notes.suggested: Yes (implied / not explicitly confirmed)
notes.detail: Canon strongly implies Jacen is Force-sensitive—he is Kanan Jarrus's son and in Ahsoka expresses a desire to become a Jedi—but no canon source has definitively depicted him using the Force. Worth flagging as strongly implied rather than firmly established.

notes.suggested: Add Mother: Hera Syndulla, Father: Kanan Jarrus, Grandfather: Cham Syndulla
notes.detail: His parentage is his defining canonical trait and is not captured in the metadata fields, only in the free-text description.

notes.suggested: unchanged
notes.detail: Both listed appearances check out: he appears as an infant/toddler in the Rebels series finale epilogue and in live action in the Ahsoka series. No correction needed.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/C-3PO.description: C-3PO, also spelled See-Threepio and usually called simply Threepio, is a 3PO-series protocol droid of Cybot Galactica design, programmed for etiquette and protocol and fluent in over six million forms of communication. Unusually among droids, he was not factory-built but assembled from salvaged scrap by the enslaved boy Anakin Skywalker in Mos Espa on Tatooine, and was operational — though still stripped of his outer plating — by 32 BBY. Fussy, anxious and endlessly voluble, he became the inseparable and perpetually exasperated counterpart of the astromech R2-D2, and the pair drifted through nearly every hinge point of galactic history: the Naboo crisis, the Battle of Geonosis and the Clone Wars, the secret marriage of Anakin and Padmé Amidala, the birth of the Skywalker twins, and a memory wipe that erased all of it before he passed into the service of Senator Bail Organa on Alderaan. Serving the Organa family and then the Rebel Alliance, he carried the stolen Death Star plans aboard the Tantive IV, was worshipped as a deity by the Ewoks of Endor, and later served the New Republic and General Leia Organa. In 35 ABY he sacrificed his own memory to translate forbidden Sith text and set the path to Exegol, a loss R2-D2 later undid. He is never destroyed in canon and survives the fall of the Final Order.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/C-3PO.timeline:
~32 BBY - Assembled on Tatooine

Built from scavenged parts by the enslaved boy Anakin Skywalker in the family hovel in Mos Espa, C-3PO is operational but unfinished, lacking his outer plating. Anakin presents him to Padmé Amidala and intends him to help his mother Shmi.

32 BBY - Left behind on Tatooine

When Anakin wins his freedom and departs with Qui-Gon Jinn to become a Jedi, C-3PO remains on Tatooine in Shmi Skywalker's care.

22 BBY - Reunion and plating

Anakin returns to Tatooine searching for his mother and finds C-3PO in the service of the Lars homestead, now fitted with metal coverings. The droid joins Anakin, Padmé and R2-D2 on their journey to Geonosis.

22 BBY - Battle of Geonosis

Caught in a Geonosian droid factory, C-3PO's head is knocked onto a battle droid body and a battle droid head onto his, leaving him charging into battle shouting "die, Jedi dogs" before R2-D2 restores him. He afterwards witnesses the secret wedding of Anakin and Padmé on Naboo.

22 BBY - 19 BBY - The Clone Wars

Attached to Senator Amidala's household on Coruscant, C-3PO is repeatedly swept into the war alongside R2-D2 — abducted, marooned, mistaken for a spy and pressed into diplomatic missions across the galaxy.

19 BBY - Fall of the Republic and memory wipe

C-3PO is present at Padmé's death on Polis Massa and the birth of Luke and Leia. To protect the twins' secret, Bail Organa orders the protocol droid's memory erased, and C-3PO enters the service of House Organa on Alderaan with his past wiped clean.

9 BBY - Service on Alderaan

Now a fixture of the Organa royal household, C-3PO attends a formal reception at the palace during the years Bail and Breha are raising the young Princess Leia.

~5 BBY - Droids in distress

Sent by Senator Bail Organa on a covert errand involving stolen weapons, C-3PO and R2-D2 cross paths with the crew of the Ghost, unwittingly aiding the early rebellion.

1 BBY - Yavin 4

C-3PO is present at the Rebel Alliance's Massassi base as Jyn Erso's team departs for Scarif to steal the Death Star plans.

0 BBY - Capture of the Tantive IV

Aboard Leia's corvette when Darth Vader seizes it, C-3PO follows R2-D2 into an escape pod carrying the Death Star plans and crashes on Tatooine.

0 BBY - Sold to the Lars homestead

Captured by Jawas and sold to Owen Lars, C-3PO meets Luke Skywalker and helps set in motion the delivery of Leia's message to Obi-Wan Kenobi.

0 BBY - Battle of Yavin

C-3PO waits out the assault on the Death Star at the Yavin base and offers his own circuits to repair a badly damaged R2-D2 after the victory.

3 ABY - Hoth and Bespin

Serving the Alliance on Echo Base, C-3PO flees with Han, Leia and Chewbacca aboard the Millennium Falcon. On Cloud City he is blasted apart by stormtroopers and carried out in pieces on Chewbacca's back, then reassembled.

4 ABY - Tatooine and Endor

Offered to Jabba the Hutt as part of Luke's rescue plan, C-3PO serves as the Hutt's interpreter before escaping. On the forest moon of Endor the Ewoks take him for a golden deity, and his storytelling wins their alliance for the strike on the shield generator.

5 ABY - 34 ABY - New Republic service

C-3PO remains with Leia Organa through the founding of the New Republic and into the Resistance, eventually overseeing its droid network, and takes on a mismatched red arm for a period before restoring his gold plating.

9 ABY - New Republic Senate errand

Acting on Leia's authority, C-3PO delivers her intervention to a Senate committee on Hera Syndulla's behalf during the hearing over her unsanctioned mission.

34 ABY - The search for Luke Skywalker

C-3PO reunites with Han Solo and helps the Resistance assemble the map to Luke Skywalker's exile, then witnesses the destruction of the Hosnian system and the assault on Starkiller Base.

34 ABY - Crait

He survives the collapse of the Resistance fleet and the last stand on Crait among the handful of survivors aboard the Millennium Falcon.

35 ABY - Sacrifice of memory

To translate a Sith inscription pointing to Exegol, C-3PO permits a forbidden override that erases his memory entirely, taking one last look at his friends before the wipe.

35 ABY - Restoration and victory

R2-D2 restores C-3PO's memory from backup. He survives the Battle of Exegol and the fall of the Final Order, and celebrates with the victorious Resistance.

birth.source: Wookieepedia "C-3PO" and "3PO-series protocol droid" (canon) — C-3PO was built from scrap parts by the enslaved boy Anakin Skywalker in Mos Espa, Tatooine, and is dated to c. 32 BBY, the year of The Phantom Menace, when he is first seen operational (though still unplated). Wookieepedia qualifies the date with "c.", since assembly began some time earlier and only his completion is anchored to 32 BBY.

death.source: C-3PO is never destroyed in canon. He is dismembered on Cloud City in 3 ABY and reassembled, and has his memory wiped in 19 BBY and again in 35 ABY (restored by R2-D2), but he is alive and active at the end of The Rise of Skywalker (35 ABY).

notes.suggested: 32 BBY (estimated)
notes.detail: Automatic check: birth year in data.json disagrees with the year Claude verified. Wookieepedia "C-3PO" and "3PO-series protocol droid" (canon) — C-3PO was built from scrap parts by the enslaved boy Anakin Skywalker in Mos Espa, Tatooine, and is dated to c. 32 BBY, the year of The Phantom Menace, when he is first seen operational (though still unplated). Wookieepedia qualifies the date with "c.", since assembly began some time earlier and only his completion is anchored to 32 BBY.

notes.suggested: -32 (guess flag can be dropped, or kept as a soft "c.")
notes.detail: Wookieepedia's canon article and the 3PO-series protocol droid article both date C-3PO's construction by Anakin Skywalker in Mos Espa to c. 32 BBY — the year of The Phantom Menace, where he is first shown operational. 34 BBY has no canon basis. 32 BBY is a sourced date rather than a positioning guess, though Wookieepedia's "c." reflects that assembly began at an unspecified earlier point (Anakin calls him "not finished" in 32 BBY).

notes.suggested: Add Manufacturer: Cybot Galactica; add Model/Class: 3PO-series protocol droid
notes.detail: C-3PO is a Cybot Galactica 3PO-series unit assembled from salvage rather than factory-produced — the distinction is one of his defining canon traits and is worth surfacing alongside "Creator: Anakin Skywalker."

notes.suggested: Track the two memory wipes (19 BBY by Bail Organa's order; 35 ABY, self-authorized, restored by R2-D2)
notes.detail: These are the closest things C-3PO has to deaths and are the reason he has no recollection of the prequel era in the original trilogy. They are major, datable milestones the entry currently does not capture.

Legends material (Tales of the Golden Droid) placed C-3PO's original construction on Affa in 112 BBY, with Anakin merely rebuilding him. That backstory is NOT canon and search engines frequently surface it as if it were — worth guarding against if this entry is ever re-sourced automatically.

No correction needed — this checks out. C-3PO is never destroyed in canon; he is dismembered on Cloud City in 3 ABY and reassembled, and is alive at the close of The Rise of Skywalker in 35 ABY. Flagging only to confirm the "unknown" treatment is right and no death should be recorded.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Omega.description: Omega was a female human clone of the Mandalorian bounty hunter Jango Fett, one of only two unaltered, pure genetic replications of his template created on Kamino — the other being Boba Fett. Because she was unmodified, she aged at a normal human rate rather than the accelerated rate of the Republic's clone troopers, and she grew up in Tipoca City as a medical assistant to the Kaminoan chief scientist Nala Se, who quietly protected her. At the close of the Clone Wars she befriended Clone Force 99, the experimental squad known as the Bad Batch, and fled Kamino with them as the Republic became the Galactic Empire, becoming the youngest member of their found family. Her unique, uncorrupted genetic material made her a target: the bounty hunters Fennec Shand and Cad Bane both hunted her, and she was eventually captured and held at Doctor Royce Hemlock's Mount Tantiss facility, where her blood was sought for the Empire's secret cloning research that would one day underpin Project Necromancer. Omega escaped Tantiss, helped free the imprisoned clones and children held there, and saw the facility destroyed, ending Hemlock's work at the cost of her brothers' long fight — Tech having already died on Eriadu. She settled on the island world of Pabu with Hunter, Wrecker and Crosshair, and years later, grown, she left home to fly for the Rebel Alliance, carrying Tech's goggles with her.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Omega.timeline:
~32 BBY - Creation on Kamino

Omega is produced in Tipoca City as an unaltered, pure genetic replication of Jango Fett's template, one of two such clones alongside Boba Fett. Unlike the clone army, she is not given accelerated aging and grows at a normal human rate.

~32 BBY to 19 BBY - Childhood in Tipoca City

Raised in the cloning facility under the watch of Nala Se, Omega works as a medical assistant and never leaves Kamino, observing the clone troopers she considers brothers.

19 BBY - Meeting Clone Force 99

Omega takes an interest in the experimental squad Clone Force 99 when they return from Kaller, and befriends them as Admiral Tarkin arrives to evaluate the clones' future.

19 BBY - Escape from Kamino

With the Republic reorganized into the Galactic Empire and Order 66 issued, Omega flees Kamino aboard the Marauder with Hunter, Wrecker, Tech and Echo, leaving Crosshair behind.

19 BBY - Hunted by Fennec Shand

Nala Se secretly hires the bounty hunter Fennec Shand to retrieve Omega, beginning a long pursuit across the Outer Rim as the Batch take mercenary work for Cid on Ord Mantell.

19 BBY - Return to Kamino and its destruction

Lured back to Kamino by Crosshair, Omega and the squad survive the Empire's bombardment of Tipoca City, escaping the flooded ruins of the only home she had known.

~19 BBY - Captured by Cad Bane and rescued

Bane abducts Omega and delivers her to the Kaminoan scientist Nala Se's Imperial handlers; the Batch and Hunter track her down and recover her.

~18 BBY - Life on the run and the loss of Tech

Working with Echo and the clone underground, Omega witnesses Tech sacrifice himself on Eriadu so the rest of the squad can survive — the first death in her family.

~18 BBY - Capture and imprisonment at Mount Tantiss

Doctor Royce Hemlock seizes Omega and takes her to the hidden Mount Tantiss facility on Weyland, where her uncorrupted M-count blood is harvested for the Empire's secret cloning experiments.

~18 BBY - Escape from Tantiss and the fall of Hemlock

Omega organizes and leads the imprisoned clones and captive children out of the vault; the Batch assault the mountain, Hemlock is killed by Crosshair, and the facility is destroyed.

~18 BBY - Settling on Pabu

Omega makes a home on the island world of Pabu with Hunter, Wrecker and Crosshair, finally living out of the Empire's reach.

~5 BBY - Leaving to join the Rebel Alliance

Grown to adulthood, Omega departs Pabu in her own ship to fly for the Rebellion, keeping Tech's goggles on her dashboard. The epilogue's exact year is not given in canon.

birth.source: Wookieepedia "Omega" article and its Talk page: Omega was one of the two unaltered, pure genetic replications of Jango Fett (with Boba Fett/Alpha, born 32 BBY) produced at the outset of the Kaminoan cloning program. No canon work states her birth year outright; 32 BBY is derived from her being created alongside Boba and from her own statement in "Kamino Lost" that she predates Clone Force 99 (created no later than 29 BBY). Her apparent age (~10-12) during the 19 BBY series makes a slightly later date (c. 31-30 BBY) equally defensible.

death.source: Omega survives the entire run of The Bad Batch; the series finale epilogue, set roughly a decade or more after 18 BBY, shows an adult Omega leaving Pabu to join the Rebel Alliance. Canon records no death.

notes.suggested: keep -32 and keep the unknown flag
notes.detail: Correctly flagged. No canon source states Omega's birth year. 32 BBY is an inference from her being one of the two pure genetic replications requested by Jango Fett alongside Boba (born 32 BBY). Her dialogue in "Kamino Lost" (she was alone before Clone Force 99 existed) caps her birth no later than ~29 BBY, but her apparent age of roughly 10-12 in 19 BBY leads many to argue for 31-30 BBY. Worth surfacing as a known ambiguity rather than tightening.

notes.suggested: about -5, or the latest year the Bad Batch epilogue can be placed
notes.detail: 40 ABY is far beyond anything canon supports for Omega. The last time she is seen is The Bad Batch series-finale epilogue, an unspecified number of years after ~18 BBY, when she is an adult leaving Pabu to join the Rebel Alliance — commonly placed somewhere between 5 BBY and 0 BBY. Drawing her column to 40 ABY implies a 70-plus-year presence with no source behind it.

notes.current: Describes only her Kamino origin and the season 1 premise, ending with her escape from Kamino.
notes.suggested: Replace with the updated summary covering Tantiss, Hemlock, Pabu and the Rebellion.
notes.detail: The entry predates seasons 2 and 3. It omits her capture by Cad Bane, her imprisonment at Mount Tantiss, her blood's role in the Empire's cloning research (the seed of Project Necromancer), the destruction of Tantiss, and her adult departure to join the Rebel Alliance.

notes.suggested: Add Gender: Female; Genetic template: Jango Fett; Affiliation: Clone Force 99, Rebel Alliance
notes.detail: Her defining trait is that she is an unaltered clone of Jango Fett who ages normally — worth calling out explicitly, as is her eventual Rebel Alliance affiliation established in the finale.

notes.suggested: No (with caveat)
notes.detail: Correct as stated — Omega is not a Force user. However, season 3 establishes she has a notably high M-count, which is why Hemlock harvests her blood for Project Necromancer. Flagging in case the field is read as 'no Force connection whatsoever'.

notes.suggested: leave absent
notes.detail: Confirming rather than correcting: Omega does not die in canon, so no endYearEvent is appropriate. The column end should be treated purely as a rendering bound.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Ezra_Br.description: Ezra Bridger is a human Force-sensitive from the planet Lothal who became a Jedi Padawan, con artist turned freedom fighter, and revolutionary leader in the early rebellion against the Galactic Empire. Born on Empire Day in 19 BBY to Ephraim and Mira Bridger, he was orphaned as a boy after his parents were imprisoned for broadcasting anti-Imperial messages, and survived alone on Lothal's streets. In 5 BBY he crossed paths with the rebel crew of the starship Ghost and was taken in and trained in the ways of the Force by Kanan Jarrus. Bridger's abilities grew rapidly, and his broadcast message of hope helped unite scattered rebel cells. He is best known for his central role in Star Wars Rebels, culminating in the liberation of Lothal, when he sacrificed his freedom to defeat Grand Admiral Thrawn by having purrgil pull Thrawn's flagship — with Ezra aboard — into hyperspace and out of the galaxy. Long presumed lost, he was revealed alive on the distant world of Peridea and returned to the known galaxy in the New Republic era during the Ahsoka series.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Ezra_Br.timeline:
19 BBY - Birth on Lothal (Empire Day)

Ezra Bridger is born on Lothal to Ephraim and Mira Bridger on the very day the Galactic Empire is proclaimed, making his birthday coincide with Empire Day.

~12 BBY - Orphaned

His parents are arrested and imprisoned by the Empire for broadcasting messages criticizing Imperial rule, leaving the young Ezra to fend for himself as a street orphan on Lothal.

5 BBY - Joining the Ghost crew

Ezra encounters the rebel crew of the Ghost during a raid on Imperial cargo and joins them, beginning his training in the Force under Kanan Jarrus (events of the Star Wars Rebels pilot, "Spark of Rebellion").

~4 BBY - Growth as a Padawan

Ezra deepens his Jedi training, acquires and later loses his first lightsaber, and encounters figures including Ahsoka Tano and remnants of the Jedi Order as the rebel cell fights the Empire.

~3 BBY - Facing the dark side

Ezra explores a Sith holocron and dark-side temptations while the crew's operations expand, drawing the attention of Imperial forces including the Inquisitors.

2 BBY - Message of hope and the Battle of Atollon

Ezra broadcasts a message of hope across the Lothal sector that helps inspire disparate rebel cells to unite, and the growing rebellion survives Grand Admiral Thrawn's assault on Chopper Base at Atollon.

1 BBY - Liberation of Lothal

In the Rebels finale, Ezra helps free his homeworld and defeats Thrawn by summoning purrgil that seize Thrawn's Star Destroyer and jump into hyperspace, carrying both Ezra and Thrawn far beyond the known galaxy.

~5 ABY - Presumed lost, search begins

After the Battle of Endor, an epilogue shows Ezra's comrades — chiefly Sabine Wren — setting out to find him, as his fate remains unknown to the galaxy.

~9 ABY - Found on Peridea and return (Ahsoka)

Ezra is revealed alive on the extragalactic world of Peridea alongside Thrawn; with the help of Sabine Wren and Ahsoka Tano he escapes and returns to the known galaxy during the New Republic era.

birth.source: Wookieepedia and StarWars.com databank: born on Empire Day, which is 19 BBY, to Ephraim and Mira Bridger on Lothal.

death.source: Ezra does not die in canon. Presumed lost after the Rebels finale (1 BBY), he is revealed alive on Peridea and returns to the galaxy in the Ahsoka series (~9 ABY). No death is established.

notes.current: Description ends with his message of hope inspiring rebel cells and does not cover his later story.
notes.suggested: Extend to note his role in the liberation of Lothal, his sacrifice pulling Thrawn into hyperspace via purrgil, and his survival/return on Peridea in the Ahsoka series.
notes.detail: The current summary stops mid-Rebels and omits the two most defining beats of his arc (the Rebels finale sacrifice and his Ahsoka return), which are core to why he matters on a canon timeline.

notes.suggested: Also add Star Wars: Tales of the Empire / other appearances if tracked; consider Forces of Destiny.
notes.detail: Ezra appears beyond the two listed shows (e.g. animated shorts and books). Optional depending on how strictly the timeline plots only major series.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Armitag.description: Armitage Hux was a human male general in the military forces of the First Order during the New Republic Era. The illegitimate son of Imperial Commander Brendol Hux and a kitchen worker on Arkanis, he was raised among the Imperial remnants that fled to the Unknown Regions and grew into a fanatical, ambitious officer. With the backing of Supreme Leader Snoke, Hux took command of the planet-killing Starkiller Base and championed technology that allowed the First Order to track ships through hyperspace. He is best known for his zealous oratory, his bitter power struggle with Kylo Ren, and his ultimate downfall: after Snoke's death and Ren's rise to Supreme Leader, a resentful Hux secretly leaked intelligence to the Resistance as their mole before being exposed and executed by Allegiant General Pryde during the First Order's final campaign.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Armitag.timeline:
~1 BBY - Birth on Arkanis
