Armitage Hux is born on Arkanis, the illegitimate son of Imperial Commander Brendol Hux and a kitchen worker, around the time of the Battle of Yavin.

~5 ABY - Flight to the Unknown Regions

As the Galactic Empire collapses, the young Hux is taken by his father and other Imperial loyalists into the Unknown Regions, where the survivors lay the foundations of the First Order.

~34 ABY - General of the First Order

Hux rises to the rank of general, commanding Starkiller Base and promoting the hyperspace-tracking technology intended to hunt down the Resistance.

34 ABY - Destruction of the Hosnian system

Hux delivers a fiery address and orders Starkiller Base to fire on the New Republic capital in the Hosnian system, before the Resistance destroys the superweapon during their assault.

34 ABY - Snoke's death and Ren's ascension

Aboard the Supremacy, Snoke is killed by Kylo Ren, who seizes the title of Supreme Leader, derailing Hux's ambitions and deepening his resentment toward his rival.

35 ABY - Resistance mole

Sidelined under Ren's rule, Hux secretly begins feeding intelligence to the Resistance, aiding the escape of captured heroes to spite the new Supreme Leader.

35 ABY - Death by execution

Exposed as the First Order's mole, Hux is shot and killed by Allegiant General Pryde aboard a Star Destroyer during the events of the Battle of Exegol campaign.

birth.source: Wookieepedia dates his birth on Arkanis to around the Battle of Yavin; commonly cited as 1 BBY, though 0 BBY also appears. No canon source states an exact year, so it is a derived estimate.

death.source: Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker (35 ABY) — Hux is exposed as the Resistance mole and shot dead by Allegiant General Pryde.

notes.suggested: 1 BBY (estimated)
notes.detail: Automatic check: birth year in data.json disagrees with the year Claude verified. Wookieepedia dates his birth on Arkanis to around the Battle of Yavin; commonly cited as 1 BBY, though 0 BBY also appears. No canon source states an exact year, so it is a derived estimate.

notes.suggested: ~1 BBY, flagged as an estimate (set startYearUnknown: true)
notes.detail: Hux's birth year is not fixed in canon. Wookieepedia places it only 'around the Battle of Yavin,' with 1 BBY most commonly cited and 0 BBY as an alternative. Presenting 0 BBY as a known fact overstates certainty; it should be marked as a guess.

Genuine canon ambiguity: no reference book gives Hux an exact birth year; the value is inferred from his being a young child during the Empire's fall (depicted in the Aftermath novels). 1 BBY vs 0 BBY both circulate.

notes.current: Episode IX: The Rise of Skywalker
notes.suggested: Killed by Allegiant General Pryde in The Rise of Skywalker
notes.detail: Cause of death is specific and worth noting: after being unmasked as the Resistance's spy, Hux is executed by Pryde. This is a distinctive story beat.

notes.suggested: Add rank/affiliation (General, First Order) and parentage (son of Brendol Hux)
notes.detail: His role as First Order General and his lineage as Brendol Hux's illegitimate son are defining facts not currently captured in metadata.

notes.suggested: Verify the Resistance appearance
notes.detail: Armitage Hux's presence in Star Wars Resistance is worth confirming; General Hux does appear/is referenced in the series, but confirm it is Armitage on-screen and not solely dialogue mentions so the appearance list stays accurate.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Boba_Fe.description: Boba Fett is a legendary bounty hunter and one of the most feared figures in the galactic underworld. He is an unaltered, unmodified clone of the Mandalorian bounty hunter Jango Fett, created on Kamino and raised by Jango as his son. Unlike his clone-trooper "brothers," Boba aged at a normal human rate and received no growth acceleration or behavioral conditioning. After witnessing his father's death at the Battle of Geonosis, he grew into a ruthless and highly capable hunter, wearing a customized set of Mandalorian armor and piloting the Firespray-class starship inherited from Jango. During the reign of the Galactic Empire he took contracts for both Darth Vader and the crime lord Jabba the Hutt, most famously capturing Han Solo. Presumed dead after falling into the Sarlacc pit on Tatooine, Boba survived, reclaimed his armor, and years later seized control of Jabba's former criminal empire, becoming the self-styled Daimyo of Mos Espa.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Boba_Fe.timeline:
32 BBY - Creation on Kamino

Boba Fett is born as an unaltered clone of Jango Fett, given to the bounty hunter as unpaid compensation for serving as the genetic template of the Republic's clone army. Jango raises him as his own son on Kamino.

22 BBY - Encounter with Obi-Wan Kenobi

As a young boy, Boba is on Kamino when Jedi Obi-Wan Kenobi investigates the origins of the clone army. He and Jango flee, engaging Obi-Wan's starfighter in a battle over Kamino and later in the asteroid field of Geonosis.

22 BBY - Death of Jango Fett at Geonosis

During the First Battle of Geonosis that ignites the Clone Wars, Boba watches as Jedi Master Mace Windu kills his father. He cradles Jango's empty helmet, an event that shapes his lifelong bitterness.

~21 BBY - Vengeance against Mace Windu

Now orphaned, Boba infiltrates a Republic cruiser under a false identity, seeking revenge on Mace Windu. His plot fails and he is captured, falling in with bounty hunters such as Aurra Sing and Bossk.

~3 BBY - Rise as a bounty hunter

Over the following years Boba builds his reputation across the criminal underworld, taking on his father's armor and ship and becoming one of the galaxy's most feared hunters during the Imperial era.

3 ABY - Hunting the Millennium Falcon

Darth Vader hires Boba among a group of bounty hunters to track the Millennium Falcon. Boba trails the ship to Cloud City on Bespin.

3 ABY - Capturing Han Solo

At Bespin, Boba claims the carbonite-frozen Han Solo as his bounty and transports him aboard his ship to deliver him to Jabba the Hutt on Tatooine.

4 ABY - Fall into the Sarlacc

During the battle at the Great Pit of Carkoon, Boba is knocked into the Sarlacc's pit after Han Solo accidentally strikes his jetpack. He is presumed dead by those present.

~4 ABY - Escape from the Sarlacc

Boba survives the Sarlacc, clawing his way out of the pit, though he loses his armor to Jawa scavengers and is left near death in the Dune Sea.

~9 ABY - Reclaiming his armor

Boba tracks down his Mandalorian armor, which has passed to the Mandalorian Din Djarin, and allies with him to rescue Grogu, reasserting himself as a formidable force on Tatooine.

9 ABY - Daimyo of Mos Espa

Boba seizes Jabba the Hutt's former palace and criminal territory, installing himself as the new crime lord, or Daimyo, of Mos Espa and defending his rule against rival syndicates.

birth.source: Wookieepedia (Boba Fett) states he was born in 32 BBY, like the first generation of clones; note Leland Chee's Holocron gives 31.5 BBY.

death.source: No canon death. Boba survives the Sarlacc and is alive as of 9 ABY in The Mandalorian and The Book of Boba Fett.

notes.suggested: 32 BBY (keep), with a note
notes.detail: Wookieepedia gives 32 BBY, consistent with the first generation of clones, but Leland Chee (keeper of the Holocron continuity database) has stated 31.5 BBY. 32 BBY is the safe, commonly cited value; worth knowing the ~half-year ambiguity exists.

notes.suggested: Kamino (birthplace) — consider noting Tatooine as his adopted base
notes.detail: Kamino is correct as his birthworld. If the timeline wants to reflect his later life, Tatooine is where he operates as Daimyo, but Kamino is accurate for Homeworld.

notes.current: Lists AOTC, Clone Wars, ANH, ESB, ROTJ, The Mandalorian, Book of Boba Fett
notes.suggested: Accurate; optionally add appearance context
notes.detail: Appearances list checks out. Boba appears in Episodes IV-VI, AOTC, The Clone Wars, The Mandalorian S2, and The Book of Boba Fett. No corrections needed.

notes.current: Uses the name 'Slave I' for his ship
notes.suggested: Consider 'Firespray-class ship' phrasing
notes.detail: Lucasfilm has moved away from the 'Slave I' name in newer canon material (referred to simply as Boba Fett's starship / Firespray gunship). Not wrong, but the name is being phased out.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Garazeb.description: Garazeb "Zeb" Orrelios was a male Lasat who served as a Captain of the Lasan Honor Guard before becoming a founding member of the Spectres, the Lothal-based rebel cell that operated aboard the starship Ghost. Trained in the traditional Lasat martial art and armed with his bo-rifle, Zeb was one of the few members of his species to survive the Empire's brutal siege of his homeworld of Lasan, an atrocity that left him with a deep, personal hatred of the Empire and of Agent Kallus in particular. Temperamental, brash, and physically powerful yet possessed of a strong moral compass and quiet compassion, he fought alongside Hera Syndulla, Kanan Jarrus, Ezra Bridger, Sabine Wren, and the droid Chopper throughout the early rebellion against the Galactic Empire. His personal journey reached its emotional peak when he discovered Lira San, the Lasat's ancestral homeworld, learning that his people had not been rendered extinct. After the Galactic Civil War he eventually reconciled with Kallus and guided the reformed Imperial to Lira San, and he continued to live on into the era of the New Republic.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Garazeb.timeline:
44 BBY - Birth on Lasan

Garazeb Orrelios is born on the planet Lasan, homeworld of the Lasat species, roughly twelve years before the Battle of Naboo.

~5 BBY - Captain of the Lasan Honor Guard

Zeb rises to the rank of Captain in the elite Lasan Honor Guard, the warriors sworn to protect the Lasat royal family, and masters the bo-rifle, a weapon unique to the guard.

~5 BBY - The Siege of Lasan

The Galactic Empire, with Agent Kallus among its forces, devastates Lasan and all but exterminates the Lasat. Zeb survives, one of the few of his kind left, and is left carrying survivor's guilt and a burning hatred of the Empire.

~5 BBY - Joining the Ghost crew

Displaced and embittered, Zeb falls in with Hera Syndulla and Kanan Jarrus, becoming a founding member of the Spectres rebel cell operating from the freighter Ghost above Lothal.

~5 BBY - The arrival of Ezra Bridger

The crew takes in the young Lothal street orphan Ezra Bridger; Zeb forms a bickering but affectionate bond with him and continues raiding Imperial supply lines and disrupting Imperial operations.

~4 BBY - Confronting Kallus

Zeb clashes personally with Agent Kallus, the Imperial responsible for atrocities against the Lasat, and the two develop a bitter rivalry that will slowly evolve over the years.

~3 BBY - Discovery of Lira San

Guided by the Lasat mystics Chava and Gron and using his bo-rifle to navigate a collapsed star cluster, Zeb pilots the Ghost to Lira San, the Lasat's original homeworld, and discovers that his people survive there in great numbers, restoring his sense of purpose.

~3 BBY - Stranded with Kallus

Marooned together on an icy moon, Zeb and Kallus are forced to cooperate to survive, beginning the thaw in their relationship that eventually turns Kallus into a rebel informant and ally.

1 BBY - Liberation of Lothal

Zeb takes part in the Spectres' final campaign to free Lothal from Imperial control, one of the rebellion's key victories in the lead-up to the wider Galactic Civil War.

~5 ABY - Return to Lira San with Kallus

After the war, Zeb brings the former Imperial Agent Kallus to Lira San, showing him that he had not truly extinguished the Lasat and completing their reconciliation.

~9 ABY - Appearance during the New Republic era

Now living into the era of the New Republic, Zeb appears alive and well, reflecting his survival long past the fall of the Empire.

birth.source: Wookieepedia (from Ultimate Star Wars reference book), which states he was born on Lasan in 44 BBY, twelve years before the Battle of Naboo (32 BBY).

death.source: Zeb does not die in canon. He survives Star Wars Rebels, settles on Lira San with Kallus after the Galactic Civil War, and appears alive in The Mandalorian (c. 9 ABY).

notes.current: endYear 20 ABY with endYearUnknown true (rendering bound)
notes.suggested: Keep endYearUnknown true; consider extending the column at least to ~9 ABY appearances
notes.detail: Correctly flagged as not a death — Zeb survives all canon appearances. He is alive as of The Mandalorian (c. 9 ABY). The 20 ABY bound is arbitrary but harmless as a rendering limit.

notes.suggested: Add 'Captain of the Lasan Honor Guard' and/or affiliation 'Spectres / Phoenix Cell / New Republic'
notes.detail: His former rank in the Lasan Honor Guard and his role aboard the Ghost are defining to the character and worth surfacing.

notes.suggested: Verify appearance once the film releases
notes.detail: The Mandalorian and Grogu (theatrical, 2026) is expected to feature Zeb but confirm his actual on-screen inclusion before treating it as canon; his The Mandalorian appearance was a live-action cameo in Season 3.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Snoke.description: Snoke was a Force-sensitive humanoid male who reigned as the first Supreme Leader of the First Order during the New Republic era, commanding its military conquest of the galaxy and personally seducing Ben Solo to the dark side as his apprentice Kylo Ren. In reality Snoke was not a natural being but an artificial genetic construct — a "strandcast" — created on the hidden Sith world of Exegol by the resurrected Darth Sidious and his Sith Eternal cultists to serve as a manipulable proxy through which the Emperor could rule the First Order from the shadows. Though engineered as a puppet, Snoke possessed an independent mind and free will, and he is best known for training Kylo Ren, orchestrating the destruction of the New Republic's Hosnian system, and taunting Rey aboard the flagship Supremacy. His reign ended when Kylo Ren, feigning loyalty, cut him in half with Rey's lightsaber — a death that ultimately served Sidious's larger design, as additional Snoke clones remained in stasis on Exegol.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Snoke.timeline:
~4 ABY - Creation on Exegol

In the aftermath of Emperor Palpatine's death at the Battle of Endor, the Sith Eternal on Exegol used cloning and genetic sciences to engineer Snoke as a strandcast — an artificial being designed to act as the resurrected Sidious's proxy in the wider galaxy.

~28-34 ABY - Rise of the First Order

Emerging from the Unknown Regions, Snoke came to rule the First Order as its Supreme Leader, rebuilding Imperial-derived military power while, unknown to most, being influenced from afar by Sidious.

~28-34 ABY - Corruption of Ben Solo

Snoke reached out to the Force-sensitive Ben Solo, son of Han Solo and Leia Organa, turning him against his uncle Luke Skywalker and molding him into the dark-side warrior Kylo Ren.

34 ABY - The Hunt for Luke Skywalker

Appearing as a towering hologram, Snoke directed General Hux and Kylo Ren in the search for the missing Luke Skywalker and oversaw the First Order's assault that destroyed the Hosnian system and the New Republic capital.

34 ABY - Death aboard the Supremacy

Aboard his flagship Supremacy, Snoke tortured the captured Rey and ordered Kylo Ren to kill her. Believing his apprentice fully turned, Snoke was instead betrayed and bisected by Ren, who ignited Rey's lightsaber at his side and seized the mantle of Supreme Leader.

35 ABY - Snoke revealed as a puppet

On Exegol, the surviving Sidious revealed dormant backup Snoke bodies suspended in tanks, confirming that Snoke had always been a manufactured vessel and vessel-line for the Sith Lord's will.

birth.source: Wookieepedia lists Snoke's creation as 4 ABY, tied to the post-Endor rise of the Sith Eternal on Exegol; canon never dramatizes an exact creation date, so the year is a reference-derived approximation rather than an on-screen fact.

death.source: Killed by Kylo Ren aboard the Supremacy in Episode VIII: The Last Jedi, dated 34 ABY per Wookieepedia and the Star Wars canon timeline.

notes.suggested: Keep 4 ABY but consider marking startYearUnknown: true
notes.detail: Canon does not dramatize a precise creation year for Snoke. Wookieepedia's 4 ABY ties to the post-Endor emergence of the Sith Eternal, but the exact year an individual Snoke strandcast was grown is not firmly stated. Presenting it as hard fact overstates canon certainty; it is better treated as an estimate (prefix ~4 ABY).

notes.current: The Force Awakens, The Last Jedi
notes.suggested: Add Episode IX: The Rise of Skywalker
notes.detail: Snoke (as unfinished/backup clone bodies in stasis tanks on Exegol) appears in The Rise of Skywalker, where his true nature as a manufactured Palpatine proxy is revealed. This is a genuine on-screen appearance the timeline currently omits.

notes.suggested: Keep, but note surviving clone bodies
notes.detail: The individual Snoke who ruled the First Order dies in 34 ABY (confirmed, TLJ). However, The Rise of Skywalker shows additional Snoke clones existed, so the 'Snoke' line/identity is not wholly terminated in 34 ABY. The recorded death is correct for the acting Supreme Leader.

notes.suggested: Exegol (place of creation)
notes.detail: Exegol is where Snoke was manufactured rather than a birthworld in the traditional sense; the label is acceptable but worth flagging as 'created on' rather than 'native to.'

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Cad_Ban.description: Cad Bane was a ruthless Duros bounty hunter from the planet Duro, widely regarded as the best and most feared bounty hunter of the Clone Wars era following the death of Jango Fett at the First Battle of Geonosis. Recognizable by his wide-brimmed hat, twin blaster pistols, and cold professionalism, he was repeatedly employed by Darth Sidious for high-risk operations, including a raid on the Jedi Temple to steal a holocron and, later, the kidnapping of Force-sensitive infants. He clashed with Jedi such as Obi-Wan Kenobi, Anakin Skywalker, and Mace Windu, orchestrated the breakout of Ziro the Hutt, and briefly allied with figures like Aurra Sing and Darth Maul. In the war's final months he was hired to recover the enhanced clone Omega, crossing paths with Clone Force 99 and mentoring—and dueling—a young Boba Fett. Decades later, during the era of the New Republic, Bane resurfaced on Tatooine working for the Pyke Syndicate, where he was ultimately killed by his old rival Boba Fett.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Cad_Ban.timeline:
~62 BBY - Birth on Duro

Cad Bane is born a Duros, on or from the planet Duro; the year is derived from his apparent age rather than a directly stated canon date.

~27 BBY - Working the underworld

Bane establishes himself as a hardened bounty hunter, working alongside associates such as Aurra Sing years before the Clone Wars.

22 BBY - Rise to the top

Following Jango Fett's death at the First Battle of Geonosis, Bane becomes recognized as the galaxy's premier bounty hunter.

~22 BBY - Holocron Heist

Hired by Darth Sidious, Bane infiltrates the Jedi Temple, steals a holocron, and later kidnaps Force-sensitive children as part of a Separatist scheme before being captured.

~21 BBY - Senate hostage crisis and Ziro's breakout

Bane seizes hostages within the Galactic Senate to force the release of Ziro the Hutt, then breaks the crime lord out of prison.

~20 BBY - Continued Clone Wars contracts

Bane takes on numerous jobs, dueling Jedi including Obi-Wan Kenobi and Anakin Skywalker and briefly crossing paths with Darth Maul's operations.

~19 BBY - Hunt for Omega

In the final year of the war and its immediate aftermath, Bane is hired to recover the young clone Omega, wounding Fennec Shand and clashing with Clone Force 99.

~19 BBY - Mentor and rival to Boba Fett

Bane encounters and duels a young Boba Fett, seemingly killing him before Boba survives, seeding a lasting rivalry.

9 ABY - Return on Tatooine

Working for the Pyke Syndicate during the Pyke conflict on Tatooine, Bane intimidates and shoots the marshal Cobb Vanth and pressures Boba Fett to abandon his claim.

9 ABY - Death at Boba Fett's hands

In the Battle of Mos Espa, Bane faces Boba Fett in a final showdown and is run through with a Tusken gaderffii stick, ending his life.

birth.source: Wookieepedia and secondary sources list 62 BBY, but this is derived from his stated age (~71 in The Book of Boba Fett, set 9 ABY) rather than a source directly stating the year.

death.source: The Book of Boba Fett (Chapter 7, 'In the Name of Honor'), set in 9 ABY; Bane is run through with a Tusken gaderffii by Boba Fett.

notes.suggested: Consider marking as estimated
notes.detail: No canon source directly states 62 BBY. The figure is back-calculated from his approximate age (~71 during The Book of Boba Fett, set 9 ABY). Wookieepedia and fan sources cite it, but it is derived, not asserted in-universe, so presenting it as hard fact overstates certainty.

notes.suggested: Remove 'Star Wars Rebels'
notes.detail: Cad Bane does not appear in Star Wars Rebels in canon. His canonical appearances are The Clone Wars, The Bad Batch, and The Book of Boba Fett. He was reportedly planned for but never featured in Rebels.

His death in The Book of Boba Fett finale (9 ABY) is depicted on-screen but his body is never explicitly shown dead afterward, and Star Wars has a history of bounty hunters surviving apparent deaths; treat as canonically-dead but note the lingering ambiguity.

notes.suggested: Confirm coverage
notes.detail: The Bad Batch entry is correct—Bane's hunt for Omega and duel with Boba Fett occur there, which is a major late-life arc worth keeping.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Hera_Sy.description: Hera Syndulla is a Twi'lek revolutionary leader and ace pilot who became one of the founding figures of the rebellion against the Galactic Empire and the Alliance to Restore the Republic. Born on Ryloth to the resistance leader General Cham Syndulla and Eleni Syndulla, she witnessed the devastation of the Clone Wars and Imperial occupation firsthand before leaving her homeworld to build her own fight across the galaxy aboard her starship, the Ghost. As commander of the rebel cell known as the Spectres, she led a crew that included the fallen Jedi Kanan Jarrus (who became her partner and the father of her son), the Mandalorian Sabine Wren, the Lasat Garazeb Orrelios, the astromech droid Chopper, and the Force-sensitive Padawan Ezra Bridger. She played key roles in the early insurgency on Lothal, the Battle of Atollon, and the liberation of Lothal, rising to the rank of general. Following the Empire's fall she continued to serve as a general in the New Republic, raising her son Jacen and searching for the missing Ezra Bridger, events central to the Ahsoka series.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Hera_Sy.timeline:
29 BBY - Birth on Ryloth

Hera Syndulla is born on the Twi'lek homeworld of Ryloth to Cham Syndulla, a freedom fighter, and his wife Eleni.

~19 BBY - Growing up amid the Clone Wars

As a child during the Clone Wars, Hera experiences the Separatist occupation of Ryloth and her father's resistance movement, developing her lifelong drive to fight oppression and forming a bond with the droid Chopper.

~11 BBY - Meeting Kanan Jarrus

During the Gorse conflict, Hera crosses paths with Kanan Jarrus, a Jedi who survived Order 66; he later joins her crew aboard the Ghost and becomes her partner.

~5 BBY - The Spectres on Lothal

Leading her rebel cell from the Ghost, Hera recruits the street orphan Ezra Bridger and unites her crew — Kanan, Sabine Wren, Zeb Orrelios, and Chopper — into an insurgency against the Empire on Lothal.

~4 BBY - Joining the wider rebellion

Hera's cell links up with the growing rebel network under Phoenix Squadron and Commander Sato, and she reunites with her father Cham during a mission on Ryloth.

~3 BBY - Battle of Atollon

Hera fights in the disastrous Battle of Atollon against Grand Admiral Thrawn's forces, escaping with a portion of the rebel fleet.

~2 BBY - Promotion and the Liberation of Lothal

Now a rebel general, Hera leads the campaign to free Lothal. During the fighting Kanan Jarrus sacrifices himself to save her and the crew, and Ezra Bridger vanishes into hyperspace with Thrawn.

~1 BBY - Birth of Jacen Syndulla

Hera gives birth to her and Kanan's son, Jacen Syndulla, who later shows an aptitude for the Force.

0 BBY–0 ABY - Galactic Civil War

Hera continues to serve the Alliance to Restore the Republic as a pilot and general through the era of the Battle of Yavin.

4 ABY - Battle of Endor

General Syndulla participates in the Rebel Alliance's decisive victory over the Empire at Endor.

~9 ABY - New Republic general

Serving as a general in the New Republic, Hera raises Jacen, works to convince the Senate of the Imperial remnant and Thrawn threat, and joins Ahsoka Tano and Sabine Wren in the search for the missing Ezra Bridger during the events of the Ahsoka series.

birth.source: Wookieepedia and multiple corroborating references (space.com, StarWars.com Databank) state Hera Syndulla was born in 29 BBY on Ryloth.

death.source: Hera survives all current canon; she is alive and serving as a New Republic general during the Ahsoka series (~9 ABY). No death year exists in canon.

notes.current: Homeworld: Ryloth; Species: Twi'lek; Force Sensitive: No
notes.suggested: Add Rank (General) and consider Affiliation (Rebel Alliance / New Republic)
notes.detail: Hera's rise to general in both the Rebel Alliance and the New Republic is a defining part of her character and is not currently captured in metadata.

notes.current: The Bad Batch, Star Wars Rebels, Ahsoka
notes.suggested: Consider adding Star Wars Rebels: Recon Missions / relevant novels if tracked; core list is accurate
notes.detail: The three plotted appearances are correct. Hera also originates in the novel A New Dawn (Gorse conflict, meeting Kanan) and appears in The Clone Wars-era material via Ryloth, but those are print/secondary and may be out of scope for the timeline.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Mitth'r.description: Thrawn, born Kivu'raw'nuru with the core name Vurawn and known during his early Chiss military career as Mitth'raw'nuru, was a Chiss male officer of the Chiss Ascendancy who became a Grand Admiral in the Imperial Navy during the Galactic Empire. Distinguished by blue skin, glowing red eyes, and an angular face, he was renowned as a brilliant, methodical, and ruthless strategist who believed victory required deeply understanding an enemy's philosophy, art, and culture. After defending the Ascendancy's borders in the Unknown Regions, he allied himself with the Empire, rose swiftly through its ranks under mentors and rivals alike, and was tasked by Governor Arihnda Pryce with crushing the growing rebellion on Lothal. His campaign against the Spectres ended when the purrgil dragged his flagship, the Chimaera, into hyperspace along with Ezra Bridger, stranding him in the distant galaxy of Peridea. Years later he returned to known space amid the events of the Ahsoka series, remaining one of the Empire's most formidable surviving commanders.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Mitth'r.timeline:
~59 BBY - Birth on Rentor

Thrawn is born a Chiss of the Ascendancy on the world of Rentor, given the name Kivu'raw'nuru. The year is not stated in canon and is an estimate drawn from later context.

~19 BBY - Rise in the Chiss Ascendancy

Adopted into the Mitth family and serving as a naval officer, Thrawn defends the Ascendancy against threats in the Unknown Regions, displaying the tactical genius that defines his career.

~19 BBY - Encounter with Anakin Skywalker

During the Clone Wars, Thrawn crosses paths with Jedi General Anakin Skywalker while pursuing a shared threat, an alliance he later recalls.

~18 BBY - Defection to the Galactic Empire

Exiled to the fringes of Chiss space, Thrawn is discovered by Imperial forces and brought before Emperor Palpatine, joining the Empire alongside translator Eli Vanto.

~5 BBY - Promotion to Grand Admiral

Through a string of decisive victories, Thrawn ascends the Imperial hierarchy to become one of only twelve Grand Admirals in the Imperial Navy.

~4 BBY - War against the rebels of Lothal

At Governor Arihnda Pryce's request, Thrawn takes command of efforts to dismantle the rebel cell on Lothal, studying his enemies and hunting the Spectres and the wider rebellion.

~2 BBY - Uncovering Project Stardust and the Bendu

Thrawn presses his campaign against the rebels, contends with the mysterious Bendu on Atollon, and oversees Imperial development projects while closing in on the Lothal resistance.

~1 BBY - Vanishing over Lothal

During the liberation of Lothal, Ezra Bridger lures purrgil to seize Thrawn's flagship, the Chimaera, and jump to hyperspace, carrying Thrawn and Ezra to the distant galaxy of Peridea.

~9 ABY - Return from Peridea

Stranded for years, Thrawn is located by Morgan Elsbeth's allies; during the events of the Ahsoka series he boards the Chimaera and escapes Peridea, returning toward the known galaxy as a renewed threat.

birth.source: Wookieepedia and fan-timeline consensus place his birth on Rentor c. 59 BBY, derived from context clues in Timothy Zahn's novels (e.g. Thrawn ~50 when he meets ~22-year-old Eli Vanto). No canon source states the year explicitly; Zahn deliberately left his age vague.

death.source: Thrawn is alive. He survived Rebels (pulled into hyperspace by purrgil with Ezra Bridger) and returned from Peridea in the Ahsoka series (c. 9 ABY). Canon records no death.

notes.current: absent (birth of 59 BBY presented as known fact)
notes.suggested: true
notes.detail: Canon never states Thrawn's birth year. 59 BBY is a fan/community estimate derived from context clues (Timothy Zahn deliberately left his age vague to allow future stories). The date should be flagged as a guess rather than asserted as known.

notes.suggested: consider noting the Thrawn Ascendancy novel trilogy / Thrawn novels
notes.detail: Thrawn's origin, homeworld (Rentor), and Chiss Ascendancy career come from Zahn's canon novels (Thrawn, Thrawn: Alliances, Thrawn: Treason, and the Ascendancy trilogy), not the shows. These aren't screen appearances but are the primary canon sources for the biographical data you display.

notes.suggested: keep as rendering bound
notes.detail: This is handled correctly — Thrawn does not die. He survives Rebels and returns in Ahsoka (c. 9 ABY) and remains alive. Just confirming the column end is a display bound, not a death.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Third_S.description:
"

Reva Sevander, known as the Third Sister, was a Force-sensitive human female who served the Galactic Empire as a member of the Inquisitorius during the early Imperial Era. Raised as a Jedi initiate in the Jedi Temple on Coruscant, she was one of the few younglings to survive the Temple's fall during Order 66, playing dead after Darth Vader cut down her clan, and she spent the following decade consumed by a desire for revenge against him. Joining the Inquisitorius under Vader's own command, she rose as the ruthless and openly ambitious Third Sister, hunting surviving Jedi while secretly plotting to get close enough to kill her master. In 9 BBY she orchestrated the abduction of Princess Leia Organa to draw the hiding Obi-Wan Kenobi out of exile, struck down the Grand Inquisitor to claim his title, and pursued Kenobi from Daiyu to Mapuzo and Jabiim. When she finally revealed her true intent to Vader, he overpowered her and ran her through with her own lightsaber, but she survived; sent afterward to kill Kenobi's secret charge Luke Skywalker on Tatooine, she found herself unable to murder a child as she had once nearly been murdered, returned the boy to his family, and abandoned the dark side. Her fate beyond that moment is unrecorded in canon.

"
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Third_S.timeline:
"

~31 BBY - Birth

Reva Sevander is born a Force-sensitive human female. Canon never names her birth world, and the year is an approximation based on her apparent age during the Great Jedi Purge.

~31 BBY - Taken into the Jedi Order

Identified as Force-sensitive in infancy, she is brought to the Jedi Temple on Coruscant and raised among the Order's youngest members.

~24 BBY - Training as a Jedi initiate

Reva trains with her clan of younglings in the Temple, studying under Jedi Master Minas Velti alongside friends who would not survive the coming war.

19 BBY - Order 66 and the fall of the Jedi Temple

As the Clone Wars end, Darth Vader and the 501st Legion storm the Jedi Temple. Master Velti is gunned down by clone troopers, and Vader slaughters Reva's clan, stabbing her in the process. She survives by playing dead among the bodies and swears revenge on the man who was Anakin Skywalker.

~19 BBY - Falling to the dark side

Orphaned by the Purge and driven by hatred, Reva turns from the Jedi teachings she was raised on and sets herself on a path toward the very Empire that destroyed her home.

~14 BBY - Joining the Inquisitorius

She is recruited into the Inquisitorius, the Empire's order of dark side Jedi hunters, taking the designation Third Sister and serving directly under Darth Vader — the only position from which she believes she can one day kill him.

9 BBY - The hunt on Tatooine

The Third Sister arrives on Tatooine with the Grand Inquisitor and the Fifth Brother, tracking the fugitive Jedi Nari. Her open aggression and obsession with finding Obi-Wan Kenobi draw the Grand Inquisitor's contempt.

9 BBY - The abduction of Leia Organa

Reva secretly arranges for bounty hunters to kidnap Princess Leia Organa from Alderaan, correctly calculating that the child's connection to Kenobi will force him out of a decade of hiding.

9 BBY - Confrontation on Daiyu

Kenobi surfaces on Daiyu to recover Leia. Reva pursues them through the city, revealing to Obi-Wan that a youngling survived the Temple and that she knows what he did — or failed to do.

9 BBY - Claiming the title of Grand Inquisitor

Confronted over her unsanctioned scheme, Reva strikes down the Grand Inquisitor with her lightsaber and, once her plan delivers Kenobi to Vader, is elevated to Grand Inquisitor in his place. Her predecessor, unknown to her, survives the wound.

9 BBY - Mapuzo and the Path

Reva hunts Kenobi and Leia across the mining world of Mapuzo, where Vader himself joins the pursuit, and later infiltrates the Path's hidden safehouse on Jabiim in her drive to capture the Jedi.

9 BBY - Fortress Inquisitorius and Jabiim

Kenobi's rescue mission into the Fortress Inquisitorius on Nur and the Empire's siege of the Path's refuge on Jabiim leave Reva increasingly isolated, her true motives close to the surface.

9 BBY - Revenge against Vader

Reva finally turns her blade on Darth Vader, declaring herself the youngling he left for dead. Vader overpowers her effortlessly and impales her with her own lightsaber, leaving her to die — but she survives a second time.

9 BBY - Sparing Luke Skywalker on Tatooine

Learning of Vader's hidden son, Reva travels to the Lars homestead on Tatooine and corners the boy Luke Skywalker. Standing over a helpless child with her blade raised, she sees herself in him and cannot strike, returning Luke to Owen and Beru Lars.

9 BBY - Abandoning the dark side

Reva casts aside her Inquisitor lightsaber in the Tatooine desert, telling Kenobi she has become what she hated and choosing to walk away. Canon records nothing of her life afterward, and she is not known to have died.

"

birth.source: Wookieepedia's "Reva Sevander" article describes her as "born around 31 BBY" — an approximation derived from her being a Jedi initiate old enough to train with her clan under Master Minas Velti at the fall of the Jedi Temple in 19 BBY. No in-universe source states an exact year.

death.source: Reva survives the events of Obi-Wan Kenobi (9 BBY). She is last seen alive on Tatooine after sparing Luke Skywalker and discarding her lightsaber. No canon source records a death.

notes.suggested: 31 BBY (estimated)

No in-universe source states an exact year.

The guess flag should stay — no source gives an exact year — but 31 BBY is the sourced approximation.

notes.suggested: https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Reva_Sevander

notes.detail: "Unknown" is technically correct — canon never names her birth world — but she was taken into the Jedi Order as an infant and raised in the Jedi Temple on Coruscant, which is worth surfacing rather than leaving blank.

notes.detail: Her Inquisitor rank is the single most identifying fact about her and is only mentioned in prose.

notes.detail: The current text is lifted from an early-season summary and stops before everything the character is actually known for, including her survival and turn away from the dark side.

notes.suggested: Keep unknown; consider clamping the column nearer 9 BBY
notes.detail: Correctly flagged as not a death — she survives the series. But she has no canon appearances after 9 BBY, so drawing her column out to 29 ABY implies a presence in the galaxy that no source supports.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Rey_Sky.description: Rey Skywalker was a human female scavenger from the desert world of Jakku who rose to become one of the last and most powerful Jedi of her era, fighting for the Resistance against the First Order during the final days of the New Republic Era. Abandoned on Jakku as a child by parents desperate to hide her from her grandfather, the resurrected Emperor Sheev Palpatine, she survived for years salvaging wreckage before her encounter with the droid BB-8 drew her into galaxy-spanning events. Strong in the Force, she sought out the exiled Luke Skywalker for training and formed a rare bond known as a dyad in the Force with Ben Solo, the fallen Jedi known as Kylo Ren. Tempted by the dark side yet ultimately choosing the light, Rey confronted and destroyed Palpatine on Exegol, ending the Sith once and for all with Ben's sacrifice. Rejecting the Palpatine name, she took the surname Skywalker in honor of her masters Luke and Leia, becoming heir to their legacy and pledging to rebuild the Jedi Order.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Rey_Sky.timeline:
15 ABY - Birth

Rey is born to Dathan — a genetic "son" and failed clone of Emperor Palpatine — and his wife Miramir, making her the granddaughter of Sheev Palpatine.

~19 ABY - Abandoned on Jakku

To keep her hidden from Palpatine and his Sith Eternal cultists, Rey's parents leave her in the care of the junk boss Unkar Plutt on Jakku and flee, later being killed. Rey grows up alone as a scavenger, awaiting a return that never comes.

34 ABY - The Force Awakens

Rey takes in the droid BB-8, flees Jakku aboard the Millennium Falcon with the former stormtrooper Finn, and experiences a Force awakening. After Han Solo's death and a duel with Kylo Ren on Starkiller Base, she travels to Ahch-To to find Luke Skywalker.

34 ABY - The Last Jedi

Rey trains briefly under a reluctant Luke Skywalker and develops a mysterious Force-bond with Kylo Ren. She fails to turn Ben Solo back to the light and rejoins the battered Resistance, helping them escape Crait.

35 ABY - The Rise of Skywalker

Rey learns she is Palpatine's granddaughter and confronts the resurrected Emperor on Exegol. Drawing on the strength of past Jedi, she destroys Palpatine, dying briefly before Ben Solo revives her through their dyad bond at the cost of his own life.

35 ABY - Taking the Skywalker Name

Returning to the abandoned Lars homestead on Tatooine, Rey buries the Skywalker lightsabers and, when asked her name, declares herself Rey Skywalker — embracing the legacy of her masters over her Palpatine bloodline.

birth.source: Wookieepedia 'Rey Skywalker' article states she was born in 15 ABY

death.source: Rey survives the events of The Rise of Skywalker (35 ABY); canon records no death. She momentarily dies on Exegol but is revived by Ben Solo.

notes.suggested: Clarify she is Palpatine's granddaughter
notes.detail: Rey is the granddaughter of Sheev Palpatine, not his direct child. Her father, Dathan, was a non-Force-sensitive genetic clone/'son' of Palpatine. 'Born a Palpatine' is loosely accurate but the granddaughter relationship is the precise canon detail.

notes.suggested: Jakku (adoptive homeworld)
notes.detail: Jakku is where Rey was raised and is commonly listed as her homeworld, but she was born elsewhere and left on Jakku as a young child. Canon does not firmly establish her birthworld. Listing Jakku is defensible but worth flagging as her adoptive home rather than birthplace.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Kaydel_.description: Kaydel Ko Connix was a human female who served the Resistance during its war against the First Order. Born on Dulathia around 13 ABY, she joined the Resistance and became an operations controller in Fleet Command, working under General Leia Organa at the base on D'Qar. She coordinated communications during the assault on Starkiller Base, after which she was promoted to lieutenant. During the subsequent Resistance evacuation she helped organize the withdrawal from D'Qar, served aboard the flagship Raddus, and joined Poe Dameron's mutiny against Vice Admiral Amilyn Holdo before surviving the Battle of Crait. By the time of the war's climax she had risen to the rank of commander and took part in the final campaign against the Sith Eternal at the Battle of Exegol, which she survived. She is remembered as one of the dedicated officers of Leia Organa's command staff who helped keep the Resistance alive through its darkest years.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Kaydel_.timeline:
~13 ABY - Birth on Dulathia

Kaydel Ko Connix is born on the planet Dulathia. Her birth year is derived from her stated age of twenty-two during 35 ABY, not from a directly recorded date.

~34 ABY - Serving in Fleet Command on D'Qar

Connix serves as an operations controller in Fleet Command at the Resistance's D'Qar headquarters, working under General Leia Organa.

34 ABY - Coordinating the Starkiller Base raid

During the war with the First Order, Connix coordinates communications for the Resistance assault on Starkiller Base. Her service leads to a promotion to lieutenant.

34 ABY - Evacuation of D'Qar and the Holdo mutiny

Connix helps organize the evacuation of D'Qar ahead of the First Order and serves aboard the flagship Raddus. During the pursuit she joins Poe Dameron's mutiny against Vice Admiral Amilyn Holdo, and afterward survives the Battle of Crait.

35 ABY - Battle of Exegol

Now holding the rank of commander, Connix takes part in the Resistance's final campaign against the First Order and the Sith Eternal at the Battle of Exegol, which the Resistance wins. She survives the battle.

birth.source: Not stated directly in canon. Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary gives her age as 22 during the events of Episode IX (35 ABY), yielding a birth year of approximately 13 ABY.

death.source: Connix does not die in canon. She survives the Battle of Crait (The Last Jedi) and the Battle of Exegol (The Rise of Skywalker); no death is recorded.

notes.current: birth year 13 ABY presented as known (startYearUnknown absent/false)
notes.suggested: set startYearUnknown: true
notes.detail: The 13 ABY birth year is not stated in canon. It is derived from her stated age of 22 in The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary during 35 ABY. The value is a sound estimate, but the app currently presents it as fact; it should be flagged as a guess/estimate.

notes.current: The Force Awakens, The Last Jedi, The Rise of Skywalker
notes.suggested: Consider noting her rank progression and role
notes.detail: seenIn is accurate. Worth tracking that she rose from Fleet Command controller to lieutenant (after Starkiller) to commander (by The Rise of Skywalker) — her rank/title changes across the films.

notes.current: Description ends at her D'Qar operations-controller role, describing only her earliest status.
notes.suggested: Extend to cover her promotions and survival through the Battle of Exegol.
notes.detail: The current description stops at The Force Awakens-era context and omits her larger arc across The Last Jedi and The Rise of Skywalker (evacuation of D'Qar, the Holdo mutiny, Crait, and her rise to commander at Exegol).

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Carson_.description: Carson Teva was a human male pilot and officer who fought for the Alliance to Restore the Republic during the Galactic Civil War and afterward served as a captain in the Adelphi Rangers, a starfighter squadron of the New Republic Defense Fleet's Starfighter Corps based out of Adelphi Base in the Outer Rim. Flying a T-65B X-wing, he became one of the few New Republic officers willing to treat the lawless Outer Rim as the Republic's problem rather than a distant nuisance, and he repeatedly clashed with a bureaucracy in the Core that dismissed reports of surviving Imperial warlords. He first crossed paths with the Mandalorian bounty hunter Din Djarin around 9 ABY over the Razor Crest's dead transponder, and thereafter became a recurring — and increasingly sympathetic — ally, investigating the massacre of Imperial remnant survivors on Morak, the Marshal Cara Dune's jurisdictional troubles on Nevarro, and Imperial-linked wreckage that Coruscant refused to take seriously. His warnings about a resurgent Imperial threat were vindicated as the Shadow Council and Grand Admiral Thrawn's return came to light; he supported Greef Karga and the Mandalorians during the fighting at Nevarro, and later backed General Hera Syndulla, testifying on her behalf before a New Republic tribunal on Coruscant and quietly aiding her unsanctioned search for Ezra Bridger. Teva is not recorded as having died in canon and remains alive as of the latest events in which he appears.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Carson_.timeline:
Unknown - Birth

Carson Teva was born a human male at an unrecorded point before the Galactic Civil War; canon establishes no birth year or homeworld for him. Our timeline's placement is a positioning guess only.

Between 5 BBY and 4 ABY - Service with the Rebel Alliance

Teva fought for the Alliance to Restore the Republic against the Galactic Empire during the Galactic Civil War, an experience that shaped his lifelong distrust of Imperial remnants.

After 4 ABY - Commission in the New Republic

Following the Empire's defeat, Teva joined the New Republic Defense Fleet's Starfighter Corps, rose to the rank of captain, and was assigned to the Adelphi Rangers, patrolling the Outer Rim from Adelphi Base in a T-65B X-wing.

9 ABY - Intercepting the Razor Crest

While patrolling the Outer Rim with fellow pilot Trapper Wolf, Teva stopped the gunship Razor Crest over its inactive transponder. After linking its pilot, Din Djarin, to a breakout aboard a New Republic Correctional Transport, the pilots pursued him to Maldo Kreis, but let him go in exchange for his help capturing the escaped prisoners.

9 ABY - Investigating the Morak massacre

Teva investigated the wreckage of an Imperial refinery transport on Morak and recovered a Mandalorian-linked lead, taking his findings to Marshal Cara Dune on Nevarro and urging the New Republic to recognize the surviving Imperial threat.

9 ABY - The prisoner on Nevarro

Teva brought Marshal Dune an assignment concerning a wanted prisoner, drawing her into a matter with personal stakes and further entangling Nevarro's local authority with New Republic business.

9 ABY - Recruiting Din Djarin

With New Republic resources stretched thin across the Outer Rim, Teva approached Din Djarin about working as an independent contractor for the Rangers, an offer Djarin declined while still cooperating with him informally.

9 ABY - Ignored warnings on Coruscant

Teva presented evidence of coordinated Imperial remnant activity to New Republic Command on Coruscant, only to be brushed aside by an amnesty-program bureaucracy that refused to believe a remnant threat still existed.

9 ABY - Nevarro relief

When Nevarro and its Mandalorian allies came under attack by Imperial forces, Teva and the Adelphi Rangers responded, vindicating his long-dismissed warnings about a resurgent Empire.

9 ABY - Supporting General Syndulla

Teva backed General Hera Syndulla's pursuit of leads on Grand Admiral Thrawn's return, including her unsanctioned mission to Seatos, and testified in her defense when she faced a New Republic tribunal on Coruscant for acting without authorization.

After 9 ABY - Fate

Teva survived all recorded events and no death is established for him in canon; his later career remains unchronicled.

notes.suggested: Keep the unknown flag; consider a later guess such as ~30 BBY
notes.detail: Canon gives Carson Teva no birth year at all — no film, series, novel or reference work states one, so flagging it as a guess is correct. However, 42 BBY would make him roughly 51 during the 9 ABY events, which sits oddly with his portrayal. The only hard constraint is that he was old enough to serve the Rebel Alliance during the Galactic Civil War (so born no later than roughly 18-20 BBY). A guess in the low 30s BBY fits the depiction better, but either way it stays a guess.

notes.current: Description stops at the Chapter 10 Razor Crest transponder encounter and calls him a Rebel Alliance captain
notes.suggested: Replace with a summary covering his full arc through The Mandalorian season 3 and Ahsoka
notes.detail: The current text covers only his first scene and omits everything he is actually known for: the Morak/Nevarro investigations, recruiting Din Djarin as an independent contractor, his repeatedly ignored warnings to New Republic Command about the Imperial remnant, his role at Nevarro, and his support of Hera Syndulla at her tribunal. It also implies 'captain' was his Rebel Alliance rank; the captaincy is his New Republic rank in the Adelphi Rangers.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Din_Dja.description: Din Djarin, known to most simply as "the Mandalorian" or "Mando," was a human bounty hunter of the New Republic Era, recognizable by his beskar armor, IB-94 blaster pistol, Amban phase-pulse rifle, and the helmet he vowed never to remove. Orphaned as a child on Aq Vetina when Separatist battle droids overran his village during the Clone Wars, he was rescued by Mandalorians and raised on Concordia as a foundling of the Children of the Watch, an orthodox splinter sect whose creed he took to be the way of all Mandalorians. By 9 ABY he was a hardened hunter working out of the covert on Nevarro, where a commission from an Imperial remnant led him to a Force-sensitive infant of Yoda's species — later known as Grogu — whom he refused to hand over, becoming a fugitive and the child's adoptive father. His search for the Jedi took him across the galaxy alongside allies including Greef Karga, Cara Dune, Bo-Katan Kryze, Ahsoka Tano, Boba Fett, and Fennec Shand, and culminated in Grogu's rescue from Moff Gideon and the child's brief tutelage under Luke Skywalker. In defeating Gideon he unknowingly won the Darksaber, and though he ceded its symbolic claim to Bo-Katan Kryze, he helped unite the fractured Mandalorian clans, redeem himself in the Living Waters of Mandalore, and lead the reconquest of their homeworld — afterward settling on Nevarro as a New Republic marshal with Grogu, whom he formally adopted as his apprentice.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Din_Dja.timeline:
~30 BBY - Birth on Aq Vetina

Din Djarin is born to human parents in a village on the Outer Rim world of Aq Vetina during the final years of the Galactic Republic. Canon never states the year; it is inferred from his apparent age during the Clone Wars-era attack on his home.

~22 BBY - Separatist droids destroy his village

During the Clone Wars, Separatist battle droids overrun Aq Vetina. Din's parents hide him in a cellar and are killed above him; a super battle droid finds the boy but he is saved at the last moment by armored Mandalorians who descend from the sky.

~22 BBY - Taken in as a foundling

Rescued and carried away, Din is brought to Concordia and raised as a foundling by the Children of the Watch, an orthodox splinter sect. He is trained in the Mandalorian fighting arts and swears the Creed, vowing never to remove his helmet before another living being.

~19 BBY-9 ABY - Life in the Tribe

Djarin comes of age within the Watch, eventually operating from a hidden covert on Nevarro. He becomes a bounty hunter with the Bounty Hunters' Guild under Greef Karga, flying the gunship Razor Crest and returning a share of his earnings to the covert.

9 ABY - The commission for the Child

Hired on Nevarro by an Imperial remnant client and Doctor Pershing, Djarin tracks a fifty-year-old asset to Arvala-7, where with the help of the Ugnaught Kuiil and the reprogrammed droid IG-11 he finds an infant of Yoda's species.

9 ABY - Rescue of the Child and flight from Nevarro

After delivering the Child and receiving a camtono of beskar, Djarin turns on his employers and steals the infant back. His covert is exposed defending his escape, and he becomes a hunted man across the Outer Rim.

9 ABY - On the run

Djarin hides on Sorgan, is ambushed by fellow hunters, is betrayed during a heist on an Imperial prison ship, and is nearly killed by the mercenary Fennec Shand's associates on Tatooine — all while keeping the Child alive.

9 ABY - The battle for Nevarro

Returning to Nevarro at Karga's request, Djarin is drawn into a trap by Moff Gideon. Kuiil is killed and IG-11 sacrifices itself, removing Djarin's helmet to heal him — the first time anyone sees his face since he took the Creed. He is charged by the Armorer with reuniting the Child with its own kind.

9 ABY - Searching for the Jedi

Djarin's quest takes him to Tatooine, to Trask where he meets Bo-Katan Kryze and learns the Children of the Watch are a cult rather than mainstream Mandalorians, and to Corvus, where Ahsoka Tano tells him the Child's name is Grogu and sends him to the seeing stone on Tython.

9 ABY - Tython and the loss of the Razor Crest

At the ruins on Tython, Grogu reaches out through the Force while Djarin fends off Boba Fett and Fennec Shand. Moff Gideon's dark troopers seize Grogu and Gideon's cruiser destroys the Razor Crest, leaving Djarin without his ship.

9 ABY - Rescue of Grogu and the Darksaber

With Fett, Shand, Kryze, Koska Reeves and Cara Dune, Djarin boards Gideon's cruiser, destroys the dark troopers, and defeats Gideon in single combat — unknowingly winning the Darksaber and its claim to rule Mandalore. Luke Skywalker arrives to take Grogu for training, and Djarin removes his helmet to say goodbye.

9 ABY - The battle of Mos Espa

Recruited by Boba Fett, Djarin fights alongside him against the Pyke Syndicate on Tatooine. He acquires a Naboo N-1 starfighter to replace the Razor Crest, is exiled by the Armorer for having bared his face, and is reunited with Grogu, who chooses him over the Jedi path.

9 ABY - Redemption in the Living Waters

Seeking atonement, Djarin travels to the ruins of Mandalore and descends into the mines beneath the Civic Center. He bathes in the Living Waters and is pulled out by Bo-Katan Kryze, who in doing so sees the mythosaur below; both are declared redeemed by the Armorer.

9 ABY - Uniting the clans

Djarin and Kryze bring the Children of the Watch and the exiled Nite Owls together. Djarin gives the Darksaber to Kryze, telling the covert she recovered it from an enemy and so returned it freely — clearing the way for her to lead.

9 ABY - The retaking of Mandalore

The united clans invade Mandalore, are ambushed by Moff Gideon's remnant and his beskar-armored praetorian guard, and fight their way through. Gideon is killed, Mandalore is reclaimed for resettlement, and Djarin's ally Paz Vizsla falls in the battle.

9 ABY - Marshal of Nevarro

Djarin takes a commission as an independent contractor for the New Republic under Captain Carson Teva, formally adopts Grogu as his apprentice — "Din Grogu" — and settles into a cabin on Nevarro, taking bounty work as it comes.

birth.source: Wookieepedia's Din Djarin article gives no birth year, stating only that he was born prior to 19 BBY on Aq Vetina 'in the waning years of the Galactic Republic.' Derived range: the Separatist droid attack on his village occurred during the Clone Wars (22-19 BBY) and he appears to be roughly 8-10 years old in that flashback, putting his birth c. 32-27 BBY. 30 BBY sits comfortably in that window.

death.source: No death is recorded in canon. Din Djarin survives the retaking of Mandalore in 9 ABY and continues on as of The Book of Boba Fett and the announced The Mandalorian and Grogu.

notes.suggested: Keep 30 BBY as a guess
notes.detail: Canon never states a birth year. Wookieepedia only says he was born before 19 BBY on Aq Vetina, in the waning years of the Republic. Working back from the Clone Wars-era droid attack (22-19 BBY) and his apparent age of roughly 8-10 in that flashback gives c. 32-27 BBY. 30 BBY is a fair midpoint and the 'this is a guess' flag is correctly set - no change needed, but it should never be presented as fact.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Plo_Koo.description:
"

Plo Koon was a male Kel Dor Jedi Master from the planet Dorin who served on the Jedi High Council through the final decades of the Galactic Republic. Renowned for his level-headed judgment, compassion and unflappable calm, he wore an antiox breath mask and protective goggles to survive in oxygen-rich atmospheres, and he was a gifted pilot and swordsman who also commanded the rare Force technique known as Electric Judgment. He sat on the Council during the Naboo Crisis and fought at the First Battle of Geonosis that opened the Clone Wars, then served as a Jedi General leading the 104th Battalion — the \"Wolfpack\" — alongside Clone Commander Wolffe, with whom he shared an unusually close and respectful bond, insisting that clones be treated as men rather than materiel. Among his most enduring legacies was discovering the Force-sensitive Togruta child Ahsoka Tano on Shili and bringing her to the Jedi Temple, remaining a mentor and protector to her for the rest of his life. He survived the destruction of his fleet by General Grievous's warship Malevolence, took part in the rescue of Even Piell from the Citadel, and fought across countless campaigns before Order 66 in 19 BBY, when the clone pilots escorting him over the bridge cities of Cato Neimoidia turned on their general and shot down his starfighter, killing him instantly.

"
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Plo_Koo.timeline:
"

74 BBY - Birth on Dorin

Plo Koon is born a member of the Kel Dor species on Dorin, a world whose helium- and gas-rich atmosphere means he must wear an antiox mask and goggles anywhere else in the galaxy.

~36 BBY - Finding Ahsoka Tano on Shili

Sent by the Jedi Order to investigate a powerful Force signature, Plo Koon locates the infant Togruta Ahsoka Tano in the wilds of Shili and brings her to the Jedi Temple on Coruscant, beginning a lifelong bond as her protector.

32 BBY - The Naboo Crisis

Now a member of the Jedi High Council, Plo Koon is present when Qui-Gon Jinn presents Anakin Skywalker for testing and when the Council debates the return of the Sith after the Battle of Naboo.

22 BBY - The First Battle of Geonosis

Plo Koon joins the two-hundred-strong Jedi strike team that descends on Geonosis to rescue Obi-Wan Kenobi, Anakin Skywalker and Padme Amidala, surviving the arena battle that ignites the Clone Wars.

22 BBY - General of the 104th Battalion

Given command of the clone troopers of the 104th Battalion, nicknamed the Wolfpack, Plo Koon forges a deep partnership with Commander Wolffe and insists his men be regarded as individuals rather than expendable soldiers.

22 BBY - Destruction of his fleet by the Malevolence

Plo Koon's task force is annihilated by General Grievous's ion-cannon warship Malevolence near Abregado; adrift in an escape pod with a handful of survivors including Wolffe, he is rescued by Anakin Skywalker and Ahsoka Tano and helps expose the weapon's nature.

~22 BBY - Defense of Kamino

Alongside Obi-Wan Kenobi, Plo Koon helps anticipate and repel a Separatist assault on the cloning facilities of Kamino led by Grievous and Asajj Ventress.

~21 BBY - The Citadel

Plo Koon takes part in the Republic operation to extract Jedi Master Even Piell from the Separatist prison fortress on Lola Sayu, commanding the relief force that recovers the survivors.

22-19 BBY - Campaigns of the Clone Wars

Across the war Plo Koon leads front-line actions from Felucia to the Outer Rim while continuing to serve on the Jedi High Council, and stands among the Masters who wrestle with Ahsoka Tano's expulsion and vindication.

19 BBY - Death over Cato Neimoidia

While patrolling the bridge cities of Cato Neimoidia in his Jedi starfighter, Plo Koon is caught by Order 66 when the clone pilots of his own escort open fire; his fighter is destroyed and he is killed in the crash, one of the first Jedi to fall in the purge.

"

birth.source: Wookieepedia's canon Plo Koon article gives his birth as 55 BFE on Dorin, sourced to the reference book Star Wars: Timelines (2023). Wookieepedia's own 'ABY-BBY' dating system article establishes the Imperial calendar epoch (formation of the Empire) as 19 BBY, so BFE years convert by adding 19: 55 BFE = 74 BBY.

death.source: Wookieepedia and StarWars.com Databank: Plo Koon was killed during Order 66 in 19 BBY over Cato Neimoidia, shot down in his Jedi starfighter by the clone pilots of his own 104th Battalion, as depicted in Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith.

notes.detail: Canon now dates Plo Koon's birth: Wookieepedia's canon article gives 55 BFE on Dorin, sourced to the reference book Star Wars: Timelines (2023). The Imperial calendar epoch is the Empire's formation in 19 BBY, so 55 BFE = 74 BBY. Our 100 BBY guess is 26 years too early and no longer needs to be a guess.
With a confirmed 74 BBY birth, the column can simply start at the real birth year; no clamping is needed since 74 BBY is well within the timeline range used for other prequel-era Jedi.

notes.detail: Plo Koon appears in the Tales of the Jedi episode "Life and Death," arriving on Shili to take the infant Ahsoka Tano to the Jedi Order — the canon depiction of his most story-significant act, which our timeline currently does not plot.

The 74 BBY figure exists only in a reference book (Star Wars: Timelines) and only in the Imperial BFE calendar, requiring a 19-year conversion; no on-screen source states his age. It is solid but worth knowing it is book-derived rather than dramatized. Separately, Legends material had Plo Koon discovering Ahsoka as a three-year-old rather than an infant on Shili — the canon Tales of the Jedi version supersedes it.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Owen_La.description: Owen Lars was a human male moisture farmer who worked the Lars homestead in the Great Chott salt flat on the desert world of Tatooine. The son of Cliegg Lars, he became stepbrother to the young Jedi Anakin Skywalker when Cliegg freed and married Anakin's mother, Shmi Skywalker. Owen met Anakin in 22 BBY, when the Padawan came to Tatooine searching for Shmi after she had been abducted by Tusken Raiders; Anakin recovered her body only for her to die in his arms, and Cliegg — crippled in the failed rescue attempt — did not long outlive her. Owen and his partner Beru Whitesun married and kept the farm running, and in 19 BBY, after the fall of the Republic and Anakin's turn to Darth Vader, Obi-Wan Kenobi brought them Anakin's infant son Luke Skywalker to raise in obscurity. Fiercely protective and deeply wary of the Jedi legacy that had destroyed his family, Owen kept Luke bound to the farm and told him his father had been a spice-freighter navigator, even warning Kenobi away from the boy. In 0 BBY, Imperial stormtroopers tracking the astromech droid R2-D2 and the stolen Death Star plans came to the homestead and killed Owen and Beru — a loss that severed Luke's last tie to Tatooine and set him on the path to becoming a Jedi.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Owen_La.timeline:
~52 BBY - Birth

Owen was born to Cliegg Lars, a Tatooine moisture farmer. No canon source fixes the year; 52 BBY is a long-standing reference-work figure inherited from earlier material.

~32 BBY - Shmi Skywalker joins the Lars family

Cliegg Lars purchased Shmi Skywalker from the Mos Espa junk dealer Watto, freed her, and married her, making Owen the stepson of Shmi and stepbrother of Anakin Skywalker, whom he had not yet met.

22 BBY - Shmi's abduction and death

Tusken Raiders seized Shmi from the homestead. Cliegg lost a leg in the failed rescue, and Anakin Skywalker arrived with Padmé Amidala searching for his mother — the first time Owen met his stepbrother. Anakin found Shmi in the Tusken camp, where she died in his arms, and she was buried at the homestead.

~22 BBY - Death of Cliegg Lars

Broken in body and spirit after Shmi's death, Owen's father died, leaving Owen and Beru Whitesun to run the farm alone.

19 BBY - The Lars family takes in Luke Skywalker

Following the fall of the Republic and Anakin's fall to the dark side as Darth Vader, Obi-Wan Kenobi delivered the infant Luke Skywalker to Owen and Beru on Tatooine. Owen accepted the child at Beru's urging and raised him as a nephew, hiding his parentage.

9 BBY - Guarding Luke from the Empire

With Obi-Wan Kenobi living as a hermit nearby, Owen rebuffed the former Jedi's attempts to train or contact Luke. When Inquisitors and bounty hunters hunting Kenobi came to Tatooine, Owen and Beru armed themselves and defended their homestead and the boy.

~0 BBY - Keeping Luke on the farm

Owen repeatedly delayed Luke's applications to the Imperial Academy and to piloting work, insisting he was needed for another season of harvest.

0 BBY - Purchase of C-3PO and R2-D2

Owen bought the protocol droid C-3PO and the astromech R2-D2 from Jawa traders, unaware that R2-D2 carried the stolen Death Star plans and a message from Princess Leia Organa for Obi-Wan Kenobi.

0 BBY - Death at the Lars homestead

Imperial stormtroopers tracing the droids' sale reached the homestead and killed Owen and Beru, burning their bodies and the farm. Luke found their remains on his return, and with nothing left on Tatooine, he left with Kenobi.

birth.source: Wookieepedia's Owen Lars article gives 52 BBY, but no canon film, series, or reference book states his birth year on-screen; the figure traces back to Legends-era material (where Owen was born in 52 BBY on Ator to Aika and Cliegg Lars) and is carried over by fan reference. Treat as a well-established estimate, not a hard canon date.

death.source: Wookieepedia and StarWars.com Databank: Owen and Beru Lars were killed by Imperial stormtroopers searching for R2-D2 during the events of Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope, dated 0 BBY.

notes.suggested: Tatooine
notes.detail: Ator is a Legends-only detail: in the old Expanded Universe Owen was born on the Core World Ator, homeworld of his birth mother Aika Lars, and returned to Tatooine as a small child. Aika and Ator have no canon existence. Every canon source (StarWars.com Databank, Wookieepedia's canon article) presents Owen simply as a human male of Tatooine.

notes.suggested: -52 with startYearUnknown: true

It is a reasonable estimate (it makes him roughly 30 at Attack of the Clones and ~52 at his death), but it should not be presented to users as established canon.

notes.suggested: Drop Aika (Legends only) and continue through the adoption of Luke in 19 BBY, the Obi-Wan Kenobi era, and the couple's deaths in 0 BBY.
notes.detail: The current text names Aika, a Legends-only character, and stops in 22 BBY — omitting the single most important thing Owen is known for, raising Luke Skywalker, and his death at the hands of stormtroopers.
