https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Marrok.description: Marrok was a mysterious, heavily armored Force-sensitive humanoid warrior who served the Galactic Empire's Inquisitorius — reportedly under the designation First Brother — after training as a member of the Jedi Order before the fall of the Republic. Inducted into the Inquisitorius before Darth Vader alongside Barriss Offee, the Fourth Sister and the Eleventh Brother in the early Imperial Era, he hunted surviving Jedi and, with the Eleventh Brother, pursued the crime lord and former Sith Lord Maul during Imperial operations on the Mid Rim world of Janix. He is best known for his silent, brooding presence in the New Republic era, when he served the Nightsister Morgan Elsbeth as a mercenary alongside the fallen Jedi Baylan Skoll and his apprentice Shin Hati, wielding a scarred, sputtering red blade in Elsbeth's search for the exiled Grand Admiral Thrawn. Marrok was cut down by Ahsoka Tano on Seatos in 9 ABY, and rather than dying as an ordinary man, his armor burst with thick green smoke as his body shriveled away — strongly implying he had been sustained or reanimated by Nightsister magick, leaving his true identity and the true moment of his death among the most persistent unanswered mysteries in current canon.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Marrok.timeline:
Unknown - Birth

Marrok's birth year, homeworld and species are never established in canon; he is identified only as a male humanoid. He was born some decades before the Clone Wars, since he was old enough to have trained with the Jedi Order before its fall.

Before 19 BBY - Service in the Jedi Order

Marrok was a member of the Jedi Order prior to the rise of the Empire, making him one of the Jedi who survived Order 66 and later turned against his own kind.

~18 BBY - Inducted into the Inquisitorius

In the early Imperial Era, Marrok knelt before Darth Vader as he was inducted into the Inquisitorius, alongside Barriss Offee, the Fourth Sister and the Eleventh Brother.

~10 BBY - Hunting Maul on Janix

Serving the Empire as an Inquisitor, Marrok worked with the Eleventh Brother during operations on the Mid Rim world of Janix, pursuing the crime lord and former Sith Lord Maul. He reported to a cloaked superior he addressed only as "my lord."

After 4 ABY - Mercenary for Morgan Elsbeth

Following the Empire's collapse, Marrok was hired by the Nightsister Morgan Elsbeth, serving in her forces alongside the Force-sensitive mercenaries Baylan Skoll and Shin Hati.

9 ABY - Ambush on Arcana

Marrok shadowed Ahsoka Tano and Huyang on Arcana, where Elsbeth's people recovered a star map pointing the way to the distant galaxy holding Grand Admiral Thrawn.

9 ABY - Duel on Seatos and death

On the forest world of Seatos, Marrok fought Ahsoka Tano and was struck down by her blade. His armor split open and released a burst of thick green smoke as his body withered and collapsed, suggesting he had been animated or sustained by Nightsister magick. His identity was never revealed.

death.source: Wookieepedia "Marrok" (via search summary) and the Ahsoka episode "Shadow Warrior": Marrok is struck down by Ahsoka Tano on Seatos during the events of Ahsoka, which is set in 9 ABY.

notes.suggested: ["Ahsoka", "Star Wars: Tales of the Empire", "Star Wars: Maul - Shadow Lord"]
notes.detail: Marrok's earliest chronological appearance is in Tales of the Empire, where he kneels before Darth Vader during the Inquisitorius induction scene alongside Barriss Offee, the Fourth Sister and the Eleventh Brother. We do not track that appearance.

notes.suggested: keep startYearUnknown: true, but consider a later guess such as -40
notes.detail: Canon gives no birth year at all, so the unknown flag is correct. However 50 BBY is a fairly aggressive guess: he was inducted into the Inquisitorius around 18 BBY as a former Jedi, and nothing indicates he was in his early 30s at that point rather than younger. A guess in the 40s-30s BBY range would be at least as defensible. This is a positioning choice, not a factual error.

notes.suggested: 9 ABY, but worth a caveat
notes.detail: 9 ABY is correct as the year Marrok's body was destroyed by Ahsoka Tano on Seatos during Ahsoka, so the value and endYearEvent both check out. The caveat: the green smoke released from his armor strongly implies Nightsister magick, and both in-universe commentary and fandom reading suggest the man inside may already have been dead long before 9 ABY. Canon has not resolved this, so 9 ABY is the correct end of his column but not necessarily the year he 'died'.

notes.suggested: keep, but note it is a recent Maul: Shadow Lord reveal
notes.detail: The First Brother designation comes from Maul: Shadow Lord rather than Ahsoka; it is worth being aware this is newly established material and that the man beneath the helmet is still officially unidentified.

notes.current: Homeworld: Unknown; Species: Humanoid; Force Sensitive: Yes
notes.suggested: add e.g. "Affiliation: Jedi Order, Inquisitorius, Morgan Elsbeth's forces" and note the possible undead/reanimated status
notes.detail: The metadata is accurate as far as it goes — canon really does leave species and homeworld unstated — but his prior Jedi membership and the strong implication he was animated by Nightsister magick are the two most distinctive facts about him and we surface neither.

notes.current: Existing summary ends with his service to Morgan Elsbeth
notes.detail: Our description never mentions how he died or the green-smoke reveal, which is the single most discussed thing about the character. Worth adding.
notes.suggested: Extend to cover the Seatos duel with Ahsoka Tano and the Nightsister-magick implication.

notes.detail: Sourcing caveat for the maintainer: starwars.fandom.com returned HTTP 402 to direct fetches during this pass, so the Wookieepedia article was read only through search-engine summaries of it plus corroborating coverage (Star Wars News Net, SlashFilm, ScreenRant, GamesRadar). The 9 ABY death and the Tales of the Empire appearance were consistent across those; the Maul: Shadow Lord Janix arc has no firmly established in-universe year, which is why the timeline marks it as approximate.

https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Rotta.description: Rotta, commonly called Rotta the Hutt and nicknamed "Pedunkee Mufkin" by his father and "Stinky" by Ahsoka Tano, is a male Hutt and the son of the Tatooine crime lord Jabba Desilijic Tiure. Born shortly before the outbreak of the Clone Wars, he became a galactic flashpoint as an infant when Count Dooku and the Confederacy of Independent Systems — aided by his treacherous great-uncle Ziro the Hutt — arranged his abduction by Asajj Ventress, intending to frame the Jedi Order for the crime and wreck negotiations that would have opened Hutt-controlled hyperspace lanes to the Galactic Republic. Anakin Skywalker and his new Padawan Ahsoka Tano tracked the huttlet to a B'omarr monastery on Teth and fought their way out with him; Rotta fell gravely ill during the flight to Tatooine and survived largely because of Ahsoka's care. After the pair evaded Dooku's attempt to kill the child and pin it on the Jedi, Rotta was returned to his father, and the Hutts granted the Republic passage through their space. He was later seen at Jabba's Palace during Baron Notluwiski Papanoida's visit. Rotta survived his father's death above the Great Pit of Carkoon and, by the New Republic era, had grown into a heavily muscled arena gladiator, becoming the object of a rescue mission undertaken by Din Djarin and Grogu on the New Republic's behalf.
https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Rotta.timeline:
~23 BBY - Birth

Rotta is born to Jabba Desilijic Tiure, the Hutt crime lord who rules Tatooine from his palace in the Dune Sea. Canon never states his birth year outright; it can only be placed sometime between 24 BBY and 22 BBY, shortly before the Clone Wars.

22 BBY - Abducted by the Separatists

In the opening months of the Clone Wars, the infant Rotta is kidnapped by Asajj Ventress on Count Dooku's orders, in a plot secretly aided by his great-uncle Ziro the Hutt. The Separatists intend to blame the Jedi and prevent an alliance between the Republic and the Hutt Clan.

22 BBY - Rescue on Teth

Jedi Knight Anakin Skywalker and his newly assigned Padawan Ahsoka Tano assault the B'omarr monastery on Teth and recover the huttlet, whom Ahsoka dubs "Stinky."

22 BBY - Illness aboard the Twilight

Rotta falls dangerously ill during the flight to Tatooine and nearly dies. Ahsoka's persistent care keeps him alive long enough to reach his father.

22 BBY - Returned to Jabba

After Anakin defeats Dooku's attempt to murder the child and frame the Jedi, and Padmé Amidala exposes Ziro's treachery, Rotta is delivered safely to Jabba's Palace. A grateful Jabba grants the Republic military access to Hutt Space hyperlanes.

~21 BBY - At his father's court

Rotta is present in Jabba's Palace when Baron Notluwiski Papanoida of Pantora comes seeking help in recovering his kidnapped daughters.

4 ABY - Death of Jabba

Jabba the Hutt is killed above the Great Pit of Carkoon during Luke Skywalker's rescue of Han Solo, and his criminal empire on Tatooine fragments. Rotta is not present and survives his father.

~9 ABY - Gladiator in the New Republic era

Now a grown, unusually muscular Hutt fighting as an arena gladiator, Rotta becomes the target of a New Republic-sanctioned rescue: Din Djarin and Grogu are sent to retrieve him in exchange for information from the Hutt Clan.

birth.source: Wookieepedia "Rotta" article — canon gives no explicit birth year; the article dates his birth to between 24 BBY and 22 BBY, inferred from a conversation between Jabba and Greedo establishing Jabba had recently become a father. 23 BBY is the midpoint of that window, so it is a reasonable positioning guess but not a canon-stated date.

death.source: No death in canon. Rotta appears alive as an adult gladiator in The Mandalorian and Grogu (released May 22, 2026), set in the New Republic era (~9 ABY), which is the first confirmation he survived his father's death in 4 ABY.

notes.current: absent (birth year of 23 BBY presented as a known canon fact)
notes.suggested: true
notes.detail: Canon never states Rotta's birth year. Wookieepedia dates it only to a window between 24 BBY and 22 BBY, extrapolated from dialogue between Jabba and Greedo indicating Jabba had recently become a father. 23 BBY is a fine positioning midpoint, but the app should label it a guess rather than fact.

notes.current: Ends with Rotta being present when Baron Papanoida visited Jabba's Palace.
notes.suggested: Add his survival past 4 ABY and his adult appearance as an arena gladiator in The Mandalorian and Grogu, where Din Djarin and Grogu are hired to rescue him.
notes.detail: The Mandalorian and Grogu (theatrical release May 22, 2026) is Rotta's live-action debut and the first canon confirmation that he outlived Jabba. The current description predates the film and is now the stalest part of the entry.

notes.suggested: Keep endYearUnknown: true, but consider clamping the column to the latest confirmed appearance (~9 ABY) or noting he is confirmed alive as of ~9 ABY.
notes.detail: There is no death in canon, so endYearUnknown is correct. However 50 ABY implies a lifespan far beyond anything canon supports; Hutts are long-lived, so it is not wrong, just unsupported. The meaningful data point is that he is confirmed alive in the New Republic era.

Reasonable, since Jabba was based on Tatooine when Rotta was born and Rotta was returned there, but canon does not explicitly state Rotta's birthplace. Nal Hutta is the Hutt species homeworld. Low-stakes, but worth knowing it is inference rather than a stated fact.

notes.suggested: "Stinky" (or "Pedunkee Mufkin")
notes.detail: Both nicknames are canon and well known: Ahsoka Tano calls him "Stinky" throughout The Clone Wars film, and Jabba calls him "Pedunkee Mufkin" ("Punky Muffin"). Fits the altTitle convention used for characters like Ahsoka ("Snips").
